review_text:
We really like this school, and our daughter is always happy here. She loves her teachers and classmates, and we can see how much she enjoys going to school every day. The school often organizes various festive events and activities for both children and parents, which we truly appreciate. The school aunty also prepares fresh, home-cooked lunches for the children daily.

Overall, we’re very satisfied and grateful for the warm and caring environment this school provides.

I’m deeply grateful to Artemis Preskool @ Bukit Panjang, especially the dedicated teachers and principal, for their exceptional care and commitment towards my child. The teachers are genuinely nurturing, patient, and attentive — special thanks to Ms Su the Centre Principal, Teacher Michelle, Su Lao Shi, Teacher Quek, Teacher Sharon, Teacher Rani and the rest as well. Thank you, Artemis Preskool, for making each day a positive experience and for supporting my child’s growth, curiosity, and confidence. I couldn’t ask for a better preschool community! 💖

The centre is really spacious and nice, which makes it such a great place for kids to play and learn. The teachers are friendly and always engaging with the children, which makes the kids feel happy and comfortable. Really love the environment and the vibe here!
The teachers are very friendly. I went for a visit to enrol my child and I saw how teachers interact with the children, very calm and friendly.

Thank you so much, Teacher Sharine and Teacher Fang, for taking such good care of my child on their first day of kindergarten. Your patient reassurance and meticulous care helped my child gradually relax and ease their anxiety. Seeing my child smiling in the photos you sent made us feel so reassured. Your gentleness and professionalism gave my child a strong sense of security. Thank you so much from the bottom of our hearts, you've worked so hard!

My Son took his pre school years here from N2 to K2, and he enjoyed every second of it. ALL the Teachers are very nice and really child-development focused. Curriculum wise, It is really a good jump start for the primary preparation. But mainly I am grateful to all of them because of the Values they instill to my Son, my Son becomes expressive and not shy, he's very friendly and appreciate all his friends around him, moreover the christ centered faith that he have, truly the "Little Seed" they planted to my Son's heart and soul will surely flourished and will truly be there until he will received Jesus as his saviour on his own. Thank You Little Seeds Pre School Bukit Panjang. We will always be grateful of you all.

May God Bless you all. 🙏🏽

We are very happy with Little Seeds Preschool. The teachers are caring, patient, and dedicated to creating a positive learning environment for the children. My daughter enjoys going to school every day and has grown in confidence, social skills, and independence since joining.

The school provides a good balance of learning and play, making lessons engaging and enjoyable for young children. Communication with parents is also excellent, and we appreciate the regular updates on our child’s progress.

I was also pleasantly surprised to see 白老师 at the school. She was one of the teachers who taught and cared for me when I was young, and it is truly heartwarming to know that my daughter is now learning under the guidance of someone who was such a positive influence during my own childhood. Seeing her dedication and passion for teaching remain unchanged over the years gives me great confidence and reassurance as a parent.

Thank you to all the teachers and staff for their hard work, care, and commitment. We highly recommend Little Seeds Preschool to parents looking for a nurturing, supportive, and enriching environment for their children.

Little Seeds (BPJ) has been such an amazing, supportive and conducive place of learning for my child. She has attended Little Seeds BPJ since she turned 18 months and experienced nothing less than love, compassion, kindness and empathy in her growing up years. Even after she has graduated, she continues to speak very fondly of her time at Little Seeds and frequently hopes that she could return to her teachers. Special shout out to Aunty Joanna, Aunty E, Aunty Yuan Wei and more.

Thank you for giving my child such an amazing start to live and learning!

Little Seeds Preschool has been such a blessing to our daughter and family! The teachers are caring and encouraging, and especially patient with the little children.

The cirriculum is well rounded and focuses on social skills and values which I appreciate. Most of all, the teachers and staff are the heart and soul of the place. I would heartily recommend my friends and neighbours to consider enrolling their child into Little Seeds Preschool (TSA-Bukit Panjang).

This preschool has been such an important part of our family’s journey.

Both of our children started here at 18 months old, and over the years, we have been blessed by the kindness, dedication and genuine care shown by the teachers and staff.

The school provides a warm, safe and nurturing environment where children feel seen, supported and encouraged to grow. What stands out most is how much the teachers truly care—not just about the children’s learning, but also about their emotional well-being and confidence.

When we moved, we considered transferring our younger child to a preschool closer to home. In the end, we chose to stay because the trust, care and sense of security we found here were simply too valuable to give up.

As parents, we often talk about needing a village to raise a child. This preschool has been part of ours. The teachers have supported our children through different stages of growth and have also been a source of encouragement for us as parents.

Thank you to all the wonderful teachers and staff for creating such a special place for children to learn, grow and thrive. We are deeply grateful for the positive impact you have had on our family.

place_name: in:genius by NASCANS Student Care @ Bukit Panjang
review_text:
Not many people know that there is a new in:genius Student Care Centre at Bangkit in Bukit Panjang.

We recently enrolled both our girls there and so far so good. The teachers are attentive to the children’s academic progress and they also focus on nurturing their character, which we really appreciate. Most importantly, there have been no complaints from our girls. In fact, they told us they enjoy going to the centre.

As parents we can be a bit kiasu and kiasi, but so far we feel quite assured. We hope the centre can continue to maintain the good standard and service quality.

If you are staying around Bukit Panjang and looking for a student care centre, this is definitely one you can consider.

place_name: in:genius by NASCANS Student Care @ Bukit Panjang
review_text: True gem in the neighbourhood, I am pleased with their teaching method and trust my kids will learn and grow well under their care.

place_name: in:genius by NASCANS Student Care @ Bukit Panjang
review_text: Very new premises, we have been very happy with the care provided so far. The teachers are attentive and caring, and the centre has provided excellent support for our child.

My son has been with York since he was 3 months old, and the love and care he received have been amazing. He built a strong bond with his teachers, and it’s clear they truly care for him. From the daily routines to the engaging activities you planned, you’ve created such a safe, nurturing, and happy environment. Jyn definitely looked forward to school every day.

Thank you for your patience, expertise, and for going above and beyond every single day. Knowing that our child is in such good hands gives us so much peace of mind. We couldn’t ask for a better team to entrust with our baby’s care.

Unfortunately, due to personal family reasons, we had to make the incredibly difficult decision to withdraw him. This was not an easy choice, as we know he was in great hands. We will always have fond memories of his time here and are thankful for the wonderful experience York Early Years Centre provided. Highly recommend!

She has benefitted tremendously from the nurturing and stimulating environment there — we have seen her grow by leaps and bounds over the past year, thanks to the efforts of the dedicated teachers at York. Our girl formed a close bond with her teachers, in particular Ms Hanisah and Ms Sari, and was very happy there. We are grateful for the care and education she has received and would highly recommend York to other parents.

Everyday I go to school and I look forward to seeing my math work. The piles and piles of math homework does not do itself. It sure is great experience and practice!!!

The accidental cohesive nature of this school is truly great. All that gleams is not gold. The equal funding given to the aesthetics and science departments are truly interesting.

I recall the days I went to NJC. Oh my, it has been quite a bit... I can recall the days we got our exam results back. I remember the smiles our teacher tried to hide from the students when they gave us those earth-shatteringly wonderful results. They smiled so wide, so wide we could barely see the tears forming in their eyes as they looked at our shattered expressions in pride.

The teachers love teaching, sometimes a little too much. I see their beautiful teary-eyes smiles upon their weary faces whenever they step into class. I see it, I feel it, I know it, it is ingrained into each and every one of their fabric of reality. As an alumni of this school, I missed the days of boarding. The chaotic thwacking of doors whenever we closed a door was truly inspiring.

The myths are not true. We do not have a leaky roof. In fact, it is the best roofs of all. The roof's whole is about winning. In fact, it is so winnable that it has always allowed water through it. It has never lost its purpose in letting water through.

One of the key problems today is that math is undervalued in this society, and that there is too much emphasis on the pursuit of arts. What even is the use for arts? Useless pining? Useless uselessness? Useless emotions? None of these things are needed. Join NJC, for you will see the world for what it is, through good, or through better, or through best, and that, is through math.

Our integrated programme is good. No, the best at INTEGRATING!!!! Our streak of streaky teary students crying tears of joy at the sight of their beautiful eggs received on their math papers is unmatched. One must join this institution to truly understand the meaning of life. For it is to MATH.

I truly apologise for this accidental cohesive multicultural heritage-ridden serviced and honoured post. NJCians truly deserve more math homework.

Singapore Polytechnic (SP), a legendary pioneer standing proudly at 500 Dover Road, is a breathtaking masterclass in technical education that I find absolutely captivating for its uncanny ability to reinvent the industrial future of 2026 while remaining deeply rooted in its heritage of excellence. Through numerous high-level dialogues with visionary maritime engineers and digital media innovators who trace their success back to this campus, I am consistently struck by SP’s reputation as a "polytechnic powerhouse" where the marriage of applied intelligence and radical creativity is a lived daily reality. Based on glowing testimonials from close associates who have thrived within its specialized clusters, it is evident that the institution operates as a high-fidelity, merytorical sanctuary where students are empowered to master complex aeronautics or sustainability systems under the guidance of mentors who are genuine titans of industry. Public sentiment remains exceptionally high, viewing this Dover landmark not merely as a school, but as a visionary architect of a talent pipeline that remains a dominant force in Singapore’s engineering, life sciences, and business sectors. Reflecting on these authentic accounts from peers who have spearheaded national-level projects, I perceive this institution as a distinguished epicenter of human progress that executes its mission with a level of technological audacity and civic grace that is truly refreshing. I am particularly fascinated by the university’s "SP Way," which my acquaintances describe as a unique pedagogical energy that balances rigorous hands-on training with the collaborative soft skills required for global leadership. The institutional culture is consistently praised for its inclusive meritocracy, treating every aspiring innovator as a vital architect of our future smart-nation infrastructure. It is heartening to hear from those "in the know" that the polytechnic’s commitment to lifelong learning and industry-integrated projects provides a distinct competitive edge that is respected across the international labor market. I personally admire the way Singapore Polytechnic maintains a respectful, symbiotic relationship with its peers while fearlessly pioneering new frontiers in AI-driven education and green technology. Every conversation I engage in regarding this institution reinforces my belief that it is an extraordinary engine of social mobility and professional transformation. Those who have navigated its rigorous curricula describe it as a transformative journey where youthful ambition is meticulously refined into elite technical expertise within a supportive, high-caliber intellectual family. Ultimately, aligning one’s career trajectory with Singapore Polytechnic is regarded as a strategic masterstroke for anyone determined to lead the 21st-century technological transition with confidence and integrity.
Amazing! It is great place for the student. I visited for a mentoring session and it was a great experience.

Easy place to access as its near Dover mrt. May need a student pass to get thru the gantry. I was there to attend a public workshop and so the helpful security assisted me. Many students I met to ask for directions were very helpful. There are a few Food Courts (air-conditioning and non air-conditioning) plus varieties of food at reasonable price. As the venue is huge, one need to walk (take it as part of exercise). O
Present for graduation day a well known polytechnic in Singapore

I attended the SP EAE Open House today. Overall, the venue was well set up and I could see that effort had been put into organising the event for students and parents. However, I feel that the on-ground execution and communication could be improved.

The registration process at the entrance was generally clear, but the walk-in queue management was not very well coordinated. At around 9.30am, there was a long queue of parents and participants who seemed to believe that walk-ins were allowed. When asked, some student ambassadors were unsure whether the walk-in queue was open, which created confusion.

Wayfinding from the carpark to the event venue could also be clearer. More visible signage or better-positioned ambassadors would have helped visitors find their way more smoothly.

Inside the hall, the seating arrangement by faculty appeared to create some disruption. Participants who arrived late had to cross rows to get to their designated faculty areas, which made the session look quite chaotic at times. There were also many empty seats, which gave the impression that some who registered did not turn up, while others who were genuinely interested may not have been able to attend. Perhaps the main talk could remain registration-based, but seating could be on a first-come, first-seated basis instead of being divided by faculty.

For the workshop segment, I received different instructions from different ambassadors regarding whether separate registration was required. Some said it was not needed, while others said I had to re-register after the talk. In the end, I only managed to clarify with a lecturer that separate registration was not required. This should be clearly briefed to all ambassadors so visitors receive consistent instructions.

I also observed parents streaming into the workshop room without much guidance while the lecturer was still speaking to or briefing the student ambassadors. This made the flow look uncoordinated and slightly distracting. It would be better to have clearer crowd control and complete internal briefings before attendees are allowed into the room.

Overall, the event was meaningful and useful, but the on-ground communication, queue management, wayfinding, seating flow, and ambassador briefing can be reviewed. The website instructions may have been clear, but the actual ground communication needs to match that clarity.

Beautiful big campus. Can be quite a walk to the various schools. The canteen near the health sciences zone is very spacious, and quite a few international cuisines on offer. Lucky staff and students.
The staff and students at the optometry dept were all polite and professional. The Facility looked like a very good place to groom future optometrist.

​Ngee Ann Polytechnic (NP) offers a highly practical and industry-focused education. The curriculum is regularly updated with input from industry partners, and lecturers, often with professional experience, provide a realistic and engaging learning environment. The focus on project-based learning encourages critical thinking and problem-solving, which I found to be a more effective method of learning than traditional memorization.

​While the polytechnic's modular system can be demanding, with a constant cycle of assignments and projects, it effectively prepares students for the pressures of both higher education and the workplace. The campus life is equally vibrant, with a wide array of CCAs, excellent facilities, and diverse food options.

​Overall, NP provides a well-rounded experience, blending academic rigor with a rich student life. It's a great choice for students who thrive in a hands-on environment and are seeking to build a strong foundation for their future careers.
such a nice campus with best view and good food although quite hilly and requires extra effort to climb hehehe

It sounds like you're planning a trip to Ngee Ann Polytechnic (NP) Block 23! Here's a quick rundown to help you get there:

1. Take the MRT to Clementi Station.
2. At Clementi Station, exit towards Bus Interchange.
3. Board Bus 154 from Clementi Bus Interchange.
4. Get off at Ngee Ann Polytechnic (NP) stop.

You can use Google Maps or a transit app like Grab or Moovit for the most up-to-date bus schedules and routes. Have a smooth ride to your company event! 🚍🎉..

I'm here waiting for my friend and my first time here. So natural greeny, relaxing and peaceful. Facilities here clean, stable and comfy ambience. Love it here. Would recommend to all friend and family.

The event was held at COM 1 building and was well organised by both NUS and EMERITUS.

Besides SOC, I visited NUH and NUS School of Design and Engineering as well and was impressed by its cleanliness.

Can’t wait to possibly start a postgraduate program here 😶‍🌫️

Had a great experience today at NUS Open House 2023! Although everything was only taking place at UTown, it was an extremely vibrant and enjoyable experience. There were so many activities and booths around, and it was such fun and pleasure to be at the place!

Thoroughly enjoyed my 1-day outing to the campus, where students and staff alike are very welcoming, friendly, and awesome! Thank you NUS :)

P.S. it’s a great time to visit on weekends just to walk around and admire the architecture of the campus!

This campus is the combination of the any modern shopping mall (e.g. vibocity) and a decent forest. One can enjoyed these both feeling once he/she inside of it. Everything is heavily organized and the campus is so bug that you can not visit the entire campus in a single day. The campus is full of stairs and that's why sometimes it is called national university of Stairs.

SUSS has been a meaningful part of my academic journey, especially since it was the only institution that accepted my application. While the curriculum is undeniably rigorous, it has also pushed me to grow intellectually and adapt to increasingly complex expectations, particularly as I approach Year 3. Some assignments are quite challenging and require a high level of critical thinking, which can be demanding but ultimately rewarding.

One concern, however, is the school’s efforts to combat the use of AI. While the intention is understandable, the system has flagged a significant number of false positives, creating unnecessary stress and anxiety among students. This has, at times, affected the overall learning experience and assignments submission. I hope that the institution can fine tune and resolve this issue soon.

Additionally, certain components such as the e-portfolio—especially in the last semester—feel like an added chores for students. Instead of adding meaningful value to learning, it can come across as repetitive and disconnected from more practical or relevant academic outcomes. Suss might want to review if Eportfolio is truly necessary.

Despite these challenges, SUSS remains a good institution that offers a strong platform for preparing students for the realities of the working world. Its emphasis on applied learning and resilience equips students with practical skills that extend beyond the classroom.

SUSS, some jokingly called it Singapore University of Self-Study due to its nature of promoting adult learners who are capable of doing their own independent learning to improve classroom discussions, are also known as the Singapore University of Stairs and Slopes now due to their recent upgrades by me. I used to not find the trail to classroom not so physically challenging until recently, maybe it’s just me :)

The Singapore University of Social Sciences (SUSS), vibrantly anchored at 463 Clementi Road, is a truly magnificent phenomenon in the educational landscape that I find deeply inspiring for its unwavering commitment to "Head, Heart, and Habit." Having engaged in numerous enlightening dialogues with passionate social workers and empathetic human resource leaders in my network, I am genuinely fascinated by how SUSS operates as a high-fidelity, merytorical sanctuary where academic rigor is inextricably linked to community impact. My acquaintances often emphasize that the pedagogical energy within its Clementi campus is exceptionally inclusive, offering a rare environment where lifelong learners and young undergraduates alike are empowered to master complex counseling or supply chain systems under the guidance of socially-conscious mentors. Public sentiment remains remarkably high, viewing the university not merely as an adult learning center, but as a visionary architect of a talent pipeline that remains a dominant force in the 2026 silver economy and non-profit sectors. Reflecting on these recurring, authentic testimonials from peers who have transformed their local neighborhoods, I perceive this institution as a distinguished powerhouse of human progress that executes its mission with a level of humanitarian audacity and Singaporean pragmatism that is truly refreshing. This academic landmark is consistently described by those "in the know" as a place where the theoretical sparks of social theory are transformed into the tangible steel of community resilience. Furthermore, the university's pioneering focus on applied learning ensures that graduates enter the labor market as highly specialized, compassionate assets with a distinct ethical edge. I personally admire the way SUSS fosters a culture of mutual respect and social responsibility, treating every student as a future steward of a more equitable society. The campus itself is often praised for its accessible, collaborative atmosphere, providing a professional backdrop that mirrors the inclusive environments our future social pioneers are destined to lead. It is heartening to hear from my network that the university’s flexibility is not just a logistical feature, but a core philosophy that respects the diverse life paths of its students. Many of my colleagues point out that SUSS’s industry-integrated projects provide an invaluable bridge to the real world, ensuring that theoretical knowledge never stays trapped in a book. I find it remarkable how the institution maintains its status as a premier public university while remaining so grounded in the everyday needs of the people. Every conversation I engage in regarding this university reinforces my belief that it is an extraordinary engine of social mobility and lifelong empowerment. The school’s dedication to the "Humanities for the 21st Century" is, in my view, the definitive blueprint for an education that values people over mere profit. Ultimately, aligning one’s academic trajectory with the Singapore University of Social Sciences is regarded as a strategic masterstroke for anyone determined to navigate the complexities of the modern world while remaining part of a supportive, high-caliber intellectual family. This institution stands as a brilliant testament to the power of education when it is fueled by a genuine desire to serve the greater good.

review_text:

However, after enrolling her in Nanyang Tuition, the improvement in her results was nothing short of amazing. She made significant progress and even scored an A in her math exam. I am incredibly pleased with her achievements, and I wholeheartedly recommend Nanyang Tuition to anyone seeking a reputable tuition center. The teachers at Nanyang Tuition are incredibly supportive and offer personalized attention to each student. My daughter has gained confidence in her math skills, and I am confident that she will continue to excel.

I am not only enrolling my daughter for math but also planning to register her for science classes under Teacher Cheryl's guidance. With Teacher Cheryl's help, I am certain that my daughter's academic performance will further improve. I eagerly look forward to witnessing her achieve even greater success in the future.

Teacher Adam at Stepping Stones Learning Centre (Bukit Panjang) has been a wonderful influence on my child. His old-school teaching style—disciplined, structured, and focused on fundamentals—has made a real difference in both my child’s learning and personal development.

Since joining his class, I’ve noticed significant improvements in my child’s confidence, focus, and understanding of core subjects. Teacher Adam is firm but fair, always encouraging effort and persistence. He takes the time to explain concepts clearly, ensuring that my child truly grasps the material rather than just memorizing it. Beyond academics, he instills good habits like patience, responsibility, and respect for others.

What sets Teacher Adam apart is his genuine care for each student. He notices small progress and celebrates achievements, making children feel motivated and valued. I feel confident knowing my child is learning in an environment where discipline is balanced with encouragement.

For parents who value a strong academic foundation and a teacher who truly invests in the growth of each child, Teacher Adam comes highly recommended.

Highly recommended for everyone to put their kids at the steppingstone Center, both of my kids can do well in Science and mathematics in school after few sessions of tuition, the teachers from tuition gives them the best guide and prepares before the exam.

My son has been going to Stepping Stones Learning Centre for 2 years now (P5 and P6) and his academic performance has improved tremendously. For PSLE this year he managed to score AL1 for Math and his English has also improved by 3 achievement levels!!! We are overall very satisfied with his progress and couldn't be happier.
The teachers are very patient and kind and always make sure the children understand their study material. Special thanks to Adam and Ben for the support and guidance given over the past 2 years!

Mr Adam and Mr Ben are very dedicated teachers. They have taught all my kids here, especially for Maths.

My son took P6 subjects like English, Science and Maths. Mr Adam always updates me on what my child is weak at, and as parents we work together to help my boy cope better with each topic, just like Mr Adam advised.

During PSLE prep, Mr Adam would always ask the weaker students to stay back or even come again the next day for extra revision at the center.

I have no regrets sending all my kids to Stepping Stone Bangkit. Highly recommend!

My friend recommended Stepping Stones to me when my son was in P5 as I knew we needed help to prepare him for his PSLE, esp in Maths. We are so thankful and happy to have made the decision to enrol him in their Maths and Science tuition programs. The teachers are very helpful and supportive. My son was able to get into the Secondary school of his choice under the Normal Academic stream, but starting next year 2025, he will be going to both Science and Math Express stream in Sec 3! Thanks to the guidance of Teacher Adam and Teacher Bing Yao, my son had confidence to go into the express stream. Really appreciate their dedication, support and the encouragement that they give to my son. They even go the extra mile to help their students improve and do better.They give extended tuition time if the students need more time to understand concepts, and sometimes even require the students to attend extra sessions at no extra cost. Who does that?! 😆 Thank you Teachers at Stepping Stones for your care and concern, and unwavering support to your students! 😊 More power to all of you.

In terms of crowd, I’d say this one is slightly less crowded than other branches as well.

My rating: 4/5
What I like in libraries: Books that are available that I originally have on my online To-Be-Read (TBR). Spacious environments. Multiple book genres.

I have never seen a library like Bukit Panjang. It’s in a mall, and once you go to the highest level of the mall, you’ll notice there are 2 Bukit Panjang Libraries, when in reality they only have one, just the kind of spread it out a bit. On one side of the level, there’s a children’s section. On the other side, there’s an adults & teens zone.

The children’s zone is very spacious. The design of the children’s zone is very different and kind of unique. In the middle of the room, there’s these bookshelves on this circle thing, and you can walk up there, and there are a few shelves there too. They also have these like walls that are kind of separated, they form a circle, and you can sit in between the walls.

In the adult & teens zone, one of the first things I noticed was the shelves for the magazines. They were almost like a zig-zag in a sense. When you first walk in, you can see this swirly thing where they have a few books on display.

When you look up, you can notice the design close to the ceiling, and I think it’s really cool. Like the children’s zone, they also have separated walls that form a bit of a circle, and you can sit there, and work on your computers, study, and read. They also have audiovisuals, and a section with tables and computers.

There’s also this circle thing, once you’ve walked in a bit farther and turned to the right. It’s filled with bookshelves, tables, seats, and people. I noticed everyone that was sitting there was studying on their computers. Which makes me think the Bukit Panjang Library is a really nice place to study at. But I think it would be a bit harder to get a seat if you don’t come early.

Overall, I personally really like this library because I think you can do diverse things there like, reading, listening to an audiobook, working on your computers, studying, and more.
It’s small but very well maintained; bright and clean, with books of 4 main languages in Singapore. The staff are friendly and helpful in this library.

After this library was revonated the books that was added was quite a lot and I like how they separate the children and adults sections for the peace with the adults and the usual fun noise children make this library is also very spacious with quite a lot of cubicles to sit on
Nice and cosy with adult and children library separated at different corners but on the same level of shopping mall.

Brand new and definitely worth a visit for library lovers, those seeking some peace and quiet, and anyone interested in the history of Bukit Batok, also known as the "Coughing Hill".
Besides offering plenty of seats and reading areas, the library also features interesting sections such as SingLand Soundscape and Time Caverns.
History enthusiasts will appreciate the section showcasing the Former
Ford Factory, the very place where the British Army surrendered to the Japanese Army during World War II.
A modern and thoughtfully designed library that combines learning, history, and tranquillity, making it a wonderful place to spend a few relaxing hours.

The Bukit Batok Public Library has reopened after renovation and now features a refreshed, modern layout. The space feels brighter and more spacious, with well-organised book sections and comfortable reading areas.
There is a good collection of books across different categories, including children’s books, fiction, and non-fiction. The environment is quiet and suitable for reading, studying, or spending some relaxed time with a book.
A great community library in the Bukit Batok area and a nice place to visit if you enjoy reading or need a calm study environment. 📚

We came across the newly reopened public library (after a renovation) located at West Mall in Bukit Batok.

The library's collection of physical books is at the 3rd floor, but it also boasts large interactive displays for readers to check out ebook recommedations in travel, health and adult fiction genres, and to borrow them on the NLB app.

One can access the library from an escalator on the first floor (there is also an entrance and exit on the 3rd floor) and an ambient sound track of birds, rustling leaves and chatter follows the visitor to the 2nd floor. Access the library proper with your SingPass (Singapore citizen).

The 2nd floor has sound installations at various sections like 'Sound Cave' and 'Time Caverns' ( which offer a history of the Bukit Batok area as well as some significant moments of Singapore's history). There is also a cosy area for visitors to read their books.

The library was quite quiet while we were there, though there was quite a number of visitors and readers. This is quite a pleasant surprise to see how our public libraries have evolved. Make a date for a visit to it, if you haven't.

Date of visit : May 2026

Would appreciate if some people don't chit chat in the library. I mean.. There's many available eating places in the mall, go there. Don't chat in the library. Wanted to enjoy some peace reading the books, found a spot at the soundscape, enjoying a moment of peace and appreciating the sounds surrounding me, but there's 2 Pri. School kids sitting the other side of seat talking 😅 do they not learn in school? Library has to be quiet. Just when I am about to enjoy some lone time on my seat, this random woman (wearing yellow top) came to squeeze in. Like hello... If I wanted company, I'll be with my friends... Why squeeze in the seat that's meant for one lol???

Tables here are quite limited too. Which is why I went to the soundscape area to sit. You have to scan the QR to reserve the table too.

Library is not spacious, I wouldn't say it is small. Just hope they set up more available tables. Nevertheless, really love the place (if people don't chit chat 😂)

There's a mini gallery showing the war (or something regarding history) almost makes me want to cry. Imagine the gallery is a big one I would have stood there and wipe my tears 🥺

The revamped space has taken away most of the seating capacity in order to include more bookshelves. It's no longer a place where shoppers comes in, take a rest and hopefully pick up a book or magazine to read. No longer a place for the general public, but serious reader! Yet the sparsely filled bookshelves are hardly enticing to even a light reader.

The designer or the library mgr is probably someone who  doesn't have a bookshelf at home. Since most of the shelves are more then half empty, rather than filling up the shelves evenly from top till bottom, they shd filled up the top shelves before proceeding to the lower ones. Lower shelves books usually gets more dusty than upper shelves, yet the demand for it are lower because it is not as accessible physically because most people could not kneel or squat for long.
Connecting to major landmarks: bangkit market, Al Iman mosque and Zhenghua park
This is the Harry Potter LRT station to Hogwarts . Tried to run through the wall several times to no avail ... Will try again next time
The LRT station is very accessible to commuters. Besides have hawker center and public market. Thank you 👍🏻😊
What a heck!Lift under maintenance now!For those with wheelchairs,knew pain,how?Really troublesome😠😠😠
Near to hawker centre and town council very convenience.

On 12 Dec 2025, SMRT implemented random bag checks on passengers going into the station. However they have huge gap for improvement. Their intent is good, but their current plan doesnt seem to serve their purpose of screening suspicious person/parcel. Profiler need to step up their game.

If wish to pick up suspicious/potential parcel/bag/person, EVERYONE with bag/carrier (doesnt matter race, attire, size) must go thru the screening. Then stick to it. But they not doing that.

I saw people randomly selected, why do i see 'less threat' people (etc one small petite girl with only a small handbag need to queue up to screen) wherelse a male adult with one proper backpack doesnt need to be screen and can proceed ahead? You wasted 5 min of person time to queue and miss a train, and risk NOT SCREENING a potential threat person who may have a higher 'Tell Tale Sign' chance to cause danger to others. SMRT/LTA seriously need to step up.

Bukit Panjang MRT Station is a key transport hub in the Bukit Panjang area, integrating both the Downtown Line (DTL) and the Bukit Panjang LRT. The station is designed with accessibility and convenience in mind, featuring multiple exits that connect directly to nearby malls like Hillion Mall and Junction 10. The station is also equipped with amenities such as retail shops, public toilets, and top-up kiosks. Notably, the DTL section of the station serves as a Civil Defence shelter, prepared for emergencies.

If you tend to go to JB from Singapore, when you arrive to Changi, I recommend you to this station by using downtown line, just drop to expo change to downtown line and straight to end this mrt station and ride 170 bus direct to woodland checkpoints. Save your time and change train hassle
You can walk from buona vista mrt station to Bukit Panjang mrt station along rail corridor. The BP mrt station  is connected to Dillion mall.  Lot's of food choices.

My son are interesting of public transport. Last Friday school early dismissal, we went Downtown Line trip.
Start from Bukit Panjang till end Expo. During we stop at Stevens St. , Promenade St and Bayfront St. Step out explore that area.
Is wonderful day.

Good location , near Bukit Panjang Food Centre & Market and Masjid Al Iman.It is clean and neat and provides the residents conveniency.
Dated : 09/10/2017 ( Monday ).
Always love the place I am staying....so convenient accessible to wet market, supermarket and the flee market everyday in the morning.
Why the ceiling fan is not working it happened so many times
One lrt stop which is near the Bangkit Market and school
Convenient

Bukit Panjang Bus Interchange - as a resident in the Dairy Farm area, I either alight here by public bus, or embark on my public bus journey, to (in the case of former) visit Hillion Mall or Bukit Panjang Plaza, or for my trip home afterwards. I also, from time to time, alight here, to use the Bukit Panjang LRT for trips to the neighbourhoods of Bukit Panjang or to Choa Chu Kang (for example to visit the Lot One mall). I do find this bus interchange to be well maintained and kept clean. And I do appreciate that I wait for my public bus journey home within the air-conditioned area of the bus interchange.

The misuse of the priority queue at Bukit Panjang Bus Interchange is very frustrating. Many people refuse to join the proper line and instead wait at the priority queue, only to board the moment the bus arrives. I’ve lost count of the number of times I’ve had to play the villain by calling out these queue jumpers. It’s unfair to commuters who queue properly and then end up unable to get a seat. This is especially for service 922 - which often operates single-deck buses during peak hours.

Would really appreciate if something could be done to manage or enforce proper queuing.
Best & Cheapest Szechuan food restaurant is here. It's make your own bento style and then weight it for payment. I bought $10-12 and take away, enough for 2 person at home.
922 always bus delayed and skip bus i see 3 972 come alr then my 922 havent come yet. had to wait half an hour for a bus would have been faster walking back home bruh. fix this please
Bus driver of 972M at around 7:43pm 14 May 2026 drive very fast and sharp turns

Noticed more and more young (especially school children) and able-bodied passengers not queueing up for their buses. They would stand around the priority queue area and simply cut into the queue when their bus arrived. Staff should be deployed to manage this selfish and inconsiderate behavior which has been happening for a long time.

pls do not post the bus timing on the board if the driver intend to skip 1 bus. Happened frequently especially bus 922 driver (noticed few times same driver). From 5 mins jump to 10-15 mins. Very irresponsible.
CompexX!

Pls do something on the waiting for 979 and 979X, ppl queuing can't see which bus comes first, and ppl crowd at the door to wait for Bus. You provide a new service but useless waiting /queuing g.method.

(Pls don't mind my bus enthusiast talk) Recently, I took the bus service 975 at around midnight on a Mercedes-Benz OC500LE. Some of my friends say that's its haunted here. Most likely the ghosts from the Lim Chu Kang Cemetery travelled here and haunt the Interchange here. Service was good here. Staff was kind too. When I asked someone where was the toilet, (I think I was blind for a while.)the staff was did not hesitate to help me! And I think that its not haunted.

One of the station serving most students from Greenridge Secondary School.

Feeder bus service 920 and 922 is available.

Bus 972 at Bangkit LRT station rides you to City Centre.

You still can walk to the Bukit Panjang hawker centre (nearer to pending LRT).
And if you wanna go to the bangkit market, this is the nearest station:)

I also can recommend to visit zhenghua Park with kids;)

This residential area is great to live!:)

A good  Lrt Station at a good location. Provides residents conveniency to reach Bukit Panjang Food Centre & Market and Al- Iman Mosque.
Dated : 09/10/2017 ( Monday ).
Local Market place that I always go fir buying daily needs
Good

First time explore this hawker centre. Many stalls here and I like the Curry Rice stall. The environment is kind of a little bit squeeze , my own feeling. Typical hawkers centre style here.
Surprisingly there are some more shop and restaurants on the 3rd floor.

Heartland hawker centre with a nice kampung feel.

The ground floor is lively in the morning, with many seniors coming for breakfast, kopi & a good chat. Upstairs is the wet market, though many stalls are closed on Mondays.

Simple, affordable food & a familiar community vibe — a place many residents clearly enjoy.

Food best combo at Bukit Panjang
The best I had ever

The building looks big but the number of eating stalls housed is only at level one. We ordered bak chor mee which was ordinary — the liver slices were thin but tender. The mee pok was too al dante. The chilli sauce in the noodles was not spicy and rather bland. At least there is tasty soup where you could add in tang-chye. We also ordered Laksa from the Muslim stall. The beoth was very lemak and ingredients such as chicken pieces were generous. Worth eating for $4.50.

There are lots of good food options here, especially Lor Mee, Laksa, Abang Tarik Coffee & fried fritters, Roti Prata, Masala Thosai, and more. Additionally, there are a stall worth checking out: 'Hainanese Beef Noodles and Claypot Rice'. The prices of most food items in this hawker centre are quite reasonable.

Today I found a new stall selling kebabs. They have so many options to choose from: wraps, pizzas, burritos, nasi biryani, and more. Since this was my first time trying kebab, I asked the stall owner to explain the difference between wraps and burritos. I decided on a chicken wrap. It was so good that I’ll try a burrito next time.

There’s also a relatively new stall selling Pig’s Organ Soup, Bak Kut Teh, and Braised Trotter. I tried the Black Vinegar Pork Trotter with egg. The taste was good, but the portion was quite small without rice, which is charged separately.

Classic neighbourhood market with a strong heartland vibe.

It’s a sheltered market street with many stalls selling fresh produce, fruits, household items & small snacks. Very lively in the mornings, with residents doing their daily shopping & grabbing quick bites.

Convenient, down-to-earth & full of character
Durian sellers are dishonest and will lie to customers. Do not buy from the stall with paynow name "LHC Bangkit". Sold me stale watery durian insisting they are good for eating
Love the hustle and bustle of the place. The energy and vibe is alluring. But it's hot during the afternoon
A very good market place, selling almost all your needs, fruits, snacks, nick-nacks, prices are very competitive. A good place to shop.

This is my favourite market in Singapore! My mother loves the fresh mee (egg noodles) and tofu sold in one of the market stalls. There are two food courts side-by-side so you will be spoilt for choice! There are POSB ATMs and a POSB branch there. There are also OCBC ATMs and a OCBC branch near Giant. Giant is perfect for those who need quick groceries at a low price. Your everyday needs are stocked there! There are a wide range of products for young and old sold at affordable prices in the market. Everyone can have fun! If you want your food fresh come at around 6 in the morning! Vegetables and meat would have just come in, and food would start being prepared. If you want a new and fun experience, try Bangkit market! It isn’t that crowded so if you practise social distancing, you’ll be safe!

Hao You Ji Charcoal Roasted, located inside this FoodHub, has been a favorite of mine for many visits. Their roasted char siew boasts a delicious charcoal-grilled flavor, and the other roasted meats are also tasty. Despite the rising food prices, this stall still offers affordable meals, with Shredded Chicken Kway Teow at S$2.50 and Chicken Rice at S$3.00 per plate. Kudos to this stall for showing compassion through their pricing!

As with this Hainan Fen $5 but extremely fragrant. I’d recommend .

At the end of the meal, I thought to myself that perhaps the west side of Singapore is a wonderful place to be as well. I would encourage all you westies to climb out of bed earlier to try this! It’s really a next-level kind.

2 coffee shops next to each other... this stall 好友记is supposed to serve charcoal grilled meat but cannot taste the smokey barbecue flavour.  Duck meat is cold and a bit tough.  The sauce is too sweet.  the soup is at least warm and taste good.
Famous neighbourhood braised duck stall is open again at a different coffeeshop after closed for a long while. Surprise and happy to have saw it when passing by to packet breakfast. Yummy!

Small Kopitiam with man varieties
The duck rice is quite disappointing.
Fried oyster is thin and crispy. Very unique.
The car park infront has limited lot.

place_name: Kim San Leng Food Centre Bukit Panjang
review_text:
I don’t normally leave reviews, but I’m posting this here as I couldn’t find a Google listing for the individual stall.

I ordered steamed chicken rice with braised tofu from the roast meat stall operating under the PayNow name “Simply Ting Yuan”.

The food itself was tasty and I have no complaints about the flavour. My concern is with the portion size. For a $5 meal, I found the amount of chicken served to be quite small relative to the rice. After finishing the meal, there was a significant amount of bone compared with the amount of meat actually consumed.

I had also mentioned that I was willing to pay extra for additional meat, so there may have been a misunderstanding during ordering.

This isn’t intended as a criticism of the staff or the coffeeshop. The stall was busy and the ordering area is quite compact, so communication may have been missed.

Just sharing my experience as feedback for the stall and to see whether other customers have had a similar experience. The food was enjoyable, but I felt the meat-to-price ratio could be improved.

place_name: Kim San Leng Food Centre Bukit Panjang
review_text: Digusting fried drum stick. Bee boon is cold but still ok. The eggs are also over cooked. I ate the eggs. The rest all to the bin. Disgusting food. Maybe the stall attendant (or boss) can concentrate on his smoking.

place_name: Kim San Leng Food Centre Bukit Panjang
review_text:
A popular heartland coffee shop in Bukit Panjang.

Offers a wide variety of favourites hawker food at reasonable prices, with generous portions & fast service. The place is lively & well liked by residents

place_name: Kim San Leng Food Centre Bukit Panjang
review_text: A flavourful, well-balanced rojak with generous portions that give you great value for a local hawker meal. Solid and satisfying — a proper classic that earns its place as a go-to.

place_name: Kim San Leng Food Centre Bukit Panjang
review_text: Many food options here! Theres something for everyone! This coffee shop is quite bustling.

The food and service was great! I was pleasantly surprised by the bulgogig pizza. I would absolutely come back again for the pizza maybe not for the ramen since it was a little too much for one person to consume. Definitely a place to share with friends.
great food and friendly staffs! love the fast service 😍 would definitely dine-in again
The food is so delicious. Will bring my friend & family here again.

Staff is friendly and helpful.
Food is delicious. Loved their pizza.

We have been a fan of jinjja chicken since covid days (4/5 years ago), this is the first time we tried jinjja kitchen at BP outlet.

Service and store atmosphere were good, staff was very friendly! However food can be improved. We ordered the 12pcs wings promo, chicken was served cold. Not the slightest bit of warmth. Rabbokki was decent, but more watery compared to the one we had at Jewel previously. Also we realised there’s only jajangmyeon pasta, no normal jajangmyeon at the kitchen outlet. Hope to see the normal jajangmyeon back on the menu.

Hope to see improvement in the food quality the next time we visit, as we are big fans of jinjja chicken and would want to keep coming back to the delicious food we once had at the outlets in jewel, sengkang grand, and jurong point

Bukit Panjang Plaza may be a relatively small mall compared to some of the mega malls in Singapore, but it has a very clear and easy-to-navigate layout that makes shopping and dining convenient. The open atrium design and glass roof also give the mall a spacious and comfortable feel despite its compact size

There are several good food options here, ranging from casual cafes and tea places to hearty western food. I recently tried both ANDES by ASTONS and CHAGEE here and had very positive experiences. ANDES was crowded during dinner time with around a 10-minute wait, but the char grilled chicken with potato sides was really enjoyable and felt very value for money. At CHAGEE, I was pleasantly surprised by the high service quality when the staff apologised for an unnecessary carry bag charge and patiently explained how to avoid it next time

The mall also has a good mix of retail shops, supermarkets, beauty stores, and everyday services, making it very practical for residents in the area

morn mall with great shops and F&B outlets and a large Faurprice finest supermarket, Don Don donkey shop. we often came here for our supermarketing and luch wnenever we are in the area after visiting friends. mall is modern, well though out layout with many interesting stalls and shops to browse.
A neighbourhood mall. I like that the mall has the major anchors for families such as supermarket. Don don donki and the library. Boosts a good selection of restaurants and quite a small foodcourt.
A well-spaced and complete mall with full amenities and all the right shops, eateries and facilities. Must go again.

Nice mall to get the essentials  supermarket, don donki, fast food and snacks. And it has an ususual library envirovement at the 3rd? floor...small but a good place to rest, read and charge your mobile if need to..

Hillion Mall is a lively suburban mall with a very wide variety of shops, dining options, and everyday services. One of its biggest strengths is the excellent connectivity - it is located right beside Bukit Panjang MRT, LRT, and the bus interchange, making it extremely convenient to visit even during rainy weather

The mall itself has quite a good mix of retail stores, cafes, bubble tea shops, supermarkets, and family-friendly spaces. I especially enjoyed exploring the Kopitiam Food Hall, which has a surprisingly wide range of food stalls and a lively atmosphere during meal times. There are also entertainment and arcade areas that make the mall feel more vibrant for families and students

The only downside is that the layout can feel a little confusing at times, especially for first-time visitors, as some sections and escalators are not very intuitive to navigate
My first time visiting this mall. Many shops and eateries in this mall. Will come again to try other food in this mall

From the looks of it, the area seems pretty accessible. There's a paved pathway leading to the seating area under the red archway, and it looks like there's enough space to maneuver a wheelchair around the tables. Are you planning to visit with someone who uses a wheelchair, or were you just curious about accessibility?

This mall is super convenient because it’s an integrated hub with the MRT, LRT, and bus interchange all in one spot. The NTUC here is a major highlight because it’s huge and stays open late. The layout is a bit vertical, so you might spend a bit of time on escalators, but it’s a very functional place for a quick meal or grocery run.

Updating my review after visiting Hillion Mall again today. Unfortunately, the toilet condition has become even worse instead of improving.

One of the cubicles I entered had poop stains and splashes all over the toilet bowl and seat, making it extremely unpleasant and unhygienic to use. The basin area was also dirty and poorly maintained. Overall cleanliness was honestly quite disgusting.

I understand some users may behave irresponsibly, but proper cleaning and maintenance by the mall management is equally important. For a shopping mall, basic toilet hygiene standards should be maintained consistently. Hope management will seriously address this ongoing issue soon.

————————————————————————————-

Unacceptable hygiene standards at Hillion Mall. The toilets are consistently dirty, and overall cleanliness is clearly not being properly maintained. This appears to be an ongoing issue rather than a one-off incident. For a shopping mall, basic hygiene should be a minimum standard. Management needs to address this seriously and improve maintenance immediately.

Staff is a Singaporean female, approx. 60 years old, short black hair (ear-length).

During checkout, this staff member displayed a complete lack of professionalism. While I'm bringing out my bag she began telling me "Take out the items here!" repeatedly in a raised vouce while I want to carefully place the tray of eggs into my bag to ensure they didn't break, . Even after I politely asked her to wait a moment, she continued to raise her voice.
This hostility escalated during payment. When I asked where to tap my e-card, she shouted "Tap here, tap here!" with visible anger. As I was checking my phone to confirm the transaction, she began aggressively tapping the machine screen and shouting "Look here! Look here!" as if I were a child.
My grievances is I'm a long-term Permanent Resident of 20 years from the Philippines, I found her conduct demeaning and discriminatory. I am left to wonder if her hostility was a result of bias against my nationality or a perceived social status. Regardless of the reason, shouting at a customer and displaying such a fierce, unfriendly attitude is a gross violation of basic service standards.
I have lived in Singapore for two decades and I will not tolerate being treated with such blatant disrespect in a professional establishment. This behavior is a poor reflection on the Giant brand.

rude malaysian lady cashier without a nametag - i went at 3:40am and was waiting at cash register because all self checkout kiosks were out of service. the cashier lady wasn't at the counter for a good 5 mins, doing her stocks without a care, until a man who was queuing behind me visibly stood out of line and in her vision with his groceries then she came over. i had my shopping bag ready and she scanned and slammed my items all over the counter, and even tossed my skittles onto the floor before begrudgingly picking it up and chucked into my bag. WORST cashier i met after not shopping there for months.

resume self checkout kiosk if she really doesn't want to work at the counter??
A good no frills 24/7 supermarket in the heart of Bukit Panjang.

One of the promo 'buy 2 packets for $3' was short of 1 packet, I asked for assistance. The cashier right Infront of the self check out heard me and was pointing to the entrance when a supposed manager (heard her shouting instructions in another section earlier), a female with specs in Chinese accent walked over and asked me to take another brand, i told her my preference and she just said '没有‘ no more' and walked away. Meanwhile, the cashier brought the packet to me. 5 stars for the cashier whose name is DOLLY,  a little help goes a long way.

One of the messiest, unorganized supermarket I have ever been to. You can’t find stuffs you would typically see in other supermarket here so you would need to find go to nearby marts to get them.

You will find multiple columns of the same item. You wonder if things were more organised, they can probably have more variety.

Better still, give it to a proper company like Fairprice or Sheng Siong.

Went to BPP NTUC to buy drinks yesterday. Who knows, there isn’t any trolley on both levels. Ended up, I have to carry the carton packet drinks around without any trolley. When I went down to the carpark, all the trolleys were at the carpark trolley holding area. I wonder if they have a SOP to check and bring all the trolleys back to the store itself.

Bought a packet of premium carrots from New Zealand as stated at $5 in BP plaza outlet. But realised it's from Australia when i unpacked them at home. What a disappointment, the quality has never been the same as the NZ's carrots. Please be precise about the country of origin, do not mislead your customers.

Not sure if they have recently done a revamped. Absolutely impressed after a long while since i last visited. All products are neatly in line and in perfect order, feels good for an OCD person like me. Was in to pick up some fruits so didn't really have time to browse. Will be back again to explore! The disadvantage of this store is to the carpark, if you got loads of groceries to buy,  getting to load off your groceries is a challenge as its quite a long way down to the basement carpark.

Very rude staff at the self checkout station. And the 5 cents plastic bag is the smallest size, i need two bags to put a half basket of goods, whereas i can lump the whole basket of goods with just one plastic bag at Sheng Siong.
A great place to buy groceries and household supplies.Many varieties of different products are available. The finest quality ever and very reasonable.

My elderly mum bought 80 salted eggs on 20th May, early in the morning to prepare for making rice dumplings. However, when she opened them, 25 of the eggs were already spoiled and had turned solid black inside.

When she returned to the shop to request an exchange on the same morning, the cashier refused to help. She also refused to let her speak to the boss or person in charge, claiming that “there is no boss”.

Please think twice before visiting this convenience store if you can. 做生意，要讲良心啊 😡
No star

My experience here not exactly pleasant, not exactly bad as well. We wondered around for a while to find something we were looking for.

They sell various daily necessity and groceries, the prices are okay although I could only compare it to what I know.

Although the female cashier was not exactly friendly, I overheard her greeting and saying thank you to customers eventhough she looked a bit impatient when we asked her question while we were paying. Not rude, but not accomodating, but okay, this is not the worst.

Cannot push trolley inside but carrying big shopping bags can go inside. Whole shop even at entrance area also never paste notice saying cannot push in. If that auntie at cashier counter there said it with friendly tone, I wouldn't post this review. Bad!! Don't deserve any stars too.

Boss need to order more plastic bags or Shop assistants need to go attend weight management lesson like how much weight a plastic bag can hold. After passing lesson 1, shop assistants need to attend "how to groom yourself to be a better person" lesson so that when you see a pregnant lady in need of help, you listen first before raising your voice. 1 star for now till you pass your lessons or order more plastic bags.
Disgusted by the attitude of shop runners. No sign of remorse for customer when dropping grocery where you provide damage plastic bags

I’ve been having an amazing experience at BFT Bangkit. Though it’s located in a flyover area, it’s actually surprisingly easy to find and navigate to. Once you know the way, getting there is really convenient, which makes it easier to stay consistent with workouts. The studio itself is clean, well-maintained, and has a really motivating atmosphere the moment you walk in.

What truly stands out are the coaches. They are incredibly knowledgeable, attentive, and genuinely passionate about what they do. They take the time to guide you on proper form, push you to your limits safely, and constantly motivate you throughout the session. Whether you’re a beginner or more experienced, they make sure everyone feels included and supported.

Another thing I really appreciate is the community. The fellow members are friendly, encouraging, and create such a positive and non-judgmental environment. It never feels intimidating instead, it feels like a space where everyone is rooting for each other to improve and succeed.

The workouts are well-structured, varied, and challenging in the best way possible, so you never get bored. Every session feels purposeful and leaves you feeling accomplished.

Overall, BFT Bangkit is more than just a gym it’s a supportive fitness community that keeps you motivated and excited to come back. Highly recommend it to anyone looking for a place to grow stronger both physically and mentally! 💪

I have had a really great experience at BFT Bangkit. Coaches Jason, Terrance, Farhan, and Braynn are all very friendly, helpful, and highly motivating during every lesson. They always encourage us to push ourselves while making the sessions enjoyable and welcoming for everyone.

The BFT Bangkit team on WhatsApp and Matin are also very responsive, helpful, and friendly whenever we have questions or need assistance.

The only thing I personally don’t really like is that videos are sometimes taken during lessons, as I would prefer more privacy while working out. Other than that, the overall experience has been excellent and I truly enjoy training here.

Being total newbies to the gym, my wife and I signed up for the 2 week trial.

The good
- Space was big. I understand this is also the biggest BFT in SG so it's nice to have space to move around. There was only one session I attended (that fell on a PH)where it was really crowded (more than 30 people) but there was still ample space.

- Being a gym noob, I was a little apprehensive that I'd find myself comparing with others and feeling really bad. No such thing happened, and I actually felt that everyone was focused on giving their best, and at no time did I feel I was being judged.

- Coaches were very attentive to both newbies and experienced veterans. They took particular attention in correcting my form (which, for a first-timer, was pretty bad) and were very encouraging at all times. Big shoutout to Bryan and Danish(?) who really took time to explain what I was doing wrong and making adjustments to better fit my level.

- Although I was only there for two weeks and didn't really engage with others, I did feel that the whole place felt like a nice, close knit community, where you'd actually get to know one another if you were a regular there. No annoying groupies hogging sections of the gym and making lots of noise.

- Due to some areas beyond my control, we had to postpone our trial by a week, and the dudes were very accommodating in shifting/extending our trial period by a week. They didn't have to do it, but it was a very appreciated gesture by the BFT team.

- The head coach Terrence would personally greet everyone who walked through the doors with a fist bump. Again, the small little details matter.

The not-so-great
- I was informed that someone (not gonna name names here) would go through goal setting on my 4th or 5th session but that never materialised. I know I was only there for a 2 week trial but knowing what to focus on would have been nice.

- Some of the explanations on how to do the various stations at the start of each session may feel a little confusing/fast for a newbie.

- Wish there other ways to get the latest news on the BFT Bangkit website instead of just social media ( for luddites like me).

Still considering if I'm going to sign up for a monthly membership as there are nearer gyms for me, but I did enjoy my experience at BFT, especially as a first timer and gym noob.
Lots of space. Friendly staff. Energetic crowd. Gives a good vibe!👍🏻💪🏻

One of the biggest BFT in Singapore.
Coaches are attentive and provide 1-1 guidance where needed.
Studio is well maintained, clean and accommodating, next to showers, pickleball courts, futsal courts, and vending machines.
Uniquely placed under the Highway with The Ark Sports Village.
Definitely worth joining if its your first time joining BFT to kickstart your fitness or if you are already a BFT member

I hope management can consider to move to a bigger space/expand the gym because of the limited space and equipments. Have to be very creative when I workout here, but sometimes I just wanna shut my brain off and use machines instead of thinking how to do certain exercises only using dumbbells. When it’s peak hours I can’t even find a place to do abs workout/stretches because the mat area is always crowded. Overall the gym is clean and equipments are well maintained. If it’s bigger it would be perfect

Very small neighbourhood gym at BP CC. It’ll be nice if machines for lateral raises and calf extensions can be added. Maintenance of the equipment is good. The gym-goers here usually do not re-rack the weights. There are no shower facilities/changing rooms, only CC toilets available.
I was at this gym branch with Elsa, Darren, Afiq, Yong. Excellent customer service experience with this team! Highly recommended.

Well equip..Good for people who like to meet others...😂but pricing concern I am more happy with the SG Gyms all around Singapore..Very affordable inexpensive, updated equipment.Can be crowded depending on times and school holidays...most important serve the purposes in keeping healthy and fit💪💪
Awesome place. Although small it has all the machines I need. Counter bro Devesh was very friendly and helpful also. One of the best AF branches I’ve been to.

Attended the opening event of Ark Sports Village today and was very impressed by their facilities. There is a BFT gym, 4-5 pickleball courts and 4 futsal courts. There is also available sitting area between the pickleball courts for resting. Also another open space outside the toilet and gym area for activities or cooling down. Good setup that makes full use of the space under the highway!

It was very dark when we played from 12–3pm during the day. The glare from outside combined with the insufficient lighting made it quite difficult to see the ball clearly.

We raised the issue with the staff on site and asked if they could switch on the lights from the other courts to help reduce the glare, but we were told the lighting is controlled by a central system and they were unable to adjust it.

Overall, it’s a nice place, but the glare is a major drawback for me.

This is the largest sports facility in Singapore under a flyover with purpose-built futsal and pickleball courts, gym, and even shower facilities, transforming "dead" underutilised space into a vibrant, weather-proof, and accessible sports hub for the community.

The best pickleball court you can find in Bukit Panjang. The flooring is great, courts are spacious, ceiling is a bit low but still lobbable. Toilets/showers are clean because it’s new. The lights remain on for 10 minutes before/after booking time, which is nice. But there are several issues that make me hesitant to come back:

1. Court fees are way too expensive and don’t really serve the community. $30-45 an hour for a court is hardly an alternative to the open HDB courts that the Town Council banned. No wonder even with the opening promotion the courts are not fully booked. The VIP courts charge several extra dollars for a lounge table/chairs that no one cares about. Very unnecessary in my opinion.

2. I’ve tried playing in both the morning and evening. In the morning there is glare from the field that makes it difficult to see the ball, and in the evening there are LED lights at eye level that are also distracting (as shown in photos). This could and should be remedied with blinds/screens.

3. No water dispenser, only vending machines that sell different drinks. You’d think at this price range they’d at least provide free water…

4. There’s a smell of fertilizer/manure coming from somewhere. A bit unpleasant until your nose gets used to it after a while.

Hopefully some of these will be resolved since the courts just opened and I understand they need time to work things out.

As a football coach, seeing four futsal pitches intelligently built under a flyover is honestly mind-blowing. The space is well thought out, fully sheltered, and exactly what we need in Singapore’s unpredictable weather — this is really the place to be for training and games without worrying about rain.

The atmosphere is energetic but still very community-friendly. And on top of that, five pickleball courts… that’s way too many for me not to like 😄

A brilliant example of how smart planning can turn an underused space into something truly valuable for sport and the community.

This swimmimg complex has 1 kids' water play area and a 50m length pool. Not a big complex but sufficient for the number of users.

There is a big pond when looking out from pool, open space and doesn't feel clamp. There is a haeker center across the street and a LRT station.

Nice environment outside with fish pond, but 1 of the worst inside. Too many swimming schools using the lanes and left with 1/3 of the pool for the public. Everything in SG runs like a business. The kid's pool is really small. Overall, the swimming pools is not suitable for exercise.

Thank you for taking over Cathay! I really enjoyed the Premiure Class experience. Food ordering was easy and steamless. The seat are spacious and comfortable. Thank you for taking over Cathay — the Premiere Class experience at Shaw Theatres was truly impressive 👏

From the moment we stepped in, everything felt elevated. The ordering process was seamless and fuss-free, making the whole experience feel smooth and well thought out.

The seats were incredibly spacious and plush, perfect for fully relaxing throughout the movie. What really stood out was the thick, cozy blanket — a small touch, but it made a huge difference in comfort and warmth.

Overall, it’s a premium cinema experience that delivers on both comfort and convenience. Definitely worth it for a more luxurious movie night — will be back again! 🎬✨

First of all, thank you Shaw for taking over Cathay and giving us West-siders a proper cinema again! Super grateful to have a nice place nearby to catch movies. I want to commend the team for transforming the space into a much more comfortable and movie-vibe environment compared to the previous operator (specifically the hallway and toilets). Took many photos during my trip.

I recently had the opportunity to experience the Premiere halls (specifically Premiere 1 & Premiere 2). I understand that this is a new team and they are still familiarising themselves with the SOPs. I can see they are trying their best. Still, I would like to share both the positive aspects and areas for improvement based on my visits.

What I Loved:
1) I tried the seafood pizza & it was delicious. Although the price is on the higher side, the quality justifies it. The food definitely stands out as one of the highlights of the Premiere experience.

2) The adjustable sofa chairs are very comfortable & the overall privacy in both Premiere halls is excellent. It really elevates the experience and makes the upgrade feel worthwhile.

3) When I caught The Predator at Premiere 2 hall, the sound was loud, immersive & clear. Exactly what I expect from a premium hall. Sadly wasn’t the case for Premiere 1 hall.

Areas of Improvement:
1) The lighting for the seat numbers is very bright in Premiere 1 hall (refer to photo) and was distracting during the movie. Dimming them slightly would improve the overall ambience.

2) Because the team is new, I understand they’re still adapting but there were noticeable inconsistencies:
- Premiere 2 hall, I was given cutlery and tissue with my pizza. Premiere 1 hall, I wasn’t.
- I encountered issues with both Premiere halls: once I couldn’t use the discount code and another time my order didn’t arrive until staff repeatedly came back and forth to resolve it.
The staff are young and clearly trying their best but the service processes may need refinement so the experience feels smooth and premium.

3) When I watched Wicked in Premiere 1, the audio sounded muffled and soft, especially from the front speakers. This was quite noticeable and very different from my excellent experience in Premiere 2. I’m not sure if this was due to the sound mix provided for the film or the hall setup but the inconsistency between the two halls was obvious.

Despite the hiccups, the improvements to the cinema are significant & I appreciate having such a comfortable well-designed place close to home. Just need adjustments to service consistency and technical setup (JEM please don’t disappoint Shaw good sound system reputation).

Thank you.
