first time coming to jem for a premium movie and it was a good experience. we only got fries but i was surprised they had ice creams and alcohol available too. the whole atmosphere is calm with dim lighting, plush seats and tons of legroom. it’s super family friendly but still feels grown up so you can actually relax. kamalii gave us a quick tour and explained all about the premium options so we knew what we were getting. the premium waiting area looks excellent and the service was really good, way better than the other outlets i’ve been to. she made us feel like vips, not just another booking. we’re definitely coming back here soon.

First time at EagleWings Cinematics, after having a great dinner at EagleWings Loft a few months back.

First impressions were excellent. The staff warmly showed us around the lounge and explained how we could enjoy the place before the movie. We were especially excited to order the lobster risotto again, which we had been craving.

The lounge experience was affected when a rowdy group arrived before what seemed to be a company movie screening. Not the cinema’s fault, but it would help if staff could gently guide such groups to available seats instead of allowing them to block walkways and inconvenience other guests.

Our sides arrived quickly, though the risotto took a little longer. Staff were attentive throughout and checked with us on whether we preferred to dine in the lounge or in the hall.

The Platinum Hall was beautifully furnished and comfortable. Our food was neatly arranged at our seats when we entered. One thing to note is that the top-row “sofa” seats do not have centre armrests, unlike the lower rows, which may matter depending on seating preference.

Before the movie started, a family of three occupied two sofa sections, and the child was climbing around and making quite a bit of noise. Thankfully, it stopped once the movie began after the finally parents stepped in. It did make me wonder whether there are checks to ensure guests are seated according to their bookings.

Overall, it was still a very enjoyable evening, and I would gladly return again, hopefully without another large corporate screening crowd while in the lounge, or rowdy children in the theatre.

Made a trip today to try out EagleWings Cinematics for the first time, and overall, it was a pleasant experience. While we initially encountered some hiccups with the lounge service, the manager and team were proactive, sincere, and prompt in addressing our concerns, which we truly appreciated. The theatre itself was well-maintained, with spacious and comfortable seating. Dining in the theatre was enjoyable, and the Loft Menu offered good food and snacks at reasonable prices. Given the many positive aspects of our visit, we look forward to returning.

Everything was good but the service of the food counter staff on duty today. We ordered popcorn from the QR code in our seats, but after 15-20 mins it still had not arrived. We went out and saw that your staff Madison who was at the popcorn counter was playing with her phone and did not take notice of our order. Re-training highly encouraged.

Amazing experience. We booked in advance the premium lounge. My son and I used their massage chairs. That alone was worth the experience. It was like getting a deep tissue massage. They seem to have upgraded their massage chairs recently. Oh and free flowing drinks (hot or hold). (I recommended u come at least 45mins before the cinema start time). Then we went in, each two people can sit together on a couch which reclines and allows you to stretch your legs. It even comes with a fresh blanket in a pouch. Then u can scan the QR code and order as if you’re at a restaurant with a garment menu. Optionally you can also order popcorn 🍿and pay as u order. or press a separate call button to order more free soft drinks.

Royal experience. We watched supermario galaxy movie and the sound system and the screen size was suitable for the size of the room.

I realized that we picked the best cinema in SG when we kept talking about how awesome the experience for 10mins while we walked back to the MRT. We started planning our next visit as soon as we walked out.

Oh and the kindness and the hospitality is something else. This great level of treatment of customers is a rare treat in Singapore.

I wish I can give it higher than 5 stars
⭐️ ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ it’s obvious whoever came up with this idea loves cinema, food and wants to share their passion with other people in SG. I wish this place more success and expansion in SG.

Kitty and I have been loyal patrons of EagleWings Cinematics for years, and we absolutely adore this cinema.  It's a truly unique experience that we keep coming back to again and again.

EagleWings Cinematics is located within the KAP Mall, and it stands out from other cinemas in Singapore because of its intimate and luxurious atmosphere.  Each theater is cozy and designed to hold a small group of people, like 12 to 20 people, making it feel like a private screening. It's so comfortable and relaxing!  The seating is fantastic, and we've tried all their classes—Silver, Gold, and Platinum.  Our favorites are Gold and Platinum, which offer plush reclining sofas with blankets to keep you warm.

But the real highlight is their amazing service and amenities.  Right next to the cinema is a fantastic restaurant where you can order a wide variety of food, from local favorites like laksa to international dishes like steak and fish and chips.  You can even order beer if you choose Gold or Platinum class.  It's a far cry from the typical cinema snacks! You can also enjoy their lounge one hour before your movie starts, with free-flowing coffee and soft drinks, and even two massage chairs to relax in!

The service is impeccable.  They efficiently deliver your food to your table in the theater, and the table lamps are perfectly positioned to illuminate your meal without disturbing other guests.  The sound system is excellent, and the air conditioning is just right.  It's a comfortable and enjoyable movie-going experience.

We love how EagleWings never feels crowded or rowdy.  The atmosphere is always so peaceful and relaxing, perfect for enjoying a movie.  We've recommended EagleWings to many of our friends, and they've all become addicted to this amazing cinema.  From that point on, they've been watching movies at EagleWings exclusively!

The overall experience is always positive.  It gives you a holistic experience, where you can enjoy your movie on the comfortable sofa seat and have delicious meals in the cinema.  The food we've tried over the years has always been above average.

EagleWings is also incredibly convenient.  All the theaters are on level 1, and if you're looking to book a private function, you can head to level 2.

We highly recommend EagleWings Cinematics to anyone looking for a luxurious and memorable cinema experience.  It's a great option for a romantic date night, a family outing, or a night out with friends.

Compact yet engaging adventure space perfect for kids. Despite its size, it cleverly incorporates various obstacles, play areas, and climb structures that keep children entertained and happily engaged, allowing them to burn off their boundless energy. Maintained diligently by NParks, the sandy pit surface adds a natural touch and is consistently mosquito-free, safe and enjoyable play environment for kids. For accompanying adults, there are convenient sitting areas available, providing a comfortable spot to supervise while the little ones explore and play. Well-designed and maintained playground, great place!

A quiet, sandy and shady children's playground along the PCN of Zhenghua Park.

Fairly spacious, for kids' sand play or trying out the slides and other climbing features.

Went on a Sat morning. The weather was great, with a few other families and their kids enjoying the playground.

Between KJE and Bangkit groups of HDB's

Highly recommended place. Very quiet and clean place. Uncle us very good, i have been here for more than 10 times. Good atmosphere around the accommodation. Clean and hygiene. Totally lurve this nature park, perfect for beginner hikers too.

If you’re lucky, you can spot wild boars as well.

It’s a well shaded park with a lot of trees, so you don’t have to worry about the scorching sun if you plan to go in the middle of the day.

It’s connected to chestnut nature park, but be sure how to get there as I almost walked into the bike trail instead.

It's a really nice place, I entered through the Bangkit Road entrance. A lot of trees and place doesn't have a lot of ppl, so nice to take a stroll or cycle there. There is a cool sand playground and fitness area, equipped with swings, slides, benches and dustbins 💀. If you walk for a while there's actually a connector into the Chestnut Nature Park in Bukit Timah Nature Reserve.
The 4th and 5th images show the connector, which is directly under the BKE. Beware though, I'm not lying it actually feels a bit creepy there and I saw a golden idol on the top of a soil mound on the right and just beside that there was graffiti on the wall saying " Be careful ". The connector is a bit dark and the path isn't really proper.

Zhenghua Nature Park is one of my favourite neighbourhood park. Today spent 1-1.5 hours walk from one end till another end. Along the journey, squirrels, chicken can be seen. Sometime if you are lucky, wild boars also can be spotted, need to be careful. Overall, It's is a good place for family, friends' outdoors activities.

A fairly linear and shady neighbourhood park, along the PCN, connecting to various nearby parks (e.g. Zhenghua Nature Park, Chestnut Nature Park, Bukit Panjang Park) and various trails for hiking and cycling. Popular with families, joggers and cyclists.

Very lush greenery, providing good shade in the morning. Nice scenic nature view along the trail. A sandy children's playground and a fitness corner along it.

Seems narrower and with some steep slopes at some parts. So do look out for other park users when cycling.

A little noisy with BKE just beside it, but this park acts as a green buffer for the nearby HDB and other residential developments.

Will visit again, and maybe explore the Chestnut Nature park trails it is connected to.

A fantastic spot for a nature walk with some of the best nature and scenery on our small island! The trails are well-maintained and leading up to the Singapore Quarry is definitely a highlight. We even spotted a monitor lizard swimming in the water.

Pro-tips for visitors:
Parking: On Saturdays, Carpark A fills up fast. We arrived at 7:30 AM and found quite a few lots, but it was basically full by 8:30 AM when we left.
Timing: While the main park is open 7 AM – 7 PM, note that the Singapore Quarry gates only open from 8:00 AM to 6:30 PM.

Highly recommended for a morning dose of fresh air!

Dairy Farm Rock Climbing Wall is a bit hard to locate and not very visible from the main walking path. If you’re just passing by on the regular trail, you probably won’t even realise it’s there. The wall itself is mostly hidden behind trees and vegetation, so it doesn’t really serve as a scenic or obvious landmark

That said, you can occasionally spot climbers in action through the gaps in the foliage, which is quite interesting if you happen to notice them

26/November/2025
Spend 1hr here in the morning. Nice walking path. Chargeable open-air parking available. Toilets were clean & well maintained.

🦋Bukit Panjang Butterfly Garden is located in front of 213 Petir Rd Community Hall, and beside Bukit Panjang Road.
🌺The Garden is thriving with many butterflies.
🦋Young children will love watching these beautiful little creatures flying around.
🦋Butterflies played an important role to the local biodiversity.
🐛The butterflies and caterpillars are food source for many small animals, like birds, and other insects.
🌹🐦‍⬛It helps with pollination and it's present or decline is an indicator to the overall ecosystem's health.
See the many varieties of flowers and different species of butterflies. Immerse in the beauty!
Needs more work to keep it attractive for butterflies to keep coming

Small garden, saw only a couple of butterflies when was there. Had nice signage on life cycle of butterfly.. Great for preschoolers... Maybe can add infor on type of butterfly varieties that often visit the garden?! Kids can then try to spot and match them.

Pang Sua Pond is a peaceful and calm place to relax and unwind. There’s a large pond, a walking bridge, and a nicely maintained landscape area. You can also enjoy a beautiful panoramic view of the nearby town. I spent about 2–3 hours here with my family and really enjoyed the quiet atmosphere. Highly recommended if you're nearby and looking for a relaxing spot.

1. Early in the 1990s, it was a stormwater collection ponds built to combat the flooding during heavy downpour by collecting surface runoff.
2. The renovation was completed on 25/3/2017.
3. In SG, the Pang Sua Pond Floating Wetland is one of the largest man-made floating wetlands with leather fern, water canna, and the miniature flatsedge.
4. The bridge inside is a 480-meter long.
5. Area of constructed floating wetlands 2,000m²; Area of Pang Sua Pond 3.2 hectares (32,000m²)

Pang Sua Pond is an absolute 5-star gem and a testament to what a beautifully designed public space can be. This isn't just a pond; it's a stunning stormwater collection point (part of the ABC Waters project) that has been transformed into a vibrant community hub.

The main feature is the wonderful boardwalk that stretches across and around the water, connecting the different parts of the neighbourhood. It's my go-to spot for a peaceful morning walk or a relaxing stroll in the evening. As my photos show, the water is often incredibly still, creating stunning, calm reflections of the surrounding HDBs and the sky.

It's also a fantastic spot for nature lovers. It's teeming with wildlife! You can often see herons and egrets, and I've frequently spotted large monitor lizards (like the one in my video) swimming calmly in the water, which is always a treat to see.

One of the best practical things about this pond is its location. It's conveniently surrounded by quite a number of food courts and kopitiams. This makes it the perfect place for a relaxing stroll after a heavy meal from one of the nearby eateries, helping to walk off all those extra calories.

It's a beautiful, tranquil, and practical public space that perfectly blends nature with heartland living. Highly recommended for residents and visitors alike!

It's a hidden gem in a busy neighbourhood. I love taking an evening stroll around the pond, listening to the sounds of nature, watching the fish swim, elders exercising and people enjoying leisure walk. It's a peaceful and scenic spot beside Senja-Cashew Community Club near Bukit Panjang MRT.

Great view and excellent for chilling especially with friends and in the evening, the environment is very nice. There were also a lot of fishes and resting places. Overall, highly recommended for those who want to relax in the evening.
Such a green park. Super cool!
Peaceful community
