Thank you for all your hard-work in keeping our children happy, loved, safe and creatively educated.

Highly recommended!

The teachers here are all very attentive and caring towards my son. He has learnt a lot from their activities and teachings. My son is 3years old now and has been attending their school since he was a baby of 4months. Teachers also maintain close and good communication with parents.

I’m very thankful for the care my daughter receives at Willow Nursery. The environment is warm and nurturing, and it gives me great peace of mind.
Special thanks to Teacher Geraldine, Teacher Mazni, and Teacher Dorothy for their patience and genuine love. They take such great care of her, and it truly means a lot to our family.

MindChamps Chinese PreSchool at Dairy Farm opened in March 2024, and we first visited shortly after for a school tour. The Director, Beifang, was very patient and accommodating, especially as my two daughters happily explored the MindGym (padded classroom for lessons on strengthening both brute and brains haha) and MindScape (read: indoor playground haha) during the visit. It immediately felt like a child-friendly and thoughtfully designed space.

We decided to switch both girls over in July 2024 as we were looking for an immersive Chinese environment. What we found, though, went well beyond language exposure. The range of programmes has been very beneficial for them, and it’s been lovely to see their curiosity and interest in learning grow. Both girls have expanded their Chinese vocabulary! Simple childhood stories are brought to life in creative ways, and the girls often surprise us with how they think and express themselves. By the way, MindChamps also has a structured Reading and Writing programme that focuses in English.

What I appreciate most is how open and supportive the staff and teachers have been. Besides the school Directors, Principal Toh, Zhou Laoshi and Ms Teng have been approachable, receptive to feedback, and take the time to work with parents on challenges a child may face. This has given me a lot of reassurance as a parent. The teachers genuinely care, and it shows in how they engage with the children. Like many young children, my daughter went through a phase where social interactions were challenging. With Zhou Laoshi’s gentle guidance and practical advice, she has grown a great deal in confidence and now looks forward to school each day.

We’re grateful for the positive experience so far and feel thankful to have found a school where our girls feel supported and excited to learn.
Our child enjoys school so much and has truly been thriving under the teachers’ care. We’re grateful for the nurturing environment and the effort the team puts into making each day meaningful.
Wonderful teachers and centre. Lots of event and my kids love to attend school.

We searched for some time for a kindergarten-preschool that had a lower student ratio and a good environment. This one's relatively new, but the teachers have been friendly and communicative, the classroom space is ample, and because it's a new area the facilities are all in very good condition. We're appreciating the weekly updates with photos of the class and the termly curriculum - it's very structured but at the same time you get a lot of information about how things are being paced, and for playgroup it's a lot of imaginative and musical activities, so there's no need to worry about hot-housing or over-teaching to toddlers. The teachers also give recommendations on books and activities to do on our own time. There are a number of trips we can participate in - our daughter enjoyed a recent one to the zoo in May, and there's another upcoming event at the end of June - and there are engagement activities like parent-teacher meetings that let the parents directly discuss with the teachers. Overall, we're quite satisfied with this school so far.
Wonderful staff at dairy farm! Chinese immersion has greatly helped in fostering mandarin interest and confidence.

This condominium complex with its address along Dairy Farm Walk has yet to be occupied, but it looks like it would be imminently so (see photos herein). While its address is indicated as along Dairy Farm Walk, some of its unit face Dairy Farm Heights and Dairy Farm Lane - in the case of the first, The Botany currently faces a vacant plot of land with a canal farther ahead, but the vacant plot of land would eventually be occupied by a condominium(?); and in the case of the second, it faces a small plot of secondary forest and a condominium called "Skywoods"; and in the case of the third, it faces an international school, the German European School Singapore. While the vicinity in which The Botany is located is currently quite quiet, I would expect this to change with a couple of other condominiums slated to be developed therein.
I was impressed with this new complex!
Hi, may i know if there are any existing any Facebook or residents chatgroup?
Good
VERY FRIENDLY SECURITY STAFF & CLEANER AND RESIDENT ALSO VERY FRIENDLY BUT MANAGEMENT STAFF VERY RUDE !

Living at Dairy Farm Residences has been a genuinely pleasant experience. One of the biggest highlights is the peaceful, green environment—it feels tucked away from the city bustle while still being very well connected. The proximity to nature parks and greenery really makes a difference to day-to-day living.

The development is well-maintained, with clean common areas and thoughtfully designed facilities that are practical rather than over-the-top. The integration with nearby amenities and shops downstairs is also very convenient, especially for daily essentials.

Overall, it’s a great place for anyone who values a quieter lifestyle without giving up accessibility and comfort. Happy to call this place home.

Nice and quiet neighbourhood. The view from the pool was great. The gym was well-equipped. The playground was enjoyable for the kids.
Great place to stay!
Close to Expressway, convenient to travel about if you are driving.
There are 2 buses to reach the condo.
There is a mall just beside it, so very convenient to shop for things.
Nice design and quiet environment. Landscaping is good and good land size.
Great convenience, direct access to supermarket, foodcourt and restaurants. Huge, well-equipped clubhouse with beautiful view of infinity pool. 1min to BKE.

DFR is surrounded by lush greenery and fresh air, offering a calm and relaxing environment after a busy day. The layout of the units is practical, and the overall design makes the place feel cosy and welcoming. With convenient access to shops, cafes, and nature parks nearby, it’s a great location for families and nature lovers alike. Truly a nice balance between city living and a tranquil lifestyle. 🌿
Dairy farm has changed drastically with buildings and new buildings too such as condo, German school etc.

place_name: Edufirst Learning Centre (Bukit Panjang) – Math, English & Chinese Tuition | Tuition Centre | Student Care
review_text:
Since I was in Secondary 4 A Math, it was pretty straight-forward for me. It definitely took some time to get used to Ms. Si Qi's style of teaching but it helped me to understand the topics more. She would often ask questions, as she hopes that I can understand the topics well. She responds to queries as fast as she could. The lessons that she constructs is far better than how my school teacher taught me in school.

The 1-to-1 lesson gives it a very conducive environment when it is during the school holidays, but do expect noise as it is located within a student care centre.

My grades improved a little bit, and I jumped from F9 to C5 in 3 months.

place_name: Edufirst Learning Centre (Bukit Panjang) – Math, English & Chinese Tuition | Tuition Centre | Student Care
review_text:
My daughter is happy going to tuition here... Teachers are friendly and caring. At times, reach early to start the tuition and end later then stipulated time.
Will definitely enrol my younger daughter to have tuition here as well.

place_name: Edufirst Learning Centre (Bukit Panjang) – Math, English & Chinese Tuition | Tuition Centre | Student Care
review_text:
I have had a fantastic experience with Edufirst Senja branch, all thanks to the dedication and professionalism of the lady in charge, Ms.Christabelle. From the very beginning, she has been incredibly welcoming, organized, and attentive to every student's needs.

She genuinely cares about the progress of each student, often going the extra mile to ensure they receive the right guidance and support. Her communication with parents is clear, timely, and thoughtful, which makes a big difference in tracking our child's learning journey.

The centre is well-run, and the environment is both supportive and motivating. Thanks to her leadership, the tutors are also passionate, patient, and committed to helping students succeed.

I highly recommend this tuition centre to any parent looking for quality education and a caring, responsible person at the helm.

place_name: Edufirst Learning Centre (Bukit Panjang) – Math, English & Chinese Tuition | Tuition Centre | Student Care
review_text: We’re incredibly grateful for the care and dedication shown by the team. Our child has truly thrived in this nurturing environment — they come home happy and excited to share what they’ve learned each day. It’s clear that the centre is a place where children feel safe, supported, and inspired. Thank you for making such a positive impact on our child’s early years!

place_name: Edufirst Learning Centre (Bukit Panjang) – Math, English & Chinese Tuition | Tuition Centre | Student Care
review_text: We’re very pleased with the quality of teaching here. The tutors are attentive and tailor their lessons to suit each student’s needs. My child feels more confident in school and always looks forward to class. Highly recommend this centre to other parents!

place_name: Qi Ming Learning Center 启明华文(Qi Ming Chinesisch-Lernzentrum) - formerly Zhang Lao Shi Chinese
review_text: I would like to recommend Qi Ming Learning Center to any parent looking for quality Chinese tuition. The small-class lessons are engaging and effective. Teacher Zhang is very responsible and communicates with me regularly on my child's progress. My child has improved significantly in Chinese. The center is newly renovated which provides a conducive learning environment to children.

place_name: Qi Ming Learning Center 启明华文(Qi Ming Chinesisch-Lernzentrum) - formerly Zhang Lao Shi Chinese
review_text:
Like many second-generation immigrants, my child was caught between worlds. At school, he excelled in English and got by with basic Mandarin lessons, but at family gatherings over video calls, he could only nod and smile.

Teacher Zhang specialised in helping my child reconnect with their heritage language through storytelling, games, and cultural activities rather than just textbooks.

Last week my Child confidently told his grandparents about his school day entirely in Mandarin over video call.

We greatly appreciate Teacher Zhang's help. A clear recommendation for all parents looking for a dedicated, competent teacher.

place_name: Qi Ming Learning Center 启明华文(Qi Ming Chinesisch-Lernzentrum) - formerly Zhang Lao Shi Chinese
review_text: I am truly grateful to Qi Ming Learning Center for their dedicated teaching. The small-class concept makes a big difference—it allows the teacher to provide personalized feedback and tailor lessons according to my child’s progress. With their guidance, my child’s Chinese results have improved. Thank you for the patience and professionalism!

place_name: Qi Ming Learning Center 启明华文(Qi Ming Chinesisch-Lernzentrum) - formerly Zhang Lao Shi Chinese
review_text:
Congratulations on the opening of Ms. Zhang's tutoring center!

My child has always lacked confidence in Chinese, but Ms. Zhang always promptly praises and encourages him when she sees his strengths, giving him a lot of confidence.

Last year, my child achieved top marks in O-level Chinese, and we are very happy. We wish Ms. Zhang's Chinese tutoring center continued success!

place_name: Qi Ming Learning Center 启明华文(Qi Ming Chinesisch-Lernzentrum) - formerly Zhang Lao Shi Chinese
review_text:
I would like to share my experience with Zhang Laoshi, who taught my child for an extended period before opening her own center in the Dairy Farm Mall.

Zhang Laoshi has a very personal and empathetic way of working with children. We especially appreciated that she didn't just focus on rote memorization, but rather addressed the individual needs and learning level of each child.

She was a real support, particularly for our child, who didn't grow up speaking Chinese as their first language, helping them catch up with the Middle Eastern Education (MOE) curriculum in primary school.

She explained things patiently, motivated our child, and boosted their self-confidence. It's clear that the well-being and progress of the children are truly important to her.

We are grateful for the time we spent with her and wish her every success with her new center. We highly recommend her to all parents looking for a dedicated and competent teacher.

The library is always an ideal sanctuary for those who appreciate some peace and quiet.
It has been years since my last visit, and it now appears to have expanded, with an entire upper floor dedicated to the Children’s Section.
Clean, well-maintained, and serene, it’s a place where one can truly enjoy a sense of calm and peacefulness.

Choa Chu Kang Public Library at Lot One is a very modern, clean, and well-designed library that is conveniently located right next to the MRT station and inside the mall. The interior feels bright and welcoming, with spacious layouts and comfortable areas for reading and studying

I especially liked the contemporary design touches and the creative displays throughout the library, which make the space feel more vibrant and inviting. Despite being in a busy suburban mall, the library still maintains a calm and relaxing atmosphere

Pleased with my visit here and how clean, modern and spacious the library is. Plenty of seating areas and even a level 5 kids area to entertain the kids. Great selection of books and many assortment of seats to accommodate many people. I also liked that borrowing a book via the NLB app was such a breeze!

Choa Chu Kang Public Library is a well-designed and cozy space for readers of all ages. The library has a good collection of books, magazines, and digital resources, along with plenty of comfortable seating for studying or reading quietly. The layout is modern, spacious, and family-friendly, with dedicated sections for children and teens. It’s also very convenient since it is located inside Lot 1 Shopper’s Mall, making it easy to drop by after shopping or dining. The staff are polite and helpful, and the environment is peaceful — a perfect spot for learning, reading, or simply relaxing.

I think that the Choa Chu Kang Library is very spacious, especially when I walked in. Once you walk around the fiction shelves, you’ll notice that there’s this seating area of 6 chairs around this round table. That’s a good place to work on your computers, study, and read.

Once you see that, just walk towards the window and look left and right, you’ll notice there’s this long table connected to the wall, and multiple people can sit there. That’s a really nice place for reading, studying, and working on your computers. The view is also nice when you look out the window, depending on where you’re sitting though.

They have this specific Malay section of books, and there’s 3 small sections that are kind of curved in, and about 4-5 people could fit in there. Once you walk a bit farther, there’s this section with teen books, and you’ll have to go down a few steps, but it has a really nice section.

Once you’ve passed the teen section, you’ll see stairs going up. If you go up those stairs, you’ll see the children’s section. First you’ll see the family and parenting section; then to the right, you’ll see the children's books.

Overall, I think the Choa Chu Kang Library is nice and spacious, and has a good selection of different books. Nice area for sitting. A good place to read, study, and work on your computers.

I often use this MRT Station to get to the nearby malls in Bukit Panjang - notably Hillion Mall to which it is "seamlessly connected" and Bukit Panjang Plaza that is just sort of "next door" to Hillion Mall. I also use this MRT Station to get to the Cashew Senja Community Centre. FYI this MRT Station is also at one end of the Downtown Line. By the way, there is a "nice" 7-Eleven convenience store at this MRT Station.

Bukit Panjang MRT/LRT station
The mrt is just beside Hillion Mall the shopping mall in Bukit Panjang is the West of Singapore.  Together with the Hillion Residences condominium that sits above the mall, the property is integrated with the Bukit Panjang bus interchange and LRT and MRT stations. According to the website of Sim Lian Group who is the developer of the project, the mall has 240,000 sq ft of gross floor area and more than 100 retail shops spread over 4 levels.   We don’t like to shop but we love to eat, so what we were really interested in is what to eat at Hillion Mall.  We took a quick tour and compiled a list of the Hillion Mall Food Places and Restaurants.  This is not a complete list as there could be some that we missed out – we suggest you refer to the official tenant directory at Hillion Mall for a full list of shops and restaurants.

Bukit Panjang MRT/LRT station is a Mass Rapid Transit (MRT) and Light Rail Transit (LRT) station on the Downtown line (DTL) and the Bukit Panjang LRT line (BPLRT), at the triple-point boundary of Bukit Panjang, Bukit Batok and Choa Chu Kang. It is located at the junction of Upper Bukit Timah Road and Petir Road in Singapore near Bukit Panjang Plaza and is part of the Bukit Panjang Integrated Transport Hub.

Hillion Mall and Junction 10 will be the nearest malls from here.

There is a 7/11 store, POSB machine and a shop named Rosyth selling snacks and breads  etc.

Many times the display monitor of train arrival not reliable.

I hope management will look into it because public will sometimes opt to take taxi if interval displayed are not correct.

SMRT please do something about the LRT. Having already only one way during off peak hours for repair works since 2019, yet we are still experiencing delays during the peak hours now in 2023. It doesn't make sense to take almost 30mins to get from Segar LRT to Bukit Panjang LRT. It's not even an one time off event. It's consecutive days/weeks. We should be moving forward with time, not deteriorate as time passes. Please buck up. Do deploy buses if required.

review_author_name: Go Red ゴーレッド Matoi Tatsumi

review_text: Bukit Panjang LRT Station - this LRT station that is situated at the doorstep of Hillion Mall and which is somewhat seamlessly connected to Bukit Panjang MRT Station beneath ground level, is my go-to public transport point to go to the neighbourhoods of Bukit Panjang (especially to the two hawker centres therein), or farther on, to Choa Chu Kang (for example to visit the Lot One mall). And whilst this LRT Station has been around for many years, it is still well maintained and kept clean - making its use all the more pleasant and comfortable.
Small train which just serves one small portion of area, like monorail in Kuala Lumpur.....

I like Bukit Panjang LRT Station because it has a features of Bukit Panjang LRT Signals which is colour in red, white & green (Swarco Futura Fahrrad Kleinsignalgeber from European Countries). This was also marks the SIngapore's first ever signal to be installed since 1999. Years ago, they uses the old green signals and replaced by aquamarine in 2016 (Platform 2) to 2020 (Platform 1). Last year, I've started to take photographs of Bukit Panjang LRT rail coloured signals as I was being obsessed.

These signals were made by Swarco Futura Fahrrad Kleinsignalgeber which currently used in Australia (Perth), European countries (Including Austria, Belgium, Czech Republic, Denmark, Finland, France, Germany, Hungary, Italy, Ireland, Lavita, Luxembourg, Norway, Netherlands, Poland, Portugal, Pristina, Romania, Russia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland & United Kingdom), Malaysia [Kuala Lumpur], Qatar [Doha], Saudi Arabia [Riyadh], Singapore [Woodlands Checkpoint, Woodlands Police Division Headquarters, Police Cantonment Complex Restricted Area & Bukit Panjang LRT Line], Syria [Damascus], Thailand [Bangkok & Chiang Mai] & United Arab Emirates [Dubai]).

These signals were similar to Swarco Futura Fahrrad Kleinsignalgeber, Carpark & Cycle Signals from European Countries (Including Austria, Belgium, Czech Republic, Denmark, Finland, France, Germany, Hungary, Italy, Ireland, Lavita, Luxembourg, Norway, Netherlands, Poland, Portugal, Pristina, Romania, Russia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland & United Kingdom).
The LRT aircon is ok, not very strong, but also not weak. It was clean, small
love this place, It is a large station with many amenities

Great to have additional breakfast option at Dairy Farm Mall.  Only the beverages store was opened when we reached around 8.15am.  So we ordered traditional breakfast set, ice milk tea, 2 egg tarts and 2 char siew soh.  The ice milk tea was bland and did not taste much of tea.  The toast were not cut evenly and had a bit of burnt taste.  But the egg tarts and char siew soh were very delicious.  Crunchy on the outside with warm tasty egg fillings for the tarts and sweet bbq pork fillings for the char siew soh.  The coffee is fine too.  Service of the staff were polite and prompt.  Total spent $13+.  Will return again to try other food there.
big store. many seats. not crowded. only weekdays crowded during after school.  now price drop compare to last time. at lvl 2. feel warm maybe aircon not working. some stores accept cashless.

This food court is quite warm and lacks good air circulation, you will be stinky after a meal. There are alot of houseflies too...hope the owner will do something to deter houseflies and reduce temperature or improve air circulation.

1.Curry chicken noodle is not bad
2.Japanese food not bad too
3. Mee rebus was fine and fare priced

Disappointing is the chicken rice

The Indian food stall at the corner next to the drink stall, in my opinion, is a hidden gem. I tried the mutton soup and was pleasantly surprised by the flavor and the thick concentration of the mutton flavor in it. I also tried the mutton and chicken biryani on different days and they were very good as well! I actually told the stall worker that his food is really good. I highly recommend you to give it a try. The portion amount vs the price is really worth it.

The Food Court Food Kakis at Dairy Farm mall has variety of food choices from Malay, indian Japanese Chinese to Western as well.  The prata at Haji Karim stall is very fluffy, not oily and nice. The thosai and chutneys are quite nice too.

place_name: The Coffee Bean & Tea Leaf - Dairy Farm Mall
review_text:
Drinks are fine. But if you're ordering food here....... erm.
I got the mushroom and chicken fusilli and it was quite.. sauceless. We asked if we could have a bit more sauce, and the staff said that it was their "SOP" and they give a certain ml of sauce. Not sure i see any though? I wonder if it's also their SOP where the chicken is rock hard? And it's like garlic pasta.
I used to like this pasta here, but i guess not anymore. :(

place_name: The Coffee Bean & Tea Leaf - Dairy Farm Mall
review_text:
Good CBTL outlet in a new mall. Not too crowded even during the morning peak and with a large number of seats.

Service was good with the batista providing us with recommendations on the dishes available.

place_name: The Coffee Bean & Tea Leaf - Dairy Farm Mall
review_text:
New cafe in Dairy Farm Mall.
Pleasant and spacious.
Love their counter look and the staff on duty was very pleasant.

However, the wafer is not nice. Abit too cool  and hard for my preference.  The eggs are fantastically done. Coffee is nice too.

place_name: The Coffee Bean & Tea Leaf - Dairy Farm Mall
review_text:
genuinely wondering if the barista here actually go through training? on how to steam a milk? because i've been to a couple of cbtl outlets and they have been giving me this much foam. am i drinking a hot chocolate cappuccino????????????????
i can't help to think how $7-8 of my money is WASTED and just gone through the wind over this ridiculous drink.

place_name: The Coffee Bean & Tea Leaf - Dairy Farm Mall
review_text: Nice addition to the Dairy Farm Mall! It’s brand new so atmosphere is clean and modern! Good consistency of service and food provided. Usually plenty of seats regardless of timing I’m there. Only gripe would be that there are no PowerPoints!

Tried the salted egg chicken, cereal prawn, prawn paste chicken, kai lan and tie ban tofu - great taste and yummy all around! Service was fast, we only waited around 15min from ordering. Food was freshly cooked. Would recommend to try!!!

Newly opened and service here is fast, which makes dining here convenient. It’s a bit pricier than your usual zi char, but with aircon and the quality of the food, it’s well worth it. The place does get overly smoky at times, though that’s more about the venue’s poor ventilation.
Very good zichar, will definitely come back again.

Absolutely terrible experience. I asked for non spicy kang kong and they gave me sambal. I asked for salted fish fried rice they gave me seafood fried rice. Honestly disappointing. If a restaurant can't get even its orders right, its a lost cause. If you go there, check your orders. What a joke.

My wife and I were here yesterday evening (22 JUNE 2026) for dinner. It was the first time for the both of us trying this zi char stall at Food Kakis at Dairy Farm Mall. We ordered the following: a Fish Head Curry, a plate of Qing Long Vegetables and two plates of rice. At only $16, the Fish Head Curry was excellent - it came in a huge claypot, bubbling away, with a decent amount of fish meat, quite a bit of vegetables and lots of curry sauce. As for Qing Long Vegetables it was nice enough. By the way, the Fish Head Curry usually costs $33. Service here was good - friendly and obliging. The dining ambience here was good as it was in air-conditioned comfort.

UPDATE OF 18 JULY 2026
I was here with my wife, our daughter and son-in-law for dinner. We ordered the following: a Fish Head Curry, a Fried Lala with Ginger, a Hotplate Tofu with Prawns and Egg, and a Fried Kangkong. As expected, all of the aforementioned were very good, as was the service.

Came here quite late at night so not many people but was able to hear nice music. Came here for the Sashimi but stayed for the Oysters and Kushiyaki. So worth it we had to get a second round.

The service were also amazing, so kind to us and attentive when we needed them! They talked to us and interacted, and found out the Fugu is named Max!

Definitely a good place to come for a chillaxing evening. Will definitely return ^-^

very good dinner crowd on a weekend night in spite of its far flung location, so i had high expectations but was a lil let down

yakitori was def the highlight. skewers were actually very well grilled (takes 20-30min to prep) but missing the added touch of sansho pepper / chilli powder flakes

karaage was also well fried. crispy and juicy but didn't have much taste

aburi salmon roll was my least fave. fish didn't taste the freshest but not rly a problem when masked w aburi and mentaiko

menu is very very extensive - in addition to the sushi and yakitori offerings that we tried, they also have ramen, dons, CEREMONIAL matcha, alcohol etc. w a menu that has something for everyone from young to old it is wonder they draw huge crowds of families / residents in the dairy farm neighbourhood.

while the food was certainly not horrible, i kinda expected more at this price point ($120 for 2 pax with 2 drinks) and given how popular it is

Visited this Japanese restaurant for dinner and was really impressed with the authentic flavors. The food was fresh and well-prepared, and the service was friendly too. Definitely a great spot to satisfy your Japanese food cravings. Just be prepared, it gets quite busy during weekend dinners, so expect a bit of a wait. Still, it’s worth it if you’re in the mood for something tasty and comforting.

This place really stands out for its excellent customer care. And you can tell they really try ti get the best source. While I felt that the sushi and oysters were really fresh, it’s probably a value buy since it lacks a sweetness that comes with awesome produce. But then again it wasn’t too expensive so I guess not too bad for the price you pay. Nice sake selection and always a good place to have a beer. Perhaps some hh pricing? 😆 the chicken skin was the best!

This place blew us away! The star was a massive 3.2kg hamachi served four ways: buttery sashimi, perfectly grilled, melt-in-your-mouth sushi, and another delicious prep (maybe the collar?). The flying fish sashimi was super fresh too.

But wait – the yakitori stole the show! Juicy, smoky skewers (try the chicken thigh or tsukune) that were absolutely addictive.

Service was on point – friendly, fast, and they knew their stuff. No complaints, just awesome food from start to finish. Would 100% come back!

I patron this cafe a couple times and during my visit I noticed several issues that affected the overall customer experience.

The aircon needs fixing,I thought its just hot during the day but I came at night  and its still humid.
I cannot imagine how it was for the employees to be working in such condition. The air coolers  thats scattered was not helping.

Secondly, almost all the time that I visit.
Its a pity that the same full time staff is running the whole show despite having a junior staff,assuming they are part timers that are not efficient. Most of the time talking to chefs and very inattentive.

As a service/ front line personnel myself, its such a disappointing sight to see.

Management need to do better in terms of cafe atmosphere and manpower planning.

Nonetheless, I would to thank service staff Jo, Sashu and An  for always going extra mile everytime I come to visit.
I hope this feedback would be helpful for your welfare. I would personally rate 5 stars to you all but your part timers need to be assessed.

See you guys again and hopefully,its for the better.

really love the environment here, gives off a very chill n calm atmosphere really great place if you’re coming here for a meetup or meeting. the food was also very good, portion size worth the price. Most importantly the staff Adeline and Sashu was really attentive and friendly, answered all my questions and gave suggestions base on my liking. F&B industry should really have more staff like this really brightens my mood after a long day. Kudos!

Some places simply get the basics right, and when they do, the whole experience becomes quietly memorable.

This branch, located near the German European School Singapore, clearly understands bread. Not just good bread, but the kind that reminds you how special it can be when it is made properly. Their selection includes excellent German style breads that are surprisingly rare to find in Singapore. Dense, flavourful loaves with that beautiful deep crust and slightly tangy crumb that feels truly authentic.

Our meal started with the Scrambled Eggs with Smoked Salmon, which was exactly what a good brunch dish should be. Soft, creamy eggs paired with generous slices of smoked salmon, served on beautifully toasted bread that held everything together perfectly.

The Fully Loaded Chicken Sandwich was equally satisfying. A hearty sandwich stacked with tender chicken and fresh ingredients, again served on their excellent bread which really made the dish stand out.

What truly impressed us was the bakery counter. The pastry and dessert section looked irresistible, and we ended up choosing the Chocolate Almond Caramel Tart. It turned out to be the highlight of the visit. Rich chocolate, nutty almond and deep caramel layered together in a beautifully balanced tart that was indulgent without feeling overly heavy.

Beyond the food, the atmosphere was warm and welcoming. The staff were genuinely friendly and attentive, which made the whole experience feel relaxed and enjoyable.

Between the excellent bread, the satisfying brunch dishes and that unforgettable Chocolate Almond Caramel Tart, it was a fantastic meal from start to finish. One of those places that makes you want to come back just for the bakery alone.

Corner restaurant at the Dairy Farm Mall. We had the Big Breakfast. It was nice, but somehow the bacon was a bit lacking in taste.
The Mango & Pineapple Smoothie was not bad.

Service wasn't as good. Staff didn't really welcome and serve guests to a table and menu upon walking in. Food was rather pricey for the quantity and quality served, about $16 for a quiche and mini salad.

Restaurant was too quiet that customers could hear each other.

I really doubt able to pull crowd to this place with limited carpark and not really anything to shout about .

I guess they only serving the Germain School and nearby Condominiums.

I came in late afternoon because happen to be there and the place since quiet for lunch crowd which I like. 🤭😋

Poor service when it comes to parking securities. Pick up and taxi stands, the cars are making it as their parking lots as they don’t want to pay a $1.50 for parking. Security can see the car are parking on the Taxi stands without the driver inside and yet security are not doing any actions. Improved please!!

Great place! There is a food court (2 stalls left excluding the drinks store) and fairprice and other shops. I just feel that there is not enough business in the food court. Fairprice is great. Will definitely go again.
Newly opened foodcourt with mixed food choices at affordable prices! Nice!

**A hidden gem tucked away from the hustle!** 🏢🌿

Located at the quiet intersection of Dairy Farm and Petir Road, this is a small but surprisingly well-rounded neighbourhood mall. It doesn't have the glitz of a mega-mall, but it has all the essentials and a very convenient, laid-back vibe. It’s a true convenience hub for the local community.

**What's inside:**
You’ll find a solid mix here—a few casual eateries, several enrichment centres for the kids, a medical clinic for peace of mind, and a **True Fitness** club if you want to break a sweat. The anchor tenant is a decent-sized **NTUC FairPrice** which is perfect for a quick grocery run without having to travel far.

**My Highlights:**
✅ **Luckin Coffee** This is a major plus point for me. Unlike the tiny, cramped kiosks you see elsewhere, this outlet is spacious with **ample seating** all inside air-conditioned comfort. It’s a great spot to bunker down with a laptop or just hide from the heat with a book.

Anywhere with a FairPrice is always appreciated, much more so when it’s a finest! It’s not the biggest of the FP finest around but it sure has a wide variety of stuff and is a great place to do your grocery shopping. The meats and Japanese food section always have good finds towards the end of the day with reduced prices as well !

I live at Dairy Farm Estate and not at Dairy Farm Residences. This NTUC that is just 10 minutes walk from my door is a very convenient supermarket for me to visit not just for the usual supermarket groceries but also to get some RTEs or Ready To Eats. And that is I love about this NTUC supermarket here - the RTE kiosks therein; in particular, the Delicato roasted meats and rice kiosk, and the Polar Puffs and Cakes kiosk as well. Today I took away a value for money Bento Rice Set 2 that came with generous portions of roasted pork belly and roasted duck slices on a bed of plain white rice but with some nice savoury gravy sauce and chilli sauce - all for just $4.95. I also took away some very good as always desserts from the Polar Puffs and Cakes kiosk - an eclair and a marble cake slice. By the way, the NTUC staff at the self checkout are always friendly and helpful.

Latest 2023 mart as big as BPP
not a mega mart like Balestier
Lots of canned / processed varieties
not enough fresh greens / seafood

Roasts, cuts, cheeses & sushi here
ceiling type full glass freezer & latest gear
convenient waist high type push  trolleys
and newest checkouts (less frustrating)

Cozy aisles, friendly staffs
Missing German / Dairy Farm footprint
given its location in front of GESS / DF
needs crowd pull as not near MRT too
Lives long & prosper to serve community
(Opens 8am to 10pm)

Not sure why I have problem touching the metal part in this NTUC. Will get static shock whenever i touch the fridge door handle and metal rack. 🤨. Anyway it's self checkout here. Friendly checkout staff. Not that crowded here.

The quality of the Farm Fresh milk is anything but fresh, and is getting from bad to worse, and my experience is that the milk in 2 out of 10 bottles of Farm Fresh milk would be bad (few times became like bad yogurt) even opened 1 or 2 weeks from the expiry dates and kept at the top shelf of my new fridge.

Even for the other cartons which are not overly bad, they would not taste as good as before. Taste funny frequently like about to go bad.

I have switched completely to the more expensive but much more consistently good Magnolia milk which has been improved and is perfect for the espresso coffee I make at home. If you feel the thickness of the carton boxes of Magnolia milk versus that of Farm Fresh, you would understand why the Farm Fresh milk is often bad or borderline

This shop has to close down at neighbourhoods and open at industrial areas. Doesn’t give exact change and expects cash. Shouldn’t visit their stalls at all and should complain to HDB to remove all their stores around HDB area.
There is another larger M28 Mini Mart just a block away at Segar Grove.
Though well stocked, the store (Keat Hong) only has cash-only as a payment option. As we are moving forward to cashless payment (Paynow, Pay wave), I feel the "cash-only" option is a set back.
The minimart is convenient, but better count your change! Was overcharged a few times. Always managed to get the amount back and the excuse is usually "scanned extra, sorry"
It's a cheap and well-stocked mini mart that's a perfect compliment to the community.  It's really convenient to have a shop downstairs that has all the daily necessities.

4 stars because have Sheng Siong. Other than the 2 mini-mart like market stall (one run by Chinese aunty and another by an Indian auntie), others sell vegetables or typical produce. Also available are the stalls selling Chinese prayer items. Used to have a halal meat produce stall that sells marinated satay meat, but since renovation can't seem to find the stall anymore. Some previous vendor like the stall selling fresh noodles and tofu have shifted to the hawker/market at Bangkit.

Fajar wet market is relatively small with 2 fresh pork stalls, 2 vegetable stall, a fresh YTF stall, 1 Indian and 1 Chinese provision shop. Here's also the Fajar SC with Sheng Siong and Giant, both 24hr; Macdonald and 7eleven. more shops... selling cake n bread, mobile phone, medical hall, ABC shop, a coffee shop and a AC food court. SG Poolz, music school at the 2nd level
Got 2 supermarket n wet market. Comprehensive choice of groceries n 2 coffeeshops for hawker food.
A neighborhood convenient center that has food centre, supermarket, wet market, hard ware shop and many more
Went for service koufu's automatic door

I had the privilege of attending the Barbell Workshop conducted by our very own Master Trainer, Coach Fad on 26 April, 2026.   I  always thought that the barbell is very heavy and you need to be very strong to lift it.  After the class, I learnt that it is all about using the correct techniques safely.  We had fun and many hands on practice on the Sunday morning.   We also had many chances to refine our techniques and build our confidence along the way.  Really appreciate the patience of Coach Fad, Coach Mat and Coach Nas and their time.  I am looking forward to more workshops conducted by Coach Fad and team!

The community at BFT Dairy Farm is still okay and I’ve been lucky enough to get to know a bunch of nice gym-goers here. 🤪🤣

I’ve always loved the vibes in class whenever Tim was coaching! He has a great way of making the sessions engaging while still being very attentive to everyone's needs.

After learning about my knee issues, Tim was consistently proactive in making sure I didn't overextend myself. He always did his best to modify exercises for me, ensuring I could stay active safely. Even on days when I didn't want to be a bother and tried to step aside, he would always come over to make sure I wasn't forgotten and had an alternative workout to do.

The same level of care and support goes for Jen, Karissa, Nat, and Kye. They are all so observant and always make sure I’m included and doing something that works for my body. I really appreciate these trainers for looking out for my safety and keeping the energy high!

One of the bigger bft gyms with three units per station. The music goes loud and soft which I found quite endearing. The coaches are above average friendly and so are the coachees. Will definitely come back again.

All the coaches are very friendly and approachable. Especially appreciate the additional trainings like the Barbell masterclass organised by head coach Fad recently, that gave me a lot more confidence in my technique for squats and bench press 👍

BFT Dairy Farm is easily accessible via public/private transport.

The trainers at BFT Dairy Farm are knowledgeable, passionate, and dedicated to helping members achieve their goals. Their expertise and guidance made all the difference in my experience, and I appreciated their patience and encouragement throughout.

Overall, I highly recommend BFT Dairy Farm for its great location and outstanding trainers. It's a great place to learn, grow, and connect with like-minded individuals. Five stars and well done, BFT Diary Farm!

place_name: Dairy Farm Park Connector Fitness Station
Dairy Farm Park Connector Fitness Station
Senja Cashew Swimming Complex (The Swimming Place)
review_text: There’s this young lifeguard that making his own rules. My daughter wants to go in to jacuzzi with her 12 years old brother, but lifeguard ask her to come out but when I told him that that she will be accompanying by her older brother he answer me that not allowed as it needs to be an adult. Meanwhile there’s this another siblings that my daughter attend the same swimming class same age with my children. They also brought their younger sister in the jacuzzi and he allowed them to play there for so long. When I confronted this lifeguard he insisted that the parents of this 3 sisters are inside when I insisted that I know this 3 sisters and I pointed that their parents are sitting down on the bench he just ignored me and not explain anything. I understand he is just doing his job but he should do it equally to all and the rules should applied to all. We are in this swimming pool every weekend as my kids attend swimming lesson here. I saw a lot of siblings go in to jacuzzi with their younger siblings. I accompany my daughter too when I’m feeling better. Photo attached my son in the jacuzzi and there's 2 kids I saw just go in and he just ignored them. They are even younger than my 12 year old. 😡

place_name: Senja Cashew Swimming Complex (The Swimming Place)
review_text: I’ve been swimming here almost every weekend, and the pool is generally clean and clear. I also appreciate that the water isn’t too cold, which is great since I can’t stand the cold. The pool is usually not too crowded as well especially during 3-5pm. Overall, it’s a good pool for doing laps. However, I’m not a fan of the mini pool beside the slides, it tends to be quite dirty.

place_name: Senja Cashew Swimming Complex (The Swimming Place)
review_text:
Nice and clean pool facilities. It seems like one of the newer/improved pool complexes around. With the booking system in place, the pool was not crowded, with around 3-4 people using 2 laps.

The kids’ water play area looks fun - like a normal playground but over water. Do note that each kid above 1 year old needs to have an ActiveSG account and they need to have a slot booked together with adults to enter.

place_name: Senja Cashew Swimming Complex (The Swimming Place)
review_text:
Thus is one of the many public pool in Singapore. It consists of one standard 50m main pool and a water feature play pool for kids.
It is crowded most of the time as it is used the members of the public and also swim school conducting swimming classes.
The toilet have been renovated and it comes with rain shower.
Normal operating hours have resumed as of June 2022
The pool is closed whole day on Wednesday for maintenance.
The kids play pool is under maintenance ( SEP 2023)

place_name: Senja Cashew Swimming Complex (The Swimming Place)
review_text: The swimming complex is nice, but at the children playground pool - the movable parts are either stucked or clogged. Unrepaired for a number of years.

chill and cozy place for couples to spend some time together.

The room feels warm the moment you step in. You can tell the owners put thought into the decor. Soft lighting and a pleasant scent. Room feels clean.

The staff was friendly and helpful. When i needed help with the TV, she provided it promptly and added some time back for the technical delay, even though its not their fault.

Definitely enjoyed my experience.

Visited CosyPlace Bukit Timah on 6 May and initially had an unpleasant experience due to the attitude and tone of one of the staff members onsite. However, I would like to acknowledge that the management team handled the matter very promptly and professionally after receiving my feedback.

They responded quickly, took the issue seriously, and provided reassurance that corrective actions were taken. I appreciate the efficiency and professionalism shown in addressing the situation and improving their customer service standards.

Thank you to the management team for handling the feedback responsibly.

Love this location at first sight. The tv was huge. Booked to watch concert and feels like im in the concert. Very cozy and clean. The host was so nice and friendly. Will definitely come here again. There is a lot of foods here too.
Room is quite spacious, sofa very very comfy, big enough to lie flat on, air conditioned and tv+sound system are perfect!!

This is truly a premier spot for private dates and birthday celebrations! Having booked here several times now, I can confidently give it a 10/10. The space is consistently clean, the sound system is excellent, and I love having full access to entertainment like Netflix and Disney+. To top it off, the prices are very affordable, and they offer great discounts for students. Highly recommended!

This Playground is actually named as part of Chestnut Close Park (as per the sign thereat), but it is (located) in the Chestnut Drive Outlet Drain area (as per the relevant photo herein). In any case, this Playground is very popular with residents nearby in the vicinities of Dairy Farm (Road, Crescent, Walk and Lane), Chestnut (Drive, Crescent and Avenue) and Jalan Petir - as attested by the people using it, especially in the evenings. While this Playground has play equipment for the young, there is nearby an exercise station and public toilet just metres away from the Park, across the "Drain."

Took a walk down a quiet Chestnut Close park to enjoy the mid autumn lighting put up. Nice lighting to bring up the festive mood but rather small scale amidst a long park. Hope the lights can stretch from Petir Rd to this park area will be more beautiful and attractive.

Stumbled across this charming park cum playground on one of my walks. It's a lovely place for a walk, and of course there are swings and stuff for kids. Walking in from Petir Road, it cuts across to Upper Bukit Timah Road. This period there's hardly anyone there especially during the day, so if you need a quiet place to recharge yourself, head on down.
Nice playground along the peaceful and well-lit park connector. It has swings, see saw and roundabout.

Love this part of Hillview
Long stretch along Chestnut
Links to Petir Bukit Panjang area
The long kang also looks very nice
Open car park with lots of space. Easy access for hikers to Bt Timah summit,  Wallace Trail yFree parking from 6.30 to 8.30am.
A good hiking trail in Singapore. It can be difficult for beginners and not so fit persons but manageable in general.
Famous natural for hiking and jogging

Nice carpark where toilets are just next to it. The carpark provides free parking in the morning from 0630 to 0830, thus very good for those who hike around dairy farm and bukit timah. The path to bukit timah requires to climb several flights of stairs, however it would be great workouts.

An alternative entry pony to Bukit Timah MTN Trail. Car park is quite full during early morning.

There is foot washing point and toilets available.

Good spacious natural green with huge open area condo. Apartments are spacious in size and well maintained.
HillView mrt station downtown line is just 2 mins walking distance. Downtown mrt station is 27 mins away from hillview mrt station. Giant, fairprice is one mrt station away and cold storage is at walking distance.
The big nature park is at the opposite side.
Amenities include swimming pool, tennis courts,squash courts, table tennis, barbecue, children play area and mini supermarket inside the  condo.

Just finished 8 year lease. Not the nice place it used to be. Influx of new people who have little regard for community living. Make a lot of noise at the swimming  pool like Ibiza till late. Always have children screaming at playground until 8-9pm. Beware pool facing units. Hdb upgraders like to dump food containers at the lift lobbies, attracting rats. The lifts are old, frequently stop working. Increase in number of smokers. You have to dry your clothes indoors and close your windows all the time otherwise the cigarette smoke comes Iin and stick. Old estate also means constant renovations. Not a week goes by without hearing jackhammer. Otherwise it’s chainsaws and grass cutters. When you think you have a quiet weekend to spend at home, someone will have house party making noise till late. Security guards don’t care. Always water pressure problems so must keep bucket of water standby. Termites bad also. Cannot complain to council, they threaten to sue you. Uncle minimart store is the only consolation.
Big landscape, it's like living in a resort. It won't be easy for the owners to agree with en bloc proposal. Good luck to all the owners and the appointed agency - Propnex
Very spacious units and well maintained common areas.

It's up for en bloc. But if I lived there,  I wouldn't.
You'll never find such spaciousness anymore.
A decent pool.  But maintenance needs to be improved!!! Like more frequent n thorough cleaning for hygienic reasons.

Dairy Farm Quarry is a quiet spot along the trail, but there isn’t really much to stop and see compared to other nearby quarries. The surroundings are mostly greenery and open space, which can be pleasant for a short pause during a walk or jog, but it doesn’t have a strong “wow” factor or a dedicated viewing area

It works more as a pass-through point rather than a destination on its own. If you’re already exploring the area, it’s fine to swing by, but it’s probably not worth making a special trip just for this quarry

There’s a nice short path from Dairy Farm Road to the old quarry, which is shared by mountain bikers. Emerging from the forest, one sees an expansive wide grassy area with the natural rock formations at the far end. A very nice place to relax and take in the scenery. Note that there are wild monkeys around - so don’t bring food nor picnic here.

Previously rock climbing was allowed in the quarry but in recent years, the govt have sealed off the entrances to the rock walls with planting of plants and signs, due to previous accidents.

⛰️Dairy Farm Quarry was a disused granite quarry site, similar to others around Bukit Timah Nature Reserve area which ceased operation under orders from the Singapore Government in 1970.
⛰️But, unlike other quarries that eventually turn to a wetland, Dairy Farm Quarry was filled in with earth and grass over.
🏔️The picturesque lush vegetation covered granite cliff and its serene surrounding is a popular destination for both hikers and mountain bikers.
⛰️The Quarry is located at a short walk from Hillview MRT station.
The jungle trail is rocky at some stretch.
⛰️Note:
🕸️There are no toilet or water point.
🏔️It is a dead end for hikers, while the mountain bikers can continue towards Wallace Trail via mountain bike trail.
🐵Beware of monkey troops.
🐒Do not carry food in the open.
tried this path and a short walk later ended up here. It's ok. not much to see around, but worth a short walk

The Dairy Farm Quarry was a disused granite quarry site, similar to others around BUKIT NATURE RESERVE area, which ceased quarrying operations in 1970. But, unlike other quarries that eventually turned into a wetland, Dairy Farm Quarry was filled in with earth and grassed over. The picturesque, lush vegetation covered granite cliff, and its serene surrounding is a popular destination for both hikers and mountain bikers. The Quarry is located at a short walk from Hillview MRT station. A sealed pathway runs past the DAIRY FARM OUTDOOR ADVENTURE CENTER. There's signposting along the way to keep you going in the right direction. Look for sign pointing to the DAIRY FARM QUARRY. There's a jungle trail which is rocky at some stretch.
It is a dead end for hikers, while the mountain bikers can continue towards Wallace Trail via mountain bike trail.

Safe travels,

Mega.

As a resident of the nearby The Dairy Farm Estate, I love this "Park Connector" or in my mind, my "Park" just outside or across the road (i.e. Dairy Farm Crescent) from where I live. This "Park" while smallish in size and simple in design is really such an oasis for residents nearby - and it even has an exercise station therein with several exercise "machines" for "Park" users. To enter this "Park", one could do so, either from Upper Bukit Timah Road (with 2 entry points of its own: 1) near the zebra crossing at the Upper Bukit Timah Road and  Dairy Farm Road junction, or 2) from Upper Bukit Timah near the aforementioned exercise station), or from Dairy Farm Crescent (near one end of the Chestnut Canal).
Greener than green.

place_name: My First Skool at Blk 443A Fajar Road

review_text: Alhamdulillah a place where I feel at ease when my children are in. Teachers do not hesitate to come forward to ask the follow-up they need to do about my children well being. Teachers continuously updating about my children and their progress. From JS my children learn loads about the importance of being a Muslim child. Definitely a student care which I recommend to all Muslim parents. Kudos to JS @ Fajar team.

My son enjoys going to JS. In JS, there are lots of program queuing for our kids to explore and expose. Eg, in the recent months, they went to Masjid Kampung Wak Hassan in Sembawang. They learnt the etiquette while in the Masjid and experienced the feeling of 'kampung'. Not only that, there are lessons like arabic learning for the kids too.
It is a good form of exposure to our kids.

Junior Seekers at Fajar is like a second home for my children. Alhamdulillah their time spend are filled with fun meaningful activities & enrichment classes. JS also provides them with a holistic care and development. Highly recommended for Muslim parents.
My children is extremely happy and loves attending the June holiday program conducted @ Junior Seekers .. thank u!
My son had wonderful experience there for the holiday camp.

Testimonial for Stepping Stones
Over my four years in Stepping Stones since 2022, I  had a fruitful experience as I benefitted a lot and enjoyed the lessons. Looking back, there were many highlights.  My English tuition teacher, Mr Wong,  often cracks jokes so the lessons are not mundane.  Seeing the class bursts into laughter made the atmosphere livelier and made my experience in Stepping Stones memorable. Meanwhile, Mr Wong is strict and serious when students do not submit homework or repeat the same mistakes. He is patient as he explains the comprehension corrections clearly and more than once when necessary. Having explained the questions, he would always encourage us to ask questions to clarify our doubts. In addition, he made use of Google classroom for convenient access to the notes and worksheets.Through regular practice every week,  I was exposed to a plethora of challenging comprehension questions and learned many new skills .   Moreover, the  essay notes were in-depth and consisted of many sentence frames we could reuse in our essays. We were also provided with sample essays to look through. I will remember his teachings and unwavering support. Thank you, Mr Wong, for being a dedicated, patient and humorous teacher. He is truly responsible for the class and tracks our grades consistently.

Tiffany , Secondary Four (2025)

I would like to take this opportunity to thank Stepping Stones Study Centre for the excellent tutoring and all the support you've given my son for his whole tuition journey in preparation for his O-Levels. His grades does shows you guys had prepared him well.
I would personally thanks Mr Leong who goes above and beyond in making sure my son, Iryan Naufal is well prepared for his A Maths and E Maths. He's always available for extra help, provides excellent resources, offers constant encouragement and even extra lesson. I still remember my son wanted to opt out A Maths but because of Mr Leong assurance to me that he will do his best to improve my son grade, I told my son to hold fort. Particularly for A Maths, his grade had improved from a C5 to a B3 while his E Maths had achieved A2. His dedication truly made a difference in our son's preparation for his Maths.
Once again, thank you.

I joined stepping stones english tuition in Sec 4 after getting a B3 for my Sec 3 end-of-year English exams. Through Mr Wong's lessons, I was able to better understand how to structure my essays, analyse questions more accurately, and express my ideas clearer. His guidance helped me to improve steadily, and I was able to achieve consistent A1s for my English exams in Sec 4. Eventually, I scored an A2 for my O-Level English. I would highly recommend this tuition to students who are currently struggling with english, or who want to improve their current language skills.

I was in SSSC from mid-2024 till my O-Levels in 2025. I joined because while my E Maths had always been good, my competency in A Maths was inadequate and I struggled to consistently attain a passing grade. Under Mr Leong's thoughtful guidance and resources, I was able to review concepts like integration and understand them in my own way, and finally scored a B3 for A Maths (A1 EM). I thank you for helping me improve from a C6-D7 to a B3 in a year and a half.

The learning environment at SSSC (or perhaps just under Mr Leong) encourages autonomy. I come in setting my own goals each week for what I want to accomplish in 2-4 hours. There is no rigid lesson plan so I got to decide what topics to focus on and if I ran into problems, I just asked the teacher. Aside from the odd interesting questions that the teacher brought up, most of the work is up for the student to decide. Resources are also printed and given out on request, so don't hesitate to ask for more practice if you wish to improve. I believe that this environment will benefit those who are self-driven and want to do well/improve. But don't take my word for it, try it out and see if it fits your style of learning.

I am a student and took my O-level in 2025, and would like to show my greatest appreciation to Mr Leong, Mr Wong, and Mr Lex for playing such a huge role in my educational journey.

Mr Leong is a dedicated and caring math teacher who played a huge role in helping me achieve a B3 in both A Math and E Math. Despite having a packed class schedule, he consistently gave us extra lessons and never hesitated to provide additional support. What truly sets him apart is his ability to help each student individually, tailoring his guidance to our different needs.

Mr Wong is an exceptional English teacher who helped me achieve an A2 in English. He prepares clear and detailed notes for every component of the subject and always does his best to ensure that everyone can understand and remember them. His witty jokes related to English not only brighten our days but also make lessons more engaging and memorable. He is also very innovative, moving our work online to make learning more efficient.

Mr Lex is an inspiring and supportive chemistry teacher who helped me improve from an E8 to a B4. His way of explaining chemistry concepts is very interesting and unique, which made them much easier to understand and remember. Beyond academics, he also shared valuable advice that was applicable not only in the classroom but in everyday life. His encouragement, patience, and dedication motivated me to work harder and believe in myself.

Rude seller at the small provision shop(chinese guy). I came to buy mineral water, donno how many times he rolled eyes at me & throw the change. I think he got mental problem.😅 I dont wish to cross path with him again.

Near to the LRT station, you can find something to eat at Dakar shopping centre:)

You also can walk to the Chi hock Keng temple from this station:)
One of the noisy stop. Will avoid if possible

A small LRT station, has a mini mart at the ground floor. Acess to the nearby Fajar shopping mall and it's HDB estates. Saw a lot of student using the station during the peak and off peak hours. It has 2 fare machines for people to top up their ezlink cards. Overall, a small station and nothing remarkable.
Convenient location to amenities - Supermarket, coffeeshops. Its the “central station” of the BP LRT loop!!!
Interesting area with lots of house hold items. Good prices!

The mid station of the lrt line - has a McD 24 hrs and a small mall with Koufu n food court , bakeries.
Wet market and SS too.
My girlfriend stay next to the station

Can park at multi- stores carpark Blk 446A. Now H2A grace period 20 mins. Can also Park opposite the shopping center on sundays and public holidays since it’s free. Just beside Fajar LRT. Shopping center has 2 coffee shops and lots of food options. Macdonalds and Chinese Medical Hall, Giant supermarket too. Very convenient! 2nd floor has 2 hair salon too.
Small shopping area at Fajar that has wet market and Giant supermarket inside with many other stalls and clinic in this small mall. This mall is open concept meaning there is no Aircon...
Quite messy as they are doing upgrading works
Neighborhood shopping centre that comes with fast food, supermarkets, eateries and plenty of shops for daily necessities.
HDB shopping area: Fajar wet market, Giant Supermart, 2 kopitiams, Macdonald, Bakery shops, TCM, Singapore Pools, Music School, Childcare Centre, etc

Went to tabao coffee C korsong as usual on visit to Fajar. Here only 1.40 a pack and taste is good! Cheaper than the coffee shop below my void deck. The guy makes the Kopi very good service and friendly as well. The mee siam also not bad. Like the chicken rice but too bad I heard the chicken rice is moving out soon. Lack of variety at the coffee shop. Hope can bring in new tenants soon.

Roasted Delights stall has some fantastic roasted dish. From roasted chicken rice, wanton noodle, roasted duck noodles are all taste amazing. I always go back to this shop whenever i go to Fajar shopping Centre.

I would like to remind my fellow customers and patrons to be observant. Now that there is hardly much Covid cases, not every person preparing food in this food court is wearing masks(covering mouth and nose) while working.

Try and notice who does not wear masks while cooking and/or handling plates/utensils. Try and observe whether the person seems to be ill like coughing but not wearing mask when handling food and utensils.

Be careful.

Local food court offering local food dishes. Have the breakfast set A from the Beverages stall. The coffee is surprisingly better than average coffee shops. The Kaya Butter bun and eggs are nicely done. Value for money.
Nice ambiene, clean environment. Western food is nice.
Teo Chew Fish Porridge. Fresh Sliced Batang  Porridge. Teo Chew Cuisine. Local Favourite Coffee Foodies. Yu Pan Fish Porridge.

This is for the “Nasi Lemak” store.

I really enjoy the food here and have been back many times because the flavours are consistently good. However, I’ve noticed a big inconsistency in portion size depending on who serves you. Unfortunately, when served by an elderly lady with short white hair, the portions are noticeably much smaller. This has happened repeatedly despite multiple visits, and it does affect the overall experience and value for money.

I hope the stall can look into standardising portion sizes so that customers get a consistent experience each time.

To the drink stall. Very disappointing experience. I ordered Kapixibing, but I received a hot coffee instead of the drink I asked for. Then I came back to buy a canned green tea, the staff's attitude was extremely rude.

The staff( short-haired, heavier-set woman) spoke in an unfriendly manner, and when I wasn't sure whether she was talking to me, she became impatient. She then slammed the cup and immediately moved on to serve the next customer. At the point, she scolded me "not knowing how to reply when someone is talking" in chinese.

Poor service                                                            Poor attitude
Good place to buy nice vegetarian food at Tanaka Vegetarian Stall. They also sell nice kueh kueh & yam cake.
The popular neighbourhood coffeeshop in Bukit Panjang area. Nice noodle stall, carrot cake, fried prawn mee, western food, roast meat, nasi kukus, economic rice and vegetarian.

Dined at GreenDot Bt Panjang Plaza a couple of times for meals. Usually dinner. The food here is delicious and flavourful. The staff preparing the food did make effort to display/arrange the food nicely in a presentable way. This enhances our appetite too. The staff here are friendly, helpful and with good customer service.

As I am a GreenDot member. The staff at the counter will address me by my name and informed me that I have accumulated points/dollars to be used and checked with me if I would like to use it.

This is a personalized service which I do appreciate.

Thank you.

I frequently eat at Greendot in Bukit Panjang Plaza, and it has consistently been a reliable vegetarian Chinese option in the area.
The food is tasty, especially the vegetarian rendang and gu lu rou, and I really like the complimentary lotus soup they serve. I’ve been there many times and am consistently impressed by the quality and standards they maintain. A solid choice if you’re looking for satisfying vegetarian food.
The soup of the soup noodle is nice. The food is pretty decent I think (seldom eat vegetarian food). Still, I will only come when I have to accommodate to vegetarian friends. It's expensive.

One of my favourites vegan restaurants, and this outlet at Bukit Panjang tops them all. Extremely patient, helpful and friendly staff and the ambience is wonderful. I like this gem in the heartland. Donkey and I were in the mood for a movie treat, and we decided to cycle from Punggol to Jurong Jem for the movie.  Unfortunately, we found out that the cinema was closed down. We decided to head to Choa Chu Kang Lot 1 Shopping Centre to catch a movie at their Shore Theatre instead.

After the movie, a really nice worker from the theatre guided us to the lift because the escalators were only going up, and we needed to get our trifold bike down to level 1. Rifle Range Nature Park is a great extension to the Bukit Timah nature area, offering a mix of easy trails, lush rainforest scenery, and interesting glimpses into Singapore’s natural and historical heritage. The park is well connected, with clearly marked paths, boardwalks, and links to nearby nature reserves

One unexpected highlight was spotting a durian tree - and even seeing fallen durians on the ground, a reminder of how rich and diverse the forest ecosystem is. Along the trails, you’ll also find impressive giant trees, dense vegetation, quarry views, and remnants of the area’s past hidden among the greenery

I loved Rifle Range Nature Park — it’s very friendly for children and the elderly. The boardwalks are well-paved, anti-slip, and mostly barrier-free, so it’s easy for prams and seniors to walk comfortably.

The access is super convenient: just take Beauty World MRT Exit A, go to Level 4 of Beauty World Centre, and cross the overhead bridge that leads directly into the park. Highlights include the Quarry Wetland, Colugo Deck, and a fun Forest Exploration Trail for kids. Highly recommended for a relaxed family nature walk.

Nice new park with natural trails and paved paths. Has many unspoilt flora and fauna, saw several durian and starfruit trees along the paths. Not so easy to get to if depending on public transport. Toilets and carpark are available and some paths r equipped with CCTV! Not crowded on a weekday morning.

A nice trek away from the hustle and bustle of the city located in the Bukit Timah area of Singapore. Amidst a lush green rain forest with thriving ecosystems Easy to access and to reach on foot and public transport from the nearest MRT station called Beauty World - Downtown Line (Blue Coloured Line). An overhead bridge links one of the entrances of the park to the roof top Food Court (highest floor) of Beauty World Shopping Centre. Easy enough for amateur trekkers, family outings, a stroll in the park or a jog. There is a main building for meeting area, car drop off and pick up, giant ceiling fans to cool you down, chilled drinking water dispenser, vending machines, maps, loo and a park ranger on duty.  Good place to shelter if it rains There are some slopes on the way up to the cliff top of the old quarry with a stunning viewing Birds Eye area perched right at the top of the cliff. Not a long distance but short slightly steep slopes but totally worth the climb. Saw families with kids around the age of 5 and above at the top so it’s really not that challenging or difficult. Also Not as far as I expected. But do take note of your way back because if you take a wrong turn and end up on Rifle Range Road it’s more than a 1 hour walk to the nearest MRT station especially if you are already tired. However the paths are all well paved and safe. Do note the park closes at 7pm. You can also access this park from the Green Corridor.

Beautiful place for a calm walk. The trail is very well-maintained and the elevated boardwalk makes the whole route super easy, even for beginners. The Colugo Deck is the main highlight amazing views and a great breeze at the top. Saw a few birds and a monitor lizard along the way. Not too crowded in the morning. Really nice spot if you want nature without a tough hike.

The Pasir Panjang Park connects Labrador Nature Reserve and West Coast Park. It is not quite connected yet at the front and end sections. But, once the Wholesale Centre and the Container Port move out, hopefully, it would be nicely connected up.
The Park, mimic the once sandy beach of Pasir Panjang with stretches of sandy vegetation.  Maritime artifacts like ship bollards, anchor, along with a canon, are featured. It also features a Nature Playgarden catering to the children.
Seniors will remember an old canon and the concrete railings at the old Pasir Panjang Park where, at that time, the shoreline was closer to the Park (before reclamation work)
Incidentally, a very small section of the Concrete Railings is still intact, behind the Food Centre. There is also an old steps leading into the water. The steps are at the canal behind the carpark, next to the Food Centre.

This section was opened early sept 2021.
Access to the park can be either through the pasir panjang food centre carpark or opposite PSA Horizon.
Not many people around I guess due to it being new and just a short stretch. There are distinct lanes for human and cyclists, small playareas dotting the linear stretch of the park. The park ends abruptly where the bukom terminal is. Not quite sure if in future it would connect to the adjacent park.
Nothing much here at the moment, perhaps wait till larger sections are completed. I took this path on the way to labrador trails to break the monotony of walking down pasir panjang road.
Will add on more once the other sectors open up and revise the ratings.
Otherwise, it is flat and wheelchair, prak friendly. It is also easily accessible either by mrt (pasir panjang) or car or bus.

Accessibility - 4*
Size - 3*
Things to do - not much
Overall 3*

I’m Ian, a Primary 6 student taught by teacher Monica, and let me tell you, being in Teacher Monica’s class is truly a joyful and fortunate experience. And here is why.

First of all, Teacher Monica always makes learning fun. Even the most boring lessons become enjoyable through her jokes and the way she explains concepts in detail. She is also thoughtful—she gives us short breaks during lessons and even prepares snacks so that we can recharge and concentrate better. On top of regular classes, she goes the extra mile by holding extra sessions, such as her weekly Writing Clinic, to support our learning.

Secondly, Teacher Monica genuinely cares for our well-being. She constantly reminds us to bring water bottles to stay hydrated and to wear our glasses if needed. Once, when my eyes were itchy and I couldn’t stop rubbing them, she noticed right away. She kindly gave me a non-alcohol wet tissue to clean my eyes and gently reminded me not to rub them to avoid infection.

Lastly, as exams draw near, Teacher Monica prepares online lessons to help us consolidate our knowledge and revise effectively.

I am truly grateful for all the effort Teacher Monica has put in—not only to improve my academics but also to guide me towards better habits. I had a great time at Mind Stretcher! Thank you, Teacher Monica, and Happy Teachers’ Day!

This Teachers’ Day, I want to dedicate my appreciation to someone who has truly helped me in my learning journey — Ms Ho, the best English teacher!!

Ms Ho is more than just a teacher, she is just like a mother to me. She is patient when we struggle, firm when we lose focus, and kind when we need encouragement. At first, she looked like the strict kind of teacher just by looking at her scary face (no offense Ms Ho), but I have learnt a lesson. Don't judge a book by its cover. Ms Ho was the kindest teacher! She has taught me that English is not only about grammar and vocabulary but also communicating with confidence. I absolutely appreciate her for teaching Show, Not Tell (SNT). It really helped me with my composition writing!

What makes Ms Ho truly special is the way she believes in her students—even when we doubt ourselves. There were times I felt like giving up, but her words pushed me to keep trying. I became actually determined.. She always reminds us that mistakes are not failures, but opportunities to grow into corrects.

Because of her, I’ve not only improved in English (especially composition and paper 2), but I’ve also learned important values: resilience and perseverance. Every lesson with her is not just about academics — it’s about life. She even makes here classes sometimes hilarious by sharing her funny occurrences to the whole class!

So today, I just want to say: Thank you, Ms Ho, for your dedication, your patience, and your endless belief in me and the whole class. You are a teacher who has made a impact on my life. You are the best! A mentor, a role model and mainly a teacher deserves a huge reward!

Happy Teachers’ Day, Ms Ho! 🎉📚❤️
