Visited Bukit Panjang food court recently. There is a good variety of food choices and prices are reasonable. However, it gets very crowded during peak hours and seating can be hard to find. Waiting time at some stalls was quite long. Overall, decent place for a quick meal if you come at a less busy time.

I simply love the Hawker’s Street concept — it’s incredibly convenient and a real win for residents in this area. IMHO, Hawker’s Street works better for me because it brings together some of the best foodie stalls under one roof, making it easy to satisfy different cravings in one visit.

This time, I tried the following:

1. Wok Hei Hor Fun — ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️½
The Sliced Fish Hor Fun had a strong wok hei, while the sliced fish and choy sim added a nice balance with a comforting, savoury broth and meaty texture.
The Lala Assorted Bee Hoon was smooth and slurpy, topped generously with seafood goodness — lala, prawns, and other seafood that made every bite satisfying.

2. Kaki Makan — ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️½
The Iced Longan had that nostalgic, old-school flavour — refreshing and not overly sweet.
They also serve a solid cup of tea, with just the right milk texture so the tea remains smooth without any bitter aftertaste.

Overall, a very enjoyable visit and definitely a place I’d return to for comfort food cravings.

Hawkers' Street at BPP is a fantastic concept, bringing some of the biggest and most famous hawker names in Singapore to the neighbourhood. I've had the chance to try four of their anchor stalls, and my experience has been consistently good, with one stall being truly exceptional.

The absolute, undisputed star of this food court for me is Flourful Delight (吕记知味面点). This stall is a 5-star gem, hands down. Their Dan Dan Noodles are a revelation, one of the best I've had, with a perfect balance of peanut, chilli, and vinegar. I've returned multiple times, and their Zha Jiang Noodles and Pan-fried Dumplings are also of an exceptionally high standard. This stall alone is worth the trip.

The other famous stalls are all solid 4-star options, though they each have minor inconsistencies that keep them from being perfect:
Tai Wah Pork Noodles: A very solid bowl of BCM. The ingredients were all fresh and well-prepared, from the pork slices and liver to the dumplings. The meatball soup was also hearty and flavourful. The only letdown was that the noodles themselves were a little overcooked and soft for my liking on the day I visited.

Pang's Hakka Yong Tau Foo: A unique, modern take on YTF. The ingredients are fresh, and the soup is clean and comforting (my wife loved the radish). The fried meatballs were also a highlight. However, the base seasoning for the noodles is very light, so you definitely need to add the provided sweet sauce and chilli to get a more robust flavour.

Hill Street Hainanese Curry Rice: The ingredients here are the strong point, especially the braised pork belly, which was excellent. But the main event—the curry sauce—was surprisingly bland and lacked the lemak kick I expected, which was a real letdown for such a famous brand.

Overall, Hawkers' Street is a fantastic addition to BPP and gets a solid 5-stars from me. It's clean, comfortable, and a great place to try a variety of famous foods. While the big-name stalls can be a bit of a mixed bag (generally great ingredients let down by a minor flaw in the base/sauce), the presence of a true 5-star gem like Flourful Delight makes the entire food court a must-visit. I will be back to try the other stalls!

Prices are definitely on the higher side here. It can be quite hard to find seats during peak lunch and dinner hours. The hill street mee pok is fantastic with its harder-than usual mee pok noodles. Portion size pictured here was $9+ but you can get the basic one for $5-6.

The Redhill hor fun is also delicious with silky rice noodles that are slightly different from the norm. There is wok hei too so it was really tasty.

Curry chicken noodles was only alright with a thin gravy base. Highlight was the tender chicken.

Remember that seniors can get 10% off at the drink store. (Kopi o was rather thin.)

Came here for Flourful delights dumplings but there are not here anymore 🥲… so I tried the Hainanese curry pork cutlet rice I saw a lot of people were having. It’s honestly very average, similar to or worse than Encik Tan but with pork. The curry has no taste / spice, the veg also. Pork cutlet is ok, at least the rice is well cooked and served very warm.

Although the taste of noodle was quite acceptable, but the prawn is unexpectedly small for $6.90 bowl of noodle. The soup doesn't have much of umami. At least still have pork lard and fried shallot oil.

£3.50-4 pricing would have been ok. But definitely not worth $6.90. However this whole food court sells food at elevated prices and demand is there.

Prawn noodles is average / acceptable. The soup although tastes ok, seems to get its taste more from soya/black sauce than from proper prawn broth.

Prefer the king of prawn noodles at Hillion mall to this.

I’m quite disappointed with my experience at this stall. I paid an extra $1 for additional noodles, but the portion I received looked exactly the same as a regular serving (see attached photos, not even half of the bowl is filled). I was standing right in front of the stall watching the preparation and did not see any extra noodles being added. I clarified this with two staff members, and both insisted that the additional noodles had already been included. However, from what I observed, there was no visible difference in portion size. If customers are paying extra for a larger portion, there should be a clear and transparent process, for example, weighing the noodles or visibly showing the added portion. Otherwise, it feels unfair to be charged more without receiving what was paid for. Overall, the experience left me feeling disappointed and somewhat cheated.
Food taste normal. Never clear the prawn shit. Overprice. Add soup need to add $1. Honestly quite disappointed.

Food was ok, not bad but definitely not the best nor worth the price. Portion was small and prawns are miserably small and cut into 2. ( already so small yet cut into 2?) At 8.90 is definitely not worth it. Can fine better value and nice prawn mee elsewhere.

My wife and I were here today (24 June 2026) for some coffee and a dessert. It was the first time for the both of us dining here. We ordered the following here today: a Latte, a Cappuccino and we shared a Matcha Strawberry Cake Slice. The hot beverages were very good. The Cake was also very good and "refreshing" due to the perky strawberry flavour and the lightness of the sponge used. Service here was okay but could have been better (I believe) if the crew was not so busy. The dining ambience here was very nice - with comfortable seating and in air-conditioned comfort.
new store. not crowded. ambient environment. many seats with pair seats and grp of 4. ceiling fans making the place cool. same menu. nothing new. only monthly weekday 2 for breakfast menu.

Provide service with patient when I ask if the latte have Caffeine inside and kindly told us that they can have “no coffee” in it and also that there’s a deal for the holiday mug(I love it!) with any holiday drinks, spicy tuna spaghetti suits for those who like spice in tomato also with tuna
I ordered "hot tea by the pot" on the menu l but I was only given a cup of hot tea with the teabag inside. No pot was given. Did I misinterpret the menu?

Like this branch better as ambience and layout is more spacious compared to the one at Lot one mall which is more cramp with chairs after their reno. This place is more cosy and relax. Service wise is ok as mentioned by some, perhaps need to be more smiley than cheerful.

Warm, indulgent food just hits different after 10pm, & sometimes all you want is supper delivered straight to your doorstep, and this HBB in the North serves udons & sandos till 4AM ?!

We had Buddy-San’s Udon ($15.90), which had QQ sanuki udon tossed in miso cream, paired with a generous portion of herb-marinated grilled chicken, seaweed & topped with tobiko. Loved the little bursts of flavour from the tobiko, alongside the sweet-savoury combo of sautéed swiss brown & shimeji mushrooms with caramelised onions. As a cream-based girlie, this was satisfyingly rich & indulgent, though it did get a little jelak towards the end (pair with your veggies!)

Fun-Guy San ($12.90) had a nicely buttered, fluffy shokupan packed with umami fried portobello, sharp cheddar, & caramelised onions. This was sweet-savoury & quite enjoyable !!

If you’re a late-night hustler, pulling an all-nighter, or simply craving something different for dinner, you know where to go ~

Tried 6 different items with my friends and they were all a hit!!
1. The cloud kare udon was so cool and innovative!
2. their original buddy-san udon was super creamy and decedent! the chicken was well marinated and the tobiko adds a nice lil crunch
3. The Ma-popo cold udon was probs my fav out of all the udons cause it had a nice kick of spice and the korean picked radish was super refreshing!!
4. The tamogo san tasted similar to the ones I've had in japan
5. the apple bottom beef was juicy and yums
6. the fun-guy san was my fav sandwich. I loveeee mushrooms and the fried portobello mushroom was juicy n tender and hits the spot!

tldr: overall super yummy food and i highly recommend!

Tried the apple bottom beef sando and buddy san udon, both were delicious and we'll definitely be back to try the other dishes!

the udon sauce was sooo good and it was topped with a very flavourful chicken. the sando had a really great patty and there was plenty of mozzarella stuffed inside (not like a sad slice of cheese haha).

bonus points for opening late, perfect for our late night supper cravings :)) also fyi that it's takeaways only!

As someone who usually doesn’t enjoy egg mayo, the Tamago-san was a pleasant surprise. Their version is well-balanced, flavorful, and far more enjoyable than the typical egg mayo. The Fun-guy-san is another highlight—honestly better than the fried mushroom burger from Shake Shack, with great flavor without feeling too heavy.

The Signature Buddy-san Udon also hits the spot, with well-marinated, tender chicken that’s comforting and satisfying.

Overall, a great experience. Looking forward to trying the rest of their menu!

4th time trying!
As a true bread lover, I really appreciate their choice of Shokupan over standard bread — it gives the sandos such a soft, pillowy texture and elevates the overall flavor beautifully. The portions are also very generous, making each visit feel satisfying and well worth the price.

Will definitely continue to support and am looking forward to trying their new items!
SO GLAD THAT THERE IS FINALLY AN OUTLET IN THE WEST!! Sushi never fail to disappoint. Staff are super friendly as well!

Really good and affordable! My favourite is probably the crispy tuna. Though that said, for some reason two of the pieces didn’t have the crispy bits once, but the others had it? But still super good.

Please add your store to grab mix and match! Would love to order together with other stores!

(Edited for grammar and typos)
lvl 1. halal store. many staff. the sushi are pack inside the box and bring home they are still intact. didn't topple. come with chopstick and sauce.
Nice staff and very nice sushi!!
Staff always super friendly & nice!! Sushi is fresh and yummy as well

Ammar Ibn Nizamudin worked part time at NTUC grocery store, and his service was highly commendable. He consistently demonstrated professionalism, responsibility, and a positive attitude while attending to customers and handling his duties

I often go to NTUC Hillion, and NTUC has always been my go-to supermarket since childhood. However, I had a really shocking experience there recently.

On 1 November 2025, around 10.30am, there was this Chinese lady (I’m quite sure she’s a PR) handling and arranging the fishes. She spoke to customers in a very unfriendly and unhappy tone, and was talking loudly with another staff member, which made me quite uncomfortable.

When I handed her my prawns, she literally threw them onto the weighing machine. While waiting for my fish, she took her own sweet time cleaning, then disappeared for a bit before continuing — all while I was standing there waiting for about 10 minutes (and there were no other customers). Not a single “sorry” or “thank you.” Simply no basic courtesy at all. Literally she is like throwing the fishes and arranging them unhappily.

Honestly, I will never buy fish from NTUC Hillion again if this is the kind of attitude shown. I’ve also noticed that NTUC staff in general seem to be getting ruder lately — it wasn’t like this before. Too many so-called “foreign talents” with unpleasant attitudes. I do understand that everyone has their own personal issues which we may not know about, but when you’re at work, you’ve got to wear a different hat. That’s just part of the job when dealing with customers.

Do take a look at the other reviews too — many are about poor service. Such a shame. I’m an observant customer, and I can see the difference — Sheng Siong staff are definitely friendlier, happier at work, and more proactive. They even promote items sincerely without us asking — and because of that friendliness, we actually end up buying!

Attached is the lady in case you are curious.

Bought two packs of rice in store (by 榮记) S$4.95 + S$3.80.  Packings looked nice, portions normal, some bones 鸡肉和鸭肉很多骨，义烧蛮硬，chicken not tender compared to 榮记own store at 680143,  if compared to the store at 680016.... good to try for this first time only.   A little bit warm,  store did not provide heating when asked. Tried to make hot at home, chilli and sauces provided are good though.  Overall,  can only say, lacklustre.  Hope to see some improvements.   18/09/2023 Mon 1400 Singapore

This NTUC Fairprice is one of two NTUCs in the area - the other being at Bukit Panjang Plaza. However, this one here is 24/7 and is strategically located at an integrated transport hub and at an integrated development with residences above it. So, how could it not do well. While I do shop here fairly often, I am sometimes disappointed by its lack of stock. Otherwise, it is a convenient supermarket to visit.
Just wanna express my appreciation to Rasy for being always friendly, helpful and approachable during my visit to ntuc on last Sunday. Thank you!!
It was a really great and fun experience at the playground. I would most definitely come once more.
Lovely park. Great for kids and walk.
My kids enjoyed playing here….
Very nice playground here.  But currently got lots of mosquitoes 🦟

A beautiful garden with amenities like playground for children, exercise machines for adults & seniors and a small amphitheater for outdoor functions. When the authorities tried a different colour on the fence one night as I was passing through the carpark garden, I quickly took this photo with my phone. Any responses?

Very nice place to either have a slow walk, trail walk, or trail run. There are challenging slopes & steps, but there are also friendly sections to catch your breath. Distance wise, one can choose to walk from a short 3 KM route or a longer 6KM+ route. With the terrain, it is a good place to burn some calories and at the same time, enjoy the greenery with some  sightings of animals in the wild. So far, I have encountered calugo, blue diamond coral snake, pangolin, wild boar, monitor lizards, drongo (bird) and of course, monkeys (plenty of them).

Went hiking at Bukit Timah and Rifle Range and it turned out to be a really refreshing experience. The trail was surrounded by tall trees and thick greenery, making the whole walk feel calm and peaceful. As I went down the wooden steps, I could hear the sounds of nature all around me—birds, insects, and the rustling of leaves.
Along the way, I noticed small details that made the hike even more special, like tiny plants growing out of old wood and patches of moss spreading across fallen logs. It reminded me how alive the forest is, even in the smallest places.
The hike wasn’t too easy, especially with the long paths and steps, but it felt satisfying. I managed to cover about 7.8 kilometers, and even though I was tired, it was a good kind of tired. Overall, it was a simple but meaningful experience—just being surrounded by nature, slowing down, and enjoying the moment.

The highest hill in Sg but there is no view at the summit. A little disappointing. Very hot and humid. The forest does not cover or cool down the trail unlike the one at Macritchi Reservoir. Pavillion are not powered with fans and have no vending machines. Prefer the treetop trail at Macritchi reservoir.

Bukit Timah Nature Reserve is a great spot to switch into hiking mode on the weekend.

There are lots of stairs, so be ready for a workout — definitely a sweat-it-out session 💦
Perfect if you’re looking to move your body and get a good sweat in nature.
Brisk walking during the early morning... able to feel the fresh air... a good place to have a short exercises as well.

​The park's main activities are conveniently clustered around McDonald's, which serves as a great central spot for dining, socializing, and recreation. Aside from active play, it is a fantastic place for gatherings, featuring camping grounds, barbecue pits, large open lawns for picnics, kite flying and jogging.

West Coast Park is a long but relatively quiet park that stretches along Singapore’s western coastline. It’s a wonderful place for a relaxed outdoor day, especially for families with kids

One of the highlights is the large playground, which is one of the better ones in Singapore. The climbing structures, slides, and play areas keep children entertained for a long time, and there’s plenty of open space nearby for running around

The park is also great for cycling and casual walks. You can easily rent bicycles or family pedal carts near the entrance and ride along the park connectors while enjoying the sea breeze

For those who enjoy spending more time outdoors, the park has BBQ pits and camping areas, making it perfect for weekend gatherings with friends and family. There are also scenic spots along the waterfront where people fish or simply relax and watch the ships in the distance

I do like to come to this nature park by the sea - to take in the salty air and the cool sea breeze, both of which relaxes and then invigorates me. I like to hear the waves crashing against the shore and to be among the greenery in this nature park - in particular, the bamboo fringed walkway as regards the latter. The amenities at this nature park is adequate - with enough car parking spaces and public toilets. And overall, this nature park is well-maintained.
Well maintained park and a good place to spend time alone or with family and friends. Suitable for all ages, whether to exercise in the mornings or catch a lovely sunset in the evenings.

West Coast Park is considerably smaller than East Coast Park (ECP), but it remains a highly viable zone for R&R. I have scouted the perimeter and identified several optimal coordinates with good sightlines to hold position and decompress.

Civilian population density in this grid is minimal. The decreased foot traffic results in a secure perimeter and a highly tranquil atmosphere—ideal for lowering your guard and recovering stamina.

Be advised on two minor variables:

First, visuals on the maritime border are compromised and do not match the aesthetics of ECP.

Second, heavy transport vessels are active in the adjacent waters. My MJOLNIR armor’s environmental sensors occasionally detected industrial exhaust drifting into the area. It is non-lethal, but noticeable without a helmet filtration system.

Overall assessment: A solid, quiet sector for downtime. Proceed to coordinates at your discretion.

🚤⛵🛳️This Promenade Lookout Jetty with a sweeping view of the stunning shoreline, often moored with ocean-going ships, small colourful fishing boats, stunning yachts and even Jackup Oil Rigs is a popular spot for fishing enthusiast, as well as a great spot to view the sunset.
🏝️West Coast Park which was built on reclaimed land was experiencing coastal erosion, in the early days.
📥To alleviate the erosion at the shoreline, a granite barrier, along with a picturesque promenade was built.
🎣🛟A Lookout Jetty was also included along with the scenic Promenade.

The Promenade Lookout Jetty at West Coast Park is a hidden gem offering a panoramic coastal view that stretches from the Republic of Singapore Yacht Club to Pasir Panjang Terminal and Jurong Island. It's a perfect spot for watching sunsets, with the serene atmosphere enhancing the experience. Photographers will appreciate the expansive views and peaceful surroundings, making it an ideal location for capturing stunning waterfront scenes. The jetty is rarely crowded, providing a tranquil escape where you can relax and take in the beauty of the coast.

Good:

1. A small quiet pier at West Coast Park.
2. Good view of Singapore Port.
3. Golden sunsets on non cloudy days..
4. Police cctv camera give a sense of security.
5. Police patrol duty every  night is very reassuring.
6. Great opportunity for photo ops and drone videography.

Bad:

1. Not a scenic beach. The view in the front is spoiled by cranes and ships.
2. Jetty is very short. Doesn't take you far into the sea.
3. Not ample shelter around. Need to run in case of sudden showers / thunder storms.
4. Do not go down into the water unless an expert swimmer. It is deceptively deep esp during high tide.
5. Lower rocks are exposed during low tide. Don't go down as these can be as slippery as ice.

It is a small spot inside the west coast park, where you can sit on a bench and look the harbour. You nearly have the same view as along the normal border path (along the harbour and on the side of the playground area). In the first picture: how you reach the spot. In the second one: the view
My first time to see super boat on the port. The area fits for circling and running, pinic also a good choice
The team here is very supportive and our child looks forward to school!

Started my journey of reconciliation with my life as I progressed with Forest School Singapore.

8 years in at this point of writing this review, enjoyed every second as part of this community.

If you are wondering you are late to this, you are not, Darren and Gen are always available to reach out to.

Dairy Farm serves as the HQ for FSS and was the first forest where I was given the opportunity to coach. The Forest School Practitioner programme offered me the time and space to renew my own relationship with nature and myself, and gave me the encouragement to develop a FS-inspired programme for children from under-resourced families in the Yishun community. I highly recommend every parent and educator (or adult who shares time and physical space with children) to get to know FSS and their various offerings for the public to learn and expand their perspectives. :)

First forest school in Singapore! Definitely a place that’s thoughtfully run with clear intention behind the programme. What I’ve learned from my experiences here have continued to shape me long after. Would recommend to parents and educators looking to learn more!

When Coach Darren launched the FSPLA I was so excited and knew that I had to sign up! Beyond the practical skills and knowledge imparted to us, I’m so grateful for Coach Darren’s empowering mentorship and the supportive community I am now a part of.

My journey as a  Forest School journey has been filled with meaningful moments. Along with the children, I’ve learned that slowing down, getting messy, and sometimes doing nothing at all is where the real magic happens.”

review_text: melvin has made biology so much more interesting and less daunting by breaking down content down into bite sized information that is easier to remember. his classes are always so full of energy and helped me to stay focused even after a long day at school. i really enjoyed my two years in his class, 10/10 would recommend
