place_name: Zenith Education Studio - Bukit Panjang | JC Tuition, Secondary Tuition & Pri | Math, Chem, Econs, Physics, GP, English
review_text: Mr Keith is the best A math tutor I have found after trying 3-4 different centres. He is always enthusiastic in class and makes lame jokes to make the class entertaining. I really like his structured teaching style which helped me to catch up with math concepts fast even though I was previously behind in school.

place_name: Zenith Education Studio - Bukit Panjang | JC Tuition, Secondary Tuition & Pri | Math, Chem, Econs, Physics, GP, English
review_text: teacher keith is the best amath tutor ever! i could understand topics and concepts i was struggling for months after a few lessons with him, and i improved so much. he is patient and makes the class engaging for all of us. 10/10 tutor!

I came into the Initiative Learning Hub in 2023 for Pure Physics Tuition as I was not able to understand the concepts of the Physics topics well. Mr Chong provided detailed worksheets and understandable explanations of the topics in physics and I managed to understand topics I could not grasp in school. My grades improved significantly from borderline passes to an A2 in my Preliminary Examinations. Thank you Mr Chong for teaching me and assisting me in scoring well in physics.

I came to The Initiative Learning Hub when I was in sec 1 around 2017. At that time my science grades were close to failing and I have struggled to grasp the concepts at school. But thru the 4 years of teaching and guiding of me till O lvls, I have managed to get an A for my science, something that I didnt imagine. Mr John Yeow Nicholas, or commonly called Uncle John by us students, is one of the nicest and most patient teachers I have met and I'm was glad to have been taught by him. He has a funny sense of humour and would crack us up with jokes at times. We also love how easy and simple his concepts are and that we can understand them immediately. Lastly, its his determination and motivation that helps encourages us students to persevere on. He has many different subjects to offer from primary to JC and from Science to English. Highly recommended for all students that are finding a good tutor!!! The best part is...the tution fees are among the cheapest in the neighbourhood and in the west region!!! Thank you Uncle John for the 4 years of teaching and guiding me! 👍👍👍😇😇😇
If you want quality tuition, take The Initiative
Not so crowd... Taxi pick point also nearby the exit
Friendly and helpful staff

Disappointed with the air-flow, wind abit stale, not ventilating well since all the ceiling fans were operated during evening time. Cant feel much wind.

Hawker is spacious and clean, but services from specific stalls are confusing.
Firstly drink stall, my friend able to make his payment through paywave at the counter, but when my turn to patronize, a staff told me "no paywave", only cash, nets, paynow and fairprice app. So in the end, made the payment thru Fairprice App.

When come about to patronise another food stall, since the place is under "Kopitiam" company, since all stalls have their barcode scanners, so I try to use the fairprice app for payment but was told cant use the app to pay. Only cash or QR code payment.

Is very confusing that one can use card payment, awhile later cant and one can use app payment, another one cant.

This place alone, eaten off the stars rating.

Quite a few stalls not operating today.
Looking forward trying the duck rice stall another day since is closed.

Spacious and well ventilated hawker centre with a wide variety of food. Sheltered car park is located conveniently below the hawker centre. There is also a community book exchange corner. Clean and big toilets are a bonus.
Ying Jia Steamed Fish - fresh (not frozen) fish & blanched leafy veg. They prepare on the spot. Bonus is it's v affordable! Unusual in HDB hawker centre. Wifey loves it!

This is long overdue that I should have done but they say it’s better than late than never😁 Anyway I just want to share my experience on dining here most on dinner time. I love the variety of food you can chooses and the place is very huge and clean. What I love the most is the beverage section the quality of my tea peng never change🧋also I very lucky that everytime I come here to eat I see Ms Irene and Dave I like them because they are very friendly no matter how busy the evening time😍 Salute to them for being good on what they doing 👌🏽

Old School Fishballs Kway Teow Mee Soup. Teo Chew Cuisine. Cockles Yong Tau Foo Laksa. Comfort Soupy Food. Nice And Delicious. Will Come Back For More. Always Long Queue . Food Standard Are Good. Prices Are Reasonable.

Enjoyed this home-based omakase concept experience. The white coffee and special latte were delicious. Had a special scone made from scratch. Even the dips are healthy and tasty! Owner is absolutely passionate and knows his stuff. Very friendly and gives attention to details. Do try if you are in the area!

Super cool omakase snack and coffeebar
Spend time with friends and family
Awesome coffee tea and snacks!
Great recommendation for ppl staying at Bukit Panjang

Fantastic experience at Keffie Slowdown Coffeebar!

JJ is incredibly knowledgeable and passionate about his craft. He shares valuable insights on great coffee, delicious food, and how to enjoy them in a healthy and balanced way.

The atmosphere is warm and welcoming, making the whole experience even more enjoyable. Highly recommended for anyone who appreciates quality coffee and meaningful conversations.

Definitely worth a visit! 😃☕️
Coffee bar with a nice intimate sitting space. Drinks and bites done well, many items done from scratch and thoughtfully healthy too! Love his yogurt!

Very chill place to slow down, everything here is homemade. From the yogurt, to the bread, to the jam and granola. The chef is very knowledgeable and passionate chat about eating well for our gut, brewing coffee, etc.

I never knew that even water and the amount and types of minerals makes a difference in how our coffee tastes!

Went there specifically to try the breakfast that they offer.

Finding APS is easy (right outside Cashew MRT!), but once at APS main gate, got lost a few times with security trying to guide me until I realise what to do (from main entrance go straight to end and climb stairs).

Despite arriving 10~ minutes before opening time, they are ready to serve me.

Waitstaff sometimes complained about my coffee habits (I drank 3 cups), I think they are concerned I go hyper caffeinated XD but otherwise I understand their concerns.

I got Chicken Porridge ($9) with Coffee (I chose this) or Tea, and Carrot Cake (I chose this) or Brownies ($2). Porridge bowl comes with peanuts, vegetables (the chicken floss was swapped with vegetables, eventually they declare chicken porridge out of stock from what I heard from waitstaff). Even comes with side dish of Youtiao (fried dough), which I ate before my porridge came.

Bowl was VERY huge (see photos), so for $9 this is great value compared to some restaurants where they give one small dollop of some ingredient and charge $20+.

Nice view, but very empty (one other person came but otherwise from 8:30 to 9:30 just only me), apparently as of posting this is last day before restaurant takes break for school holiday.

OK, at least I know now how to make waitstaff life easier XD!

PS: My reservation was 8:30AM. Waitstaff did not seem to check reservation. Maybe they know I am only customer somehow!

The restaurant is clean and has a good vibe. I ordered a set lunch for $16, which comes with soup of the day, main course, dessert and drink (coffee/tea). Service was decent, but don’t expect too much as they are students/trainees. So please be patient. The waiter missed out on my water, and also accidentally charged my friend for set lunch when he only order ala carte. Food wise, it’s good and healthy and of decent portion. Nonetheless, it’s an overall pleasant experience, and I’ll come again!

26 Jan 26
Just sharing my personal opinion — everyone has different taste preferences.

I’ve visited Assumption Restaurant for Training (ART) a few times. What makes ART meaningful is that it provides students with real-world F&B and service training. Dining there feels purposeful, knowing that you are supporting their learning and development.

On my recent visit, I ordered the fish and chips. The portion was generous and reasonably priced. The batter was crisp without being overly greasy, and the fish was fresh and moist. Overall, the dish was above average, especially considering the value for money.

I would also like to highlight their pastries, which are one of the main reasons I return. The pandan cake was moist, light, and fragrant — not overly sweet, and well-balanced in flavour. For the quality offered, the pricing is very fair.
Service may occasionally reflect that the staff are trainees, but they are generally polite and sincere, which makes the dining experience pleasant.

Overall, ART offers good food at reasonable prices, while supporting a meaningful training initiative. I would recommend giving it a try, especially their pastries.

I had a very good experience here. The students took really good care of my group and I left feeling well fed. I ordered the baked herb chicken with black pepper sauce ($11) and as a set meal ($5) which included a soup, a slice of focaccia bread, dessert and hot tea with a cookie! It was an affordable, well balanced and tasty meal.

The restaurant is bright, spacious, clean and welcoming. I see this as a wonderful initiative that empowers students, keep it up!

This monthly buffet is a fantastic way to enjoy authentic traditional flavors. The porridge is comforting, and the variety of side dishes is impressive—especially the tender braised duck and flavorful prawn paste chicken. It’s not just about the food; the event supports student training and community, making every visit meaningful. Highly recommend booking early as it fills up fast.

A fantastic hidden gem with faultless service!

We stumbled across FlagWhite tucked away in a corner during our morning walk, and what a great find it turned out to be! The food was absolutely delicious. We stopped in for brunch and really appreciated the options to customize our plates, a very smart touch. The coffee was just as delicious and exactly what we needed to start the day.

The whole experience was incredibly smooth, thanks to their easy-to-use ordering app. But what truly stood out was the staff. The service was amazing, full of smiles, and absolutely faultless. Overall, a fantastic experience. We will definitely be back to try their dinner menu and check out the tap beers!

Chicken Parmigiana with tater tots was excellent. The chicken was juicy and not dry at all. This is my second time here, and it didn’t disappoint. I had the schnitzel on my previous visit, which was just as good.

It’s definitely a great spot to hang out and work from. I also love their coffee, and they get pretty creative with their drink menu too. I tried the orange coffee and would order it everytime.

I always get too full before I could even try their dessert. But it always looks so good when other customers have them.

Called in here after going past on the bus and thinking it looked like a lovely spot. I was not wrong! It was super busy but the girl serving was so kind, I initally ordered just a takeaway coffee but a seat became avaliable while I was waiting. Im so glad it did because I ended up ordering brunch. The choices were fantastic, fresh and cooked quickly.
The staff were genuinely lovely, I would 100% come back if I was ever in the area again.

i had a pleasant time at flagwhite, this being my second visit, i got to try their food this time

There are plugs avaliable at the counter seats for the WFH /study!

the iced white is good and strong as usual, $7 for 5oz. The fish and chips came as a large portion, i almost didnt finish it. Originally, I wanted to try the chicken parm, but it wasnt avaliable at 11am( though it appeared on the app later)

They have a lunch set meal from 12pm onwards, applicable on selected mains. You'll get a free drink!

This cafe is definitely on the pricier side, so I was grateful that there was no GST or SC

The mains were a major letdown; both Pork Schnitzel and Panko Breaded Fish & Chips were significantly over fried and had that distinct "old oil" smell and taste 🤢🤮 the only highlight was definitely the Black Sesame Seed tart from Keong Saik Bakery; thick nutty vibes with the QQ mochi and the peanut cream

Hi there, 7-11 is currently running a promotion where you can get $2 off any food item when paying with DBS PayLah. The discount applies to any onigiri flavor, not just tuna flavour. I have also bought other variety flavours at different 7-11 stores and the discount was applicable.

However, when I visited this particular outlet (hillion mall) just now at 7pm, the cashier lady named Connie, insisted that the promotion only applies to the tuna flavor even after i told her is for all flavours. This was very frustrating and a waste of my time after queueing.

Please ensure your staff are properly trained and updated and not arguing with the customers with false information as there are so many people queueing right behind.

This 7-Eleven store in Hillion Mall that faces out therefrom, looking on to Upper Bukit Timah Road, is I think as big as the 7-Eleven store in the depths of the, in my opinion, very nearby Bukit Panjang MRT Station, which in the case of the latter, sees a lot more footfall than the former. Nevertheless, this 7-Eleven store also has a reasonably wide variety of products and it is also sufficiently well stocked. Service here at this 7-Eleven store is quite okay, and the shopping ambience therein is nice enough.

had such a pleasant experience with the young lady cashier. she was sooo friendly, meticulous, and even took the time to make small talk with customers. hope she gets recognised for her service & efforts! :)
Location was hard to find. Try McDonald in Hillion Mall first. Then look for this from outside Hillion Mall.

I felt that the staff dishonest. I brought four wines and i remember there were bags for me and the malay woman told me no more and if i need to pay for the carrier. I will not buy anymore things from that outlet. Owner please beware of your employees

🤹Bt Batok Nature Park Playground is a sand-filled Children's Playground meant for children age 2 to 5 yrs.
🛝There are age appropriate Slides, Dome Climbers, Stepping Stones and Spinners.
🪣For toddlers, they can sandplay.
Kids will have an enjoyable time around the Playground.
🚻The Park's Public Toilet is just nearby, if need cleaning up.
🧙The Nature Park is wheelchair friendly, so all 3G may enjoy family bonding time together at the Playground.

Nice playground for the kids to play and run around. A stationary fitness area nearby to do exercises. Been there for many years, people are nice and friendly. Just don't hog the station too long so other users can make use of it. Please keep the area clean.
Wonderful place to spend time with Jesus. Quiet place for Prayer and meditation. Great place for exercises.
Establishing a park of this nature within the community zone constitutes an excellent strategy.
A good variety of equipment and space for kids (and bigger kids!) to use and enjoy.

Hindhede Nature Park is one of Singapore’s hidden gems for anyone looking to enjoy nature without committing to a long or strenuous hike. Despite its relatively small size, the park packs in an impressive variety of rainforest scenery, peaceful walking trails, and one of the most rewarding viewpoints in the country

The trails are well-maintained and easy to follow, making the park suitable for families, seniors, and casual walkers. Along the way, you’ll find lush tropical vegetation, towering trees, interesting plant life, and plenty of shaded paths that make for a relaxing stroll even on warmer days. The shelters and lookout points are thoughtfully placed, offering great spots to rest and soak in the surroundings

The highlight is undoubtedly the former quarry lookout. The emerald-green water framed by dramatic rock cliffs creates a surprisingly scenic landscape that feels far removed from the city

It seems this is my favourite spot to hike and train myself for my next climb. The trail is like super easy so I do many repetitions. The place is nice. Plenty of wild snakes and monkeys to look for. If you are lucky, juvenile king cobras too.
Nice and peaceful park with an easy walking trail. Great for a short stroll, and the quarry view is really nice.
This is an easy trail and accessible via either from car park or Bukit Timah visitor center. That’s a playground for children age 7-12 also. No admission ticket required.

Such a pretty park ! Super near Bukit Timah Summit so it can be a little outing. Went around noon and I was almost fried by the sun. Lowkey got lost but it was relatively easy to find my way to the Quarry ! Recommend to visit in the late afternoon to avoid the sun hehe. Not sure why they say you need to buy tickets but it’s free !!

Dear Ms Pave, Teacher Sherryl and Ma Laoshi, many thanks for everything you do. We would like to express our appreciation for your dedication in fostering confidence and nurturing our kid in her learning journey with LFP (Segar). Many thanks for taking good care of our kid so that we have a peace of mind at work. Good job and keep it up! 幸苦了!

We moved here recently from Canada and found this school for our little one.
This school is the best thing that happened to us since we moved here.
All teachers are very empathetic to kids and loving. Principal is very much involved in each and every kid. Amazing staff here. My kid is learning so much including Mandarin.
My kid got one fav teacher already, enjoy school here so much.
10 on 10 school. Thank you all teachers and staff for helping our kid. Keep it up.

We appreciate the teachers take good care and show concerns to our girl, and teach a lot of stuffs to her which benefit to her up growing, plus the teachers are very responsive to any matter which required their attention and clarification.

The group of teachers in Segar Team are very joyful and kind which our girl enjoy going to school everyday.

We do notice the teachers are willing to give extra mile for the children to make them feel comfortable and enjoy their daily schooling too.

Thank you for everything!

Happy Teacher's Day! ❤️

We would like to express our genuine gratitude and appreciation to all staffs in LFP BP 546 in particular to Teacher Anis, Ma Lao Shi and Lu Lao Shi. Thank you for everything you have done. Thank you for making learning fun and interesting so that my girl enjoys going to school daily and shares interesting stuffs to us after school. She enjoys all the lessons, enrichment classes, art and craft, school activities and field trip. You have inspired her to be more engaging, more vocal and more independent. You truly care about students and have gone above and beyond to help students learn and grow. Thanks you very much!! Much appreciated it.

Little Foot Prints (Segar Road) provides excellent infant care with dedicated teachers, a safe environment, and engaging activities. The staff is warm and attentive, ensuring personalized care for each baby. Daily updates give us peace of mind, and we’ve seen great progress in our baby’s development. Kudos to Teacher Xiu Jing, Lily and Yvonne!

place_name: Raffles Kidz @ Bukit Panjang – Best Childcare Centre And Preschool
review_text:
Saying goodbye to this preschool is harder than expected. It has been such a special place for my son and our family. The warmth, patience, and dedication of the teachers made all the difference, and we will forever be grateful for the strong foundation they helped build.

To teacher su, wang Lao shi and principal Nicole, Thank you for the memories, the growth, and the love. We will miss you!

place_name: Raffles Kidz @ Bukit Panjang – Best Childcare Centre And Preschool
review_text: My daughter has been studying here for almost two years. The school has a well-organized management system and communicates news effectively. The learning is play-based, which makes it fun and encourages children to want to learn more. There are also a variety of activities, and the environment feels homey, so children don’t feel like school is a scary place. Even teachers who aren’t their class teachers know the children well. The principal is also friendly and familiar with both the students and parents, which gives us confidence that we can easily consult or ask questions when needed.

place_name: Raffles Kidz @ Bukit Panjang – Best Childcare Centre And Preschool
review_text:
We’ve seen wonderful progress and development in our child, thanks to the nurturing environment created by a team of dedicated, high-quality teachers.

From being highly sensitive and introverted to becoming a cheeky and expressive child, we’ve witnessed a remarkable transformation in both his behaviour at school and at home. The teachers have been incredibly patient, gently encouraging him in areas where he needs support—even offering to help with potty training during school hours.

The regular updates on his well-being and daily activities, complete with thoughtful commentary and beautifully captured photos, have been very reassuring. Any special requests from teachers or parents are handled promptly and efficiently. I was especially impressed by the recent Parent-Teacher Meeting with Teacher Aliyah and Wu Lao Shi. They took the time to walk us through our child’s progress in detail and shared practical tips we could apply at home. It was clear that they take these meetings seriously and are genuinely passionate about each child’s growth.

Principal Nicole is always approachable and willing to chat about parenting tips. She’s also quick to follow up and investigate whenever we raise concerns, such as when we notice minor injuries on our child.

One area for improvement would be for RKI management to provide greater transparency around event pricing. Making these events more affordable would allow more children to participate and enjoy the experience with their peers—without putting a strain on families.

place_name: Raffles Kidz @ Bukit Panjang – Best Childcare Centre And Preschool
review_text: My little one looks forward to school every day because the curriculum is fun and engaging. Although he had difficulty adjusting initially, Principal Nicole and her teachers took the time to connect with him, making him feel safe and welcomed. Their warmth and patience helped him settle. Principal Nicole, in particular, is incredibly nurturing and loving towards the children, creating a supportive and caring environment.

place_name: Raffles Kidz @ Bukit Panjang – Best Childcare Centre And Preschool
review_text: It is a preschool hidden in a dead-end lane second to the main Choa Chu Kang Road. The outdoor playground is great! I wasn’t very comfortable how playgroup kids are together with nursery toddlers, and that the lunch area is at semi outdoor space, which is very hot at noon time. The high ceiling of the first floor of the landed house is a plus though. The teachers here also seem rather patient. The place is kept nicely and tidely as well. The tuition is around $1100 before subsidy, which is affordable, compared to schools such as mindchamp.

Previously, my scheduled appointments were at Choa Chu Kang Polyclinic. Ever since Bukit Panjang Polyclinic opened, all my appointments have been transferred here. I like this polyclinic’s layout and atmosphere — it’s clean, well-ventilated, and with good air conditioning.

When it first opened in 2021, the staff were neither helpful nor courteous, but they have improved a lot since. Now there are friendly, smiling, polite staff who greet patients with a ready-to-serve attitude. The young doctors deliver quality service: they are polite, listen attentively, and provide detailed explanations. The pharmacists are equally commendable — fast and efficient.

I applaud this polyclinic for its excellent service and organized processes that make the queues smooth and efficient.

I went to see Dr Alif Asyraf (Room 316) because I was having blood in my stool and was quite anxious about it.

He handled my case with a lot of care and professionalism, and took time to explain everything clearly in detail. I really appreciated how calm and reassuring he was throughout the consultation.

He made me feel comfortable discussing something quite sensitive, and didn’t rush the consultation at all. By far one of the best doctors I’ve met; very attentive, patient, and thorough in his explanation.

I left the clinic feeling much more reassured and less worried. Highly recommended.

Their dental department is excellent. Most of the doctors are very patient and take the time to explain everything in detail. I first visited them with a serious dental issue, and they referred me to a specialist until my treatment was fully completed. Now, I no longer need to see a specialist. I will continue to return here regularly every six months for routine cleaning, scaling, and check-ups.

I went there to make an appointment for medical check-ups and was seamlessly guided to the clinics counter. There, the receptionist created an appointment for me, and within seconds, it was all over. The services provided were professionally done. I revisited for a Bivalent COVID-19 jab and was asked to go up to the 5th floor. To my surprise this side is very clean & spacious. The service provided was fantastic & efficient. But today my COVID-19 was done on the 4th floor & the service nurse was pleasant looking. She chatted with me to calm myself and explained everything I needed to know before giving me a jab. Also on the 5th level there's a Dental Services for Scaling, Polishing and Filling. Dr Wu did a fantastic job of filling both of my teeth.

Don’t bother to come here if you can’t secure an appointment at 10pm the night before. Walked in before 9am for severe diarrhea, and waited till 10.45am without being called. Upon asking, the receptionist confirmed that I might only be called as late as at 2pm.

place_name: Coffeeshop (Kimly Seafood) at Blk 163A Gangsa Road, Bukit Panjang
review_text: There are many nice food stalls here but don’t order coffee or tea get can drinks instead. Their coffee tea are all pre-made and sold to you the moment you order.. come on! we are not in a rush for a freshly made coffee or tea. I order less sugar they don’t care and give a regular one. Thanks it’s fast but too sweet T.T

place_name: Coffeeshop (Kimly Seafood) at Blk 163A Gangsa Road, Bukit Panjang
review_text:
Here’s a professional but honest way you can comment on a bad experience at Kimly Coffee’s dim sum store:

I've given Kimly Coffee's dim sum store more than one chance, but unfortunately, the experience has been consistently disappointing. It's not just about the food — the service really leaves a lot to be desired. The staff served the dim sum without a word, no greeting, no response, and with an unfriendly expression that made me feel unwelcome. This isn’t the first time it’s happened, which makes it even more disappointing. I hope the management takes customer service more seriously — it’s just as important as the food.

place_name: Coffeeshop (Kimly Seafood) at Blk 163A Gangsa Road, Bukit Panjang
review_text:
Coffee is extremely bad, worst in my life , drink stall staff says it’s “fresh”.

Better not order coffee from here, other kimly coffeeshops all the same.

place_name: Coffeeshop (Kimly Seafood) at Blk 163A Gangsa Road, Bukit Panjang
review_text: Coffee stall aunties have extremely bad attitude. Ordered takeaway hot coffee in cup. Aunties did not tell me no takeaway cups available. I asked a couple more times when she was proceeding to pour my drink in a plastic bag n she then shouted impatiently NO CUP!!! They could have told me nicely when i ordered or when i asked.

place_name: Coffeeshop (Kimly Seafood) at Blk 163A Gangsa Road, Bukit Panjang
review_text: One of the best coffee shops in Bukit panjang with a variety of good tasting dishes. The Koka noodles is famous and people come here in the wee hours for it. Other stalls are great too such as the chicken rice and western food
Easy and windy area .clean,nice view having to see the monsoon drain but clear clean water..Good variety of local food  ...
Tried the Tze char stall, One Ocean. The dinner was great and pretty affordable
Not bad with reasonable pricing

Since not willing to accept CDC Voucher, then why paste the CDC voucher sticker at the 1st place! Rude supervisor Michelle
I've never seen coffeemaker taste customers coffee or tea before handing over to customer only this coffeeshop Ivy! Disgusting staff!
Rating is solely for the economical rice. Extremely overpriced and super little portion.  Do not visit.

A Hot Hideout @ Junction 10 has truly become my little “secret hideout” whenever I need some quiet me-time, especially during off-peak hours. Their Mala Stir Fry is my go-to—flavourful, balanced, and consistently satisfying. I appreciate how they tailor the spice levels to suit local palates—not overly spicy or numb, but still packed with that familiar mala kick. For those who like it hotter, there’s always Level 3 and the option to add extra numbness.

My wife prefers the Tomato soup base, which is rich, comforting, and just as enjoyable in its own way. While many go for the popular fried potatoes and lotus roots, I personally enjoy sticking with the regular ingredients—it’s all about how well they prepare and season everything.

Great food, comfortable setting, and customisable flavours—this place is a gem, and definitely deserving of 5 stars!

The atmosphere, food and service as expected were really good!!

Went here with my friend and bought the 小辣 mala soup and it was so delicious, wasn’t too spicy it was just nice totally recommend!! Especially love that they fry your Mantou and potatoes!! The thai milk tea also was made just right wasn’t overly sweet.

Overall had a great time here at Hot Hide Out J10, totally recommend!!

Looks like the report card of a hardworking student.

I have been eyeing this eatery since hearing about them from my ex students in NTU. Now that they have opened at J10, it doesn't necessitate a trip to visit my alma mater.

So here I am supporting my fellow Singaporean and having one of my least favourite food. I know, oxymoron right? But A Hot Hideout will probably be one I'll eat again, once in a while lah. Not every time my wallet can tahan the prices here.

The selection of vegetables and meat is reasonable and the kiosk to settle the order was intuitive. I got jio'ed by my cousin and followed the tips on how the food is prepared.

I'm just eyeing the scrambled eggs, and the potato crisp that kept appearing on my feed years ago.

Peanuts, coriander 💚, and spring onion is complimentary. Collect secret powder or sprinkle over your deep fried ingredients.

Great place for mala dinner! The lotus root chips is my absolute favourite.

Price is reasonable and it is flat rate regardless of the item you pick.

Fried items are placed in a separate bowl so it remains crispy.

Paying can be a little daunting for first timers, luckily staff is watchful and ready to help. Also better is that there is 2 counters for payment so I don't feel so stressed holding the line up.

Queue system for entering and seating is orderly, though queue can get really long.

Overall it tastes great and highly recommended!

Regular customer here, I eat HHO quite often (about 1–2 times a week). I figured it’s worth sharing some feedback, especially since management seems responsive to reviews 👍🏻

I usually order xiao la, but there have been a few occasions where the spice level was clearly much higher (see picture), and it was quite difficult to finish. There were also instances where ingredients we didn’t order ended up in our dish, and it took some time to remove them.

It would really help if there were better controls/checks in place for spice levels and order accuracy.

Yesterday was my first disappointing experience (around 11pm, takeaway). I noticed my order was ready and sitting at the counter for about 5–10 minutes, but no staff attended to it. I tried to get a staff’s attention, but the staff member seemed occupied and then went on to pack a delivery order instead of clearing the ready orders (there were about three at that point). As a result, the fried potato became a bit cold and soggy.

That said, I’m still a big fan of the food and will continue supporting, as long as the service and quality remain consistent. Wishing Ray Sheng a smooth and speedy recovery as well 🙏

Wow, I found a café with a really unique view! 🚛

It’s located on Level 3, overlooking lots of lorries and truck tracks, which makes it quite an interesting place to chill.

The food menu isn’t very extensive, but the pizza and pasta were both delicious! The pasta looked simple, but the tomato sauce was the star it tasted like a rich, homemade tomato sauce. The pizza was so good too, definitely worth ordering.

I originally wanted to try the Coconut Cloud Coffee, but my friend accidentally ordered the wrong drink. Surprisingly, it turned out to be really nice! It wasn’t overly sweet, which I actually enjoyed.

Overall, it’s a cozy café with a unique view and tasty food. I’ll definitely be back again!

Booked an appointment via Quandoo. It was an afternoon 2.30pm on a Sunday. It was not fully packed. I managed to get a seat at the window seat where I get to enjoy the view of the highways. But it can be quite hot on a Sunny day.

I ordered
Diavola SGD 18.00
Iced Mocha Low Fat SGD 7.50

Pizza was served hot and crispy crust. I quite loved it and would love to try the other flavours the next time.

Total SGD 25.50

Overall great experience here, being able to sit and watch the highway and cars pass by is indeed a unique experience. Service staff were all really polite as well.

Enjoyed the pizza, pasta was okay but the truffle fries were a disappointment. Sauce for the fries had not enough truffle flavor, might also be better to serve it as a dip on the side instead.

Really chill atmosphere when there is no crowd but it can be really overwhelmingly loud during peak hours due to the acoustics of the place.

Went there on Friday afternoon at 1.45pm and cafe was super crowded with limited seating left. Balcony seats overlooking the highway is super limited (8/9 seats) and mostly occupied by students studying. Would prefer if studying was prohibited during the peak hours since they only open until 5pm. Noise level was also overwhelming with chatter due to a large group upstairs and it being full house, was barely able to hear my partner speak half the time.

Food wise, pizza was super yummy and 4 slices (ard 6 inch size) just nice for sharing between 2 person with 1 other main.

Truffle mushroom pasta was nicely done, but I would prefer the truffle taste to be more prominent as it tasted more like mushroom pasta than truffle mushroom.

Truffle fries used truffle sauce and not truffle oil, hence truffle taste not as prominent and tasted more like truffle mayo instead. I would assume the truffle pasta and this truffle sauce came from the same sauce base.

The pistachio coffee was very aromatic  with hints of pistachio taste, pistachio bits was a nice topping as well. Lavender orange fizz didn't taste like typical orange syrup and had a very nice orange scent to the drink and very refreshing.

Tiramisu was unfortunately sold out by the time we reached, recommended to come by 11am for full menu availability.

Minimal service and an expressway view, but the food speaks for itself. The black coffee hit the spot, and the truffle mushroom linguine with Japanese egg yolk was delicious. Finished off with a great tiramisu, though portions are definitely on the smaller side for the price. Worth a visit if you are tracking down good pasta.

I went to this outlet looking for whip cream but they were out of stock. Staff name Jasper came for the rescue called up Fajar outlet to reserve one for me. Kudos to him for having such excellent service.

Popular supermarket with many outlets.
Well received by the general public for its low pricing.
The service standard for Seng Siong Supermarket has been known not to be high. So do expect only the very basic service standard.
Ample variety of food and household items available. This outlet is also selling fresh produce, such as chilled chicken and seafood.
There is a car park nearby too.
And within walk-in distance to the LRT. There is also a shelter walkway linked to the multiple storey carpark.

I frequently go to this outlet as it’s right opposite my home. All the staff are very friendly and sweet! Whenever I’m at the counter paying for my items, they always tempt me with their promotional offers — and I always end up buying because they’re just too nice! Hahaha. Keep up the good work — I really love your service! 💖
Nice place for shopping .. you will get a lot of daily used items at a cheaper price ...

Very friendly staff and is well stocked with fresh veggies. I usually order my veggies from Open Taste, but this outlet's fresh vegetables are more value for money at the same freshness. It's a great neighbourhood supermarket 👍🏻

Collected a few parcel there, seems like she doesnt want to handle shoppee customers, lousy attitude, dont need her to smile and greet, aleast acknowledge a human standing infront of your counter.
send her for customer service course fast.

To the Owner reading this,
Your Shop is fine, reasonable pricing, convenient to buy daily items. But your cashier is killing your business, collecting parcel also bring foot fall to your shop too.

When her work attitude and behaviour improves, so will your ratings.

Hope  to see cashier improvement soon.

Very very bad service by the cashier, it’s a Vietnamese. First and foremost I’ve got no issues with foreigners, but I’m really fed up with her service. Bought a total of 13 coca colas and asked her if there was any bulk discounts, but she got fed-up instead and rejected me rudely, and I just asked for the whole price and she was fed up and kept sighing here and there like we’re like cheapskate customers. It’s only just asking about the price and I’m a paying customer here. Could have tell me politely. What’s wrong with her? Please take a good look at the photos

Horrible service. When I got my items and approached the cashier, she didn't acknowledge me! She actually continued fixing her parcels for a good few mins while I was just standing there🤯 should i have just left? Would that be doing her a favour? Will not be back even tho I live just opp. Also, her parcels were all over the counter, customers can't even put their purchases down. Joke.

Looks like they're too busy with their parcel collection side biz, to scan ur items for payment🤡

Quite a big supermarket located at a new public housing project. The supermarket is located right below the multi storey carpark, facing the main road. The supermarket was pretty well stocked up when I was there. Reasonable pricing.

If you’ve ever wondered what it’s like to be treated like an inconvenience for daring to be a customer, look no further than this charming little convenience stall near my place. Based on several recent reviews, I see I'm not alone in experiencing the warm hospitality of their cashier – a Malaysian aunty with bleached blonde hair tied up in a ponytail and a permanent expression that screams, “You owe me rent.”

She greets every paying customer like it’s the worst part of her day (and possibly her life), and I've personally witnessed her tossing out curt, borderline rude responses to people just trying to… you know, pay for things. One of my neighbors – a poor soul – even shared that when he politely suggested she be a little more courteous, she shouted back at him. Now that’s customer engagement, isn't it?

The best part? There’s little to no info available about who runs this place, so if you thought you might want to raise a complaint or even just leave feedback – good luck! But hey, who needs accountability when you’ve got that level of charm behind the counter?

Prices aren’t particularly great either, but if you’re in the West Coast Park area and feel like ruining a perfectly good day with a side of attitude and a splash of rudeness, this place is an absolute must-visit!

This is the main building of University Sports Centre, also known as USC. Located opposite Yusof Ishak House, this three-storey facility boasts features like swimming pools, squash courts, a gymnasium, and a rock-climbing wall. It’s also effectively a sheltered path for those travelling to Valour House from the direction of YIH.
Home of Team NUS. There are plenty of sports facilities here including swimming pool and squash courts.

The sports centre houses a variety of sports facilities, including two olympic-sized swimming pools (indoor and outdoor), gym, squash, among others.

The swimming pool is generally clean, and may get busy at times. I also particularly appreciate the clean changing rooms/toilet.
Well managed sports centre with vibrant community.
Sports complex for basketball, swimming, squash, table tennis etc. for NUS students and staff.

🌳Bukit Panjang Neighbourhood 5 Park is located right behind Pang Sua Pond.
🌳The hilly neighbourhood park provides great connectivity for residents of Jelapang, Saujana and Fajar to commute to the Bukit Panjang main road, where the MRT Stations and the Malls are located.
🌳Morning joggers, brisk walkers and seniors on wheelchairs with their caregivers frequent the neighbourhood park in the morning hours getting around the labyrinth of paths at the hilly terrain.
🌳Several BBQ Pits and Pavilions are strategically located around the park.
🌳There is also a Children Playground standing side by side to an Elderly Fitness Corner located at the northern end of the Park.
🌳An Amphitheatre stood a little distance from where the Playground is.
🌳This serene shady Neighbourhood Park is a welcome green lung around the Bukit Panjang Estate. 🌿🍀
It's a great and natural place to bike and walk. You can also have barbecue parties here. Awesome view of the pond is also visible. Sometimes a bit scary at night, but it is a 'must visit' place.

There's a whole lot of amenities here, I think the only thing missing is a dog run. The nearest dog run around is Segar but it's quite small and inconvenient. Whereas we get a lot of space in the neighborhood park and not all of them are properly utilised. Some of the bigger parks have dog runs / fenced areas, so please consider allocating an area or even a small patch of grass would be good. 🙏🏼
Nice to walk at but i am am scared to do at night but still nice maybe i am just scared of the dark
Slow morning walk, peaceful and relaxing, not that crowded during weekdays.

A lovely small rainforest park tucked away in the west that feels surprisingly immersive despite its compact size. The walking trails are well-paved and shaded by tall trees, making it comfortable for both casual strolls and light workouts. There’s also a playground and a simple fitness corner, so it works well for families as well as those looking for a quick exercise session

One of the highlights is the lookout point overlooking the quarry — a peaceful spot where you can pause, enjoy the greenery, and listen to the sounds of nature. Wildlife sightings are quite common too; it’s not unusual to spot colourful birds, squirrels, and even monkeys if you’re lucky. History buffs will appreciate the remnants of WWII structures such as old pillboxes scattered around the park, which add a subtle layer of heritage to the natural scenery

The only downside at the moment is that the path leading to the Syonan Chureito (the WWII Japanese remembrance site) is under maintenance, so access is temporarily closed

It was my first and hope it's not the last visit to this place. I really want to go there again.

Really liked the place and enjoyed walking around this place. This would definitely be a cute for all of your sadness.

Didn't realise there was a quarry in the park, took a long walk in nature and found many wonderful things! Was caught by a sudden downpour, appreciated the thick trees around the area which sheltered me from the rain.

Good mix of trails, some paths are cobblestones so be in a proper footwear.

Caught a squirrel in action too!

🌳A lush greenery that is just a stone throw away from the Bukit Batok Housing Estate, providing residents living here a green lung to relax, exercise and be with nature.
🚶‍➡️🏃‍➡️Many came here in the morning for exercises and brisk walk.
🛝🏋️At the Bt Batok Nature Park, besides having numerous shelters and walking paths, there is a Children Playground for little ones to enjoy, a Foot Reflexology Path and an Adult Fitness Corner that are next to the Playground.
🏞️But the centrepiece is the Lookout Point, where one can rest, take in the sight of the tranquil water and it's scenic surrounding.
⛩️Another interesting place around the Park with World War II history is Syonan Chureito Memorial
🚧Unfortunately, the access path from the park proper is closed for safety reason and is undergoing stabilisation work which is expected to be completed end Dec 2027.
Nevertheless, the site can be reached from Lorong Sesuai.
🪨Fun fact:
Bukit Batok Nature Park was formerly a granite quarry site.
Like many other granite quarry around, it ceased operation for safety and environmental reasons. And was then transformed into a nature park for all to enjoy.
Bukit batok natural park nice and peaceful park see sunrise depend on the cloud good place exercise 2 jan 2026
Nice BLK outside looking
Carpark quite narrow.
They have both raw and cooked options available, and they're not expensive.

We absolutely loved the Chinese New Year celebration at LFP 631!  The event was beautifully organized, with vibrant decorations, engaging activities, and such a joyful atmosphere. My kid enjoyed it so much and came home excited, sharing everything he experienced. It’s clear how much effort and care the teachers put into making it special and meaningful for the children. We truly appreciate the dedication and creativity of the entire team. Thank you for creating such wonderful memories for our kids!

A very special thank you to Principal Christina, Guo Laoshi, Teacher Devi, and Chen Laoshi for your incredible dedication and hard work.

Our daughter transferred to this centre last year in K1 and she has grown and blossomed under the care and guidance of her dedicated and loving teachers, Teacher Devi, Guo Laoshi, Teacher Hazel and Chen Laoshi.

Within a year, our daughter's mastery of phonics. numeracy, and Chinese has improved by leaps and bounds. She is now even able to recite Chinese poems fluently. The school also encourages the students to speak in front of the class which helps to nurture their public speaking skills. The teachers are also very proactive to share feedback about our daughter during drop-off and feedback, which gives us a huge peace of mind. The principal, Ms Christina, is also extremely supportive, responsive to any queries and welcomes feedback from parents.

As parents, we also really enjoyed taking part in the field trips and parent-child bonding events organised by the school, such as the dumpling-making activity led by Chen Laoshi. We are really grateful to have found such a wonderful preschool for our daughter.

My child had an amazing time at the K2 camp! It was such a wonderful experience for her.
She enjoyed working together with her friends to pitch their tent. The trip to Sungei Buloh was another highlight — they got to explore nature and spot wildlife up close. She came home so excited, telling us how she and her friends listened to all the different sounds of nature!
There were dance activities and games. I really appreciate the teachers’ care and attention and making sure she rested well at night. It was a well-organised, engaging and meaningful experience that helped the kids learn, play and grow together.
Lastly, I would like to express my heartfelt thanks to the school principal, Tr Devi, Guo laoshi and all the little footprint staff for creating such a meaningful and memorable preschool experience for my child.
Had a fun time with the school’s Mother’s Day activity. Teachers were fun and engaging.

Thank you Ms Christina, Bai laoshi and all teachers at LFP @ BP631. My son loves going to school and I know he is in good hands - teachers are passionate in receiving the kids every morning, proper checks are done at entry, kids well being are monitored closely during the day, feedbacks are given promptly on developmental growth and milestones as well as daily behaviors.

place_name: My First Skool at Blk 654 Senja Road
review_text:
Initially, I have lots of reservation about the school. Whether if my kid will be well taken care of and if the Teachers and staffs are capable.

However, my concerns were uncalled for. The Teachers and even the cleaner aunties are always on a constant look out for all the kids. Treating each like their own.

The teachers also do make it a point to share with parents on any interesting encounters with our kids.

Hope the school continue to stay this way and always delivering excellence in raising our next generation.

place_name: My First Skool at Blk 654 Senja Road
review_text:
The teachers in My First Skool at Blk 654 Senja Road are very nurturing teachers. I like how they update me about how my child is adapting to the environment in school. I have 2 children in this centre.
My first born is able to adapt to the school very fast and she did not have any problems interacting with the teachers there. She has been in this school from toddler (2020) until now, she is in Nursery (2022).
My second born is in toddler now and he has a hard time adjusting to the school initially as he wasn't able to part ways with my husband when he send him to school. The teachers in his centre calm him down. And he was able to stay calm and continued playing in the centre. I also like how they updated my child's routines and eating time in the communication book so that I will have a peace of mind on how he is progressing in school.

place_name: My First Skool at Blk 654 Senja Road
review_text:
MFS Senja Centre teachers and staff are awesome! My two children grown up from Infant all the way to K2 (my younger daughter is in K2 now). I always joked with the teachers that my children like school so much that they are always not in hurry to go home.
Teachers and staff are caring and attentive to children's overall well-being.
Environment is clean, tidy and comfortable.
I am assured that my children are very well taken care of by a great team!
- A satisfied mum with two happy children graduated (graduating) from MFS SJC 👍🏻

place_name: My First Skool at Blk 654 Senja Road
review_text: Teachers and principal is very passionate,patience and positive qualities! I love how they update and even upload photo on the activities that were done :) I can see how my girl enjoy her day there! Thank you to all the teachers,principal and even the staff there:) It is not easy to handle children nonetheless Appreciate all the commitments,care and concern to help my child learn and gain confidence too! 👏🏻👏🏻

place_name: My First Skool at Blk 654 Senja Road
review_text:
Both of my kids attended Myfirstskool and I am very pleased with the principal and the teachers. When my kids just started preschool, they often cry when they reached the school. Fortunately, Teacher Geetha and Mdm Liu were extremely patient with my boy and taught him well. They only took a short while and my boy was able to adapt to the new environment. Teacher Sue has also built a relatively good foundation for my kid’s English language despite language can be a challenge especially for such young children.

My girl also enjoys going to school. And I felt Teacher Rabia and Mdm Liu were very good with kids and made lessons very fun and interesting for my girl.

Teacher Wai Meng and Teacher Xiao has put in great effort for the class and involved much hands-on for the children. The kids can experience many types of stuff such as cooking, baking, planting and painting.
The principal, Ms Amanda, is very prompt and responsive whenever I have enquiries. Overall, I felt the teachers were gentle and patient. They teach the children how to control their emotions well and learning to be independent. Children are able to grow and learn in many innovative ways as they are exposed to various activities which allow them to dream big. One of my kids has graduated last year and their graduation was a great success despite the Covid situation at that point of time. I am very glad to meet these very friendly, approachable, wonderful teachers and principal.

Convenient for people to go and transfer train.
Clean.
With vending drinks inside.
Clean and quiet HDB estate with good transport connectivity.
This station is surrounded by the HDB block:) not much special things to see but it’s very quiet and. I’ve to walk around:) there’re some nice playgrounds inside the HDB near to this station:)
Nice place and very neat n clean
I just wanna say this mrt helps to move around and is available very early

Code Choice - Priority Use Sign
Code Context - Sign is located beside the lift in Senja LRT

Code Preference - Its in English as its one of the main language in Singapore. The priority sign is meant for the elderly, pregnant women and those in need of it since the lift is the only accessible way to go to the 2nd floor for the LRT besides the stairs. Hence, why the sign has English as it is the most well-known language in Singapore.

Inscription - The sign is in blue colour, probably in reference of how important the sign is. With stick images depicting the various people we should priortise taking the lift up. The 'o' in 'priority' is also been replaced with a pink heart, suggesting that we should help each other out in the community and helping others with kindness and love without expecting anything in return.

Emplacement - Considering how tall the stairs are in Bukit Panjang LRT stations, the lift maybe the most suitable way for those in need, hence why the priority use sign is placed beside the lift. The sign is there mainly to remind residents that the lifts can still be used but to be mindful of those that may need it more, ie the people depicted in the sign.
Just take note senja platform 1 lift not in service

Opened on 6 November 1999 as part of the Bukit Panjang LRT line operated by SMRT Trains, this LRT station is located at Bukit Panjang Ring Road near the junction with Senja Road. The station brings direct rail connectivity for residents of Bukit Panjang Neighbourhood 5 and 6 along Senja Road, Jelapang Road and Bukit Panjang Ring Road bringing them along to Bukit Panjang Town Centre as well as the Choa Chu Kang Town Centre. The bus services serving Senja LRT station are SMRT Buses feeder 920 and 922 between Bukit Panjang Bus Interchange and Bangkit Road, service 972 and 972M between Bukit Panjang Bus Interchange and Orchard Road and service 976 between Choa Chu Kang Bus Interchange and Bukit Panjang Bus Interchange, all of which are under Choa Chu Kang-Bukit Panjang Bus Package (Gali Batu Zone). Senja is named after Senja Road, which means "dusk" or "twilight" in Malay.
Say 11 close 10.55 close change ur timing . Don’t waste my time

Review is for the overall coffeeshop and Hao Wei cai peng stall. Coffeeshop is breezy (big fans) cool and clean even on a hot day. Can use Fairprice app to pay and get 10% off for both food and drinks 👍
Many retirees like myself during lunchtime but easy to find seat on weekday around noon.

Cai peng is average and after discount good value for $4.05($4.50 b4 discount), 2 veg one meat. Portions are fair for the price. Taste is industrial canteen standard which is not a bad thing..tasty but don't expect refinement. Eggplant though a little oily very tasty and soft and lots of onions. Bittergourd is tender and soaked in the stock. Chicken is little on the sweet side but ok.

This $8 spicy salmon don from Japanese Dons was amazing, but I couldn't find their Google page to leave a review. Generous portion and taste was great. Would definitely come back for this again.

Clean coffee shop and has a good variety of stalls.

bought a bun from the kopitiam corner and intended to pay using the Kopitiam app. However, the auntie selling the buns and kueh said they did not accept it and asked me to use PayNow instead. Unfortunately, I was having issues with my PayNow setup and couldn’t make the payment. I then told her I was unable to pay and not buying the bun.
The auntie looked unhappy and complained to the next customer that I didn’t even have $1.30 cash and couldn’t use PayNow either. So I replied that I simply would not buy it then — it was not that I had no intention to pay, but they did not accept the Kopitiam app payment in the first place.
I probably won’t buy from this bun stall again in the future to avoid getting upset.

During 1600hrs no crowd and comfortable on a weekday. I had late lunch and early dinner(I suppose). I ordered a chicken chop and add on spaghetti(option) side order (either coleslaw or French Fried) is included beside spaghetti $1, total :$8. Tiger beer $8( weather so warm need to cool down). This eatery coffee shop have 5 Chinese stalls, one Japanese food, one Muslim food and  one western food. If you using NTUC apps,  you will be given 10 per cent discount.  Payment on app. Cash no discount. The western food i had was good, Japanese not bad(but fried stuff can be oily). Washroom one entry for lady and man (one standing cubicle one room). Washroom avg clean. Evening dinner can be crowded, especially weekend. Most stall food will sold out around 8pm. Well ventilated and two Hugh fans(nice). Table are clean too. Economical rice reasonable rice and friendly servers. Noticed the roasted Chinese stall price doesn't goes with the quantity of food orders few pieces of pathetic meats. The only stall that have very few customers

Nice yong tao fu. Minimum 6 pieces and have curry, tom Yam or traditional soup option. Plus 10% discount using kopitiam app, definitely worth.
Visited last week. Stall permanently closed. What a pity. 😢

Came here to study a few times and I honestly love it! The place is so peaceful, making it a great environment to focus. There’s also a clean toilet with a bidet, which is definitely a plus point!

I also love the staff here they’re all super friendly and welcoming 🥺 Special mention to Syahirah (I hope I got the name right!), who was incredibly kind and didn’t mind that I stayed for a long time to study. The only small downside is that the air-conditioning isn’t really that strong, so it can get a little warm at times. Other than that, it’s a lovely place to study and spend a few hours.

The air conditioning was barely working and the indoor temperature was like an oven. I reported this to the staff, and even though they were sweating bullets themselves, they just brushed me off without offering any solution. After suffering for half an hour, I asked if we could open the door to let some air in, but they completely ignored my request. Terrible environment and unacceptable service attitude! My daughter had told me that regret to visit Coffee Bean.

Nice quaint CoffeeBean outlet with nice greenery outside. It’s really nice for a lazy morning and the breakfast sets are delicious at an affordable price! Eggs are nicely cooked and the tuna sandwiches were well toasted and had a good amount of filling. Thankful to have this breakfast place in the hood, keep it up! 👍

I would much rather have a kaya butter toast set from other stores.

Kudos to Manager on 10 Feb '25 Always a positive experience...
5 stars, thank you!

It is just an extremely overpriced instant korean maggi mee and tenders that are just bigger popcorn chicken bits. $5.50 for a Kimchi ramyeon and $3.40 for the chicken tenders.  Crazy margins. A kimchi ramyeon with no kimchi inside. And the presentation was really pathetic for a dine-in experience. It’s just the same tabao paper bowls and cups.

For $5.50 , i could have gotten REAL kimchi in my ramyeon and proper chicken slices inside the bowl from a neighbourhood food stall. First time trying Nene as it was new and what a letdown it was. Never ordering ramyeon from it again.

Located at the kopitiam under the HDB blocks, the chicken (by Korean standards) was a little overcooked and on the hard side, lacking the soft, juicy interior and crispy texture we were expecting.

We ordered the K-Kitchen Tenders set meal (Soy & Swicy), which came with cheese fries. The fries were actually really good, and we ended up enjoying them more than the chicken.

Luckily, we were able to use our CDC vouchers; without them, it wouldn’t have felt quite worth the price.

Updated May 1st, 2026 - Food City need to do something about all their houseflies infestation. Cannot finish a meal properly without swapping houseflies frequently.

Posted Apr 17, 2026 - This coffeeshop has both indoor and outdoor seating. Indoor is air-conditioned and brightly lit. We visited Sunday 6pm and it was almost fully packed. Tried again on Friday 5pm and it has no crowd yet. 😀 Tables and chairs are all clean, floor too! Food is typically local standard. No complain. Plenty of seats available. Many stalls of various cuisines. It’s comfortable to dine in as the air conditioning is pretty powerful.

New, clean, bright Aircon Coffeeshop, selling chilli dry ban Mian, old airport wanton mee, cze char, mixed vege rice (looks good), Muslim food, Indian food, chicken rice, etc.

Good and delicious dishes served by different stalls. Even the budget Meal Chicken Macaroni Soup tastes superbly delicious. I went there a second time just to eat Chicken Macaroni Soup. There are also snacks for supper.

Market as traditional toast but serve the normal white bread slices toasted. Not even browned. Half boiled eggs even though I left longer than they suggest still have alot of uncooked white. I am extremely disappointed with my experience at the Senja NTUC Blk 628 branch self-checkout area.

A staff member named Jasmine stood behind me throughout my entire transaction and stared at me continuously without greeting or offering assistance. Her body language and facial expression made it feel as though I was being treated like a potential shoplifter rather than a paying customer.

I understand that staff need to monitor self-checkout counters. However, supervision should be done professionally and respectfully. There is a clear difference between doing your job and making customers feel uncomfortable or targeted.

What makes this worse is that other staff members at this branch are generally friendly and polite — they greet customers or at least smile. This particular experience was in stark contrast and left a very negative impression.

I hope management takes this feedback seriously and ensures proper customer service standards are upheld.

However, because it is so small, only one Smith machine so probably only good for those who mainly use machines.

I miss this place 🥲 we've stayed here before then moved to Jelapang. I really like this place. The playground is very beautiful. Sometimes I sit here with my cousins or even alone to relax. I wish I could stay again in this place. Thank you 😊👍🏻

This Nature Park is in the greater area of Bukit Panjang and the "smaller area" of Zhenghua that is part of the former. I often walk or run in this Nature Park by entering it just off Chestnut Avenue and ending it near Segar, to benefit from its "hilly" terrain - albeit paved throughout - and to be amongst nature; taking in the fresh air and tranquility. By the way, this Nature Park also borders Chestnut Nature Reserve or Park, which is one the biggest Reserve or Park in Singapore.

🌿Zhenghua Nature Park is located at Segar Rd, opposite Zhenghua Community Club, bounded by Bukit Panjang Ring Rd and the busy Bukit Timah Expressway.
⛳Its inviting Open Lawn is a great place for picnic.
⛹️⛳Kids can run about freely and play ball at the green open lawn.
🛝Nearby is a sand-filled Children Playground designed for kids age 2 to 12 years old.
🏋️Next to it, is a Fitness Corner.
🛖A Pavilion housing the toilet is located nearby, should the kids needs cleaning up.
🪵There are two Shelters located at each end of the Nature Park.
🚴A walking/cycling path wounds around the serene Park where cyclist can cycle around the park.
🚧Zhenghua Nature Park is well connected to Zhenghua Park, Chestnut Nature Park, Dairy Farm Nature Park and nearby Bukit Panjang Park Connectors.

Best of Both worlds: Biggest Happiness 😊❤️😊 Zhenghua PCN and Across BKE is the Observation Tower at Chestnut Nature Park & MTB leads through the Central Catchment area till Mandai Track 15, Mandai Lake.

This is a linear park in Bukit Panjang that stretches alongside BKE and the park is shady due to mature trees with some sections having undulating terrain and steep slopes. It is a major link for hikers and cyclists, connecting the Bukit Panjang Park Connector to Bukit Timah Nature Reserve and Chestnut Nature Parks. There is also a sand-based playground, which is increasingly rare, with swings and climbing obstacles.

Our first visit having just moved into the area. We ended up cycling from our house which is close by. 7km round trip with 8yr and 4yr old. Great path for cycling with kids, quite shady too and 2 playgrounds on our route coming from Eco Sanctuary condo area. Large long hidden grass area used by mountain bikers kept the kids entertained for a long time. Great if you're looking for a good walk or cycle and live in the area not necessarily a destination park.
